blocks:
سُورة المائدة

Medinan, and it consists of 120 verses.

Sūrat al-Mā'idah

It is Medinan according to the saying of Ibn 'Abbas and Mujahid. It is also said that it is entirely Medinan, except for His saying: الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ (This day I have perfected for you your religion), for it was revealed during the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجة الوداع). It consists of one hundred and twenty verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ. (1) O you who have faith! Fulfill the contracts. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Meaning, the covenants (العهود). I say: Al-Ifā' (الإيفاء) and al-Wafā' (الوفاء) are synonymous, and al-'Aqd (العقد) is the confirmed covenant (العهد الموثق). Here, it encompasses everything that Allah has contracted upon His servants and obligated them to fulfill: belief in Him, His angels, His Books, His Messengers, and the successors (أوصياء) of His Messengers; declaring His lawful things lawful (تحليل حلاله) and His unlawful things unlawful (تحريم حرامه); performing His obligatory duties (فرائضه) and His Sunnah (سنَته); observing His limits (حدوده), His commands (أوامره), and His prohibitions (نواهيه); and everything that the believers contract upon themselves for the sake of Allah and among themselves, such as contracts of trusts (الأمانات) and permissible (الغير المحظورة) transactions (المعاملات). Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Jawad (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم contracted them to accept the Caliphate (الخلافة) of 'Ali in ten places, then Allah revealed: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ ** (O you who have faith! Fulfill the contracts) which were contracted upon you for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). أُحِلَّتْ لَكُمْ بَهِيمَةُ الْأَنْعامِ ** (Lawful for you are the animals of grazing livestock) It is said that this is an addition for clarification, by which the eight pairs (الأزواج الثمانية) are intended (1). What is understood from the apparent meaning of the narrations is that the clarification regarding the lawfulness of the livestock (الأنعام) is found in other verses. And what is intended here is the clarification regarding the lawfulness of the fetuses (الأجنّة) that are in their wombs. In al-Kafi, al-Tahdhib, al-Faqih, and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them) regarding its interpretation: The fetus (الجنين) in its mother's womb, if it has grown hair (أشعر) and wool (أوبر), its slaughter (ذكاة) is the slaughter of its mother. And it is added in al-Kafi and al-Qummi: That is what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, meant by it. In one narration: And if it is not complete (تامّاً), do not eat it. Al-'Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the fetuses (الأجنّة) in the wombs of the livestock (الأنعام). The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) used to command the selling of the fetuses (الأجنَة). I say: Perhaps this is one of its meanings. It is possible that the intent of these narrations is to clarify the less obvious instance (الفرد الأخفى), or that the initial restriction is due to its naming as al-bahīmah (البهيمة) and its lawfulness, which does not contradict the general application, especially since it is an explicit text regarding the lawfulness of the mother. Al-'Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): 'Ali (peace be upon him) was asked about eating the meat of the elephant (الفيل), the bear (الدّب), and the monkey (القرد). He replied: "This is not from the grazing livestock (بهيمة الأنعام) that is eaten." إِلَّا ما يُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ ** (except what is recited to you) (its prohibition) غَيْرَ مُحِلِّي الصَّيْدِ وَأَنْتُمْ حُرُمٌ ** (without making hunting lawful while you are in pilgrim sanctity.) It is said: Meaning, they were made lawful for you while you refrain from hunting (الصَّيْد) and you are in the state of ihram (مُحرمُونَ), so that no hardship (يتحرج) is placed upon you. I say: And this does not contradict the general lawfulness of them in all other states. إِنَّ اللهَ يَحْكُمُ ما يُرِيدُ ** (Indeed, Allah decrees whatever He desires.) such as declaring lawful (تحليل) and declaring unlawful (تحريم).

footnote (١) The eight pairs (*الأزواج الثمانية*): goats (*المعز*), sheep (*الضّأن*), cattle (*البقر*), and camels (*الإبل*), both male and female. What clarifies this will follow.

(٢) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُحِلُّوا شَعائِرَ اللهِ (2) O you who have faith! Do not violate Allah’s sacraments, Do not treat lightly the sacred things (حرمات) of Allah. (شعائر) is the plural of (شعيرة), which is what Allah has made the emblem (شعار) and sign (علامة) of the religion, such as the rites of Hajj and other things. وَلَا الشَّهْرَ الْحَرامَ nor the sacred month, by fighting therein. (1) In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning a man from Banu Rabi'ah called al-Hutm. I say: Meaning, when he arrived as a pilgrim (حاجّاً) and the Muslims intended to kill him during the Sacred Months (أشهر الحرم) due to his disbelief (كفره) and transgression (بغيه), and he had driven away the livestock (سرح) (2) of Medina. It is said that this [ruling] is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ (Then kill the polytheists wherever you find them). And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): Nothing in this Surah has been abrogated, nor anything in this Ayah, because it is not permissible for the polytheists (المشركون) to initiate fighting during the Sacred Months (الأشهر الحرم) unless they are fought against. وَلَا الْهَدْيَ nor the offerings, (1) That which is offered as a gift (أهدى) to the Ka'bah. وَلَا الْقَلائِدَ nor the garlands, That with which the offering (الهدي) is garlanded (قلّد), such as a sandal in which one has prayed or other things, so that it may be known thereby that it is an offering (هدي) and thus not be exposed to harm (لا يتعرض له). وَلَا آمِّينَ nor those who repair (2) To the Sacred House (الْبَيْتَ الْحَرامَ), intending to visit it. يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلاً مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَرِضْواناً seeking grace from their Lord and His pleasure. That He may reward them from His grace and be pleased with them, or that He may provide them with sustenance through trade and be pleased with them through their rites (نسكهم)—according to their claim. The intention is the prohibition against harming these people. And (رضواناً) is read with a dammah on the rā' (بضمّ الراء). وَإِذا حَلَلْتُمْ But when you are out of pilgrim sanctity, From your state of ihram (إحرامكم), then hunt (فَاصْطادُوا) if you wish. وَلا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ and let not the hatred

(١) And الشَّهْرَ الْحَرامَ ** is either specifically the month of Hajj, or a genus that includes all the Sacred Months (الأشهر الحرم), which are Rajab, Dhu al-Qa'dah, Dhu al-Hijjah, and Muharram.

(٢) (السّرح) is like (القتل), transitive (متعديا) meaning sending forth (الإرسال), driving out (الإخراج), and other meanings; and intransitive (لازماً) meaning traveling in the plain (السّهلة). What is meant here is that he intended to travel to Medina.

(٣) And let it not incite you, nor let it earn you شَنَآنُ قَوْمٍ —the intensity of their hatred (بغضهم) and enmity (عداوتهم). And it was recited with the sukūn on the nūn—أَنْ صَدُّوكُمْ عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ ** because they barred you from the Sacred Mosque, meaning during the year of al-Hudaybiyyah. And it was recited with the kasrah on the hamzah—أَنْ تَعْتَدُوا ** by taking revenge (بالانتقام). وَتَعاوَنُوا عَلَى الْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوى (Cooperate in piety and God-wariness) upon forgiveness (العفو), overlooking (الإغضاء), following the command (متابعة الأمر), and avoiding caprice (مجانبة الهوى). وَلا تَعاوَنُوا عَلَى الْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوانِ ** (and do not cooperate in sin and aggression) for the sake of gratification (التشفي) and revenge (الانتقام). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ إِنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ** (and be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is severe in retribution), for His retribution is more severe.

(٣) حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ ** (You are forbidden carrion)—a clarification of ما يُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ (what is recited to you)—وَالدَّمُ (and blood), meaning the flowing (المسفوح) part of it, due to His Almighty saying: أَوْ دَماً مَسْفُوحاً (or flowing blood). It is said that during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية), they used to pour it into the intestines (الأمعاءِ) and roast them. وَلَحْمُ الْخِنْزِيرِ ** (and the flesh of swine), even if it is slaughtered (ذكي). It was specified for mention, unlike the dog (الكلب) and others, because they were accustomed to eating it, unlike others. وَما أُهِلَ ** (and that over which is invoked)—the raising of the voice—لِغَيْرِ اللهِ بِهِ (a name other than Allah’s) like their saying: "In the name of al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā" when slaughtering it. وَالْمُنْخَنِقَةُ وَالْمَوْقُوذَةُ ** (and the strangled animal, and the beaten animal)

(٤) وَالْمُتَرَدِّيَةُ ** (4) and that which has fallen from a height, (٥) وَالنَّطِيحَةُ وَما أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ إِلَّا ما ذَكَّيْتُمْ وَما ذُبِحَ عَلَى النُّصُبِ وَأَنْ تَسْتَقْسِمُوا بِالْأَزْلامِ ذلِكُمْ فِسْقٌ (5) and that which has been gored, and that which a beast of prey has eaten—barring that which you slaughter [in time]—and that which has been sacrificed on altars, and that you seek to partake by the divining arrows. All that is transgression. In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation: الْمَيْتَةُ وَالدَّمُ وَلَحْمُ الْخِنْزِيرِ ** (The dead animal, blood, and the flesh of swine) is known. وَما أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللهِ بِهِ ** (and that over which the Name of Allah has not been mentioned) means that which was slaughtered for idols. As for الْمُنْخَنِقَةُ (that which has been strangled), the Magians (المجوس) would not eat slaughtered animals (الذَبايح) but would eat carrion (الميتة). They would strangle cattle and sheep, and when they were strangled and died, they would eat them. وَالْمَوْقُوذَةُ ** (and that beaten to death) they would tie its legs and beat it until it died, and when it died, they would eat it. وَالْمُتَرَدِّيَةُ (and that which has fallen from a height) they would tie its eyes and throw it from the roof, and when it died, they would eat it. وَالنَّطِيحَةُ ** (and that which has been gored) (1) they would make rams (الكباش) gore each other (2), and if one of them died, they would eat it. وَما أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ إِلَّا ما ذَكَّيْتُمْ (and that which a beast of prey has eaten—barring that which you slaughter [in time]) they used to eat what the wolf and the lion ate, so Allah forbade that. وَما ذُبِحَ عَلَى النُّصُبِ ** (and that which has been sacrificed on altars) they used to slaughter for fire temples (بيوت النّيران), and Quraysh used to worship trees and rocks, so they would slaughter for them both. وَأَنْ تَسْتَقْسِمُوا بِالْأَزْلامِ ذلِكُمْ فِسْقٌ ** (and that you seek to partake by the divining arrows. All that is transgression) He said: They would take a camel (الجزور) (3) and divide it into ten parts, then they would gather around it and bring out the arrows (السّهام) and hand them to a man. They were ten: seven had shares (أنصباء) and three had no shares. Those that had shares were: al-Fadh (الفّذ), al-Tu'am (التّوأم), al-Musbil (المسبل), al-Nafis (النّافس), al-Hils (الحِلس), al-Zaqib (الزقيب), and al-Mu'alla (المعلّى). Al-Fadh had one share, al-Tu'am had two shares, al-Musbil had three shares, al-Nafis had four shares, al-Hils had five shares, al-Zaqib had six shares, and al-Mu'alla had seven shares. Those that had no shares were: al-Safih (السّفيح), al-Manih (المنيح), and al-Waghd (الوغد). The price of the camel (الجزور) (4) was borne by the one for whom none of the shares came out, and this was gambling (القمار), so Allah forbade it.

And al-Qummi narrated the like of it.

In al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him), is a narration close to it, except that He (peace be upon him) said: وَالْمَوْقُوذَةُ (that beaten to death) is that which became sick and the sickness weakened it (وقذها) until it had no movement. He (peace be upon him) said: And they used to, in the Time of Ignorance (الجاهلية), buy a camel among ten people and seek to partake of it by the arrows (بالقداح). Then He mentioned the names of the seven and the three, just as they were mentioned. He said: They would then cast the arrows among ten people. Whoever had an arrow come out in his name from those that had no shares was obligated to pay one-third of the price of the camel. They would continue thus until the three arrows that had no shares fell to three of them, obligating them to pay the price of the camel. Then they would slaughter it, and the seven who had not paid anything toward its price would eat it. When Islam came, Allah, exalted be His remembrance, forbade that among what He forbade, and He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَأَنْ تَسْتَقْسِمُوا بِالْأَزْلامِ ذلِكُمْ فِسْقٌ (and that you seek to partake by the divining arrows. All that is transgression), meaning: forbidden (حرام).

I say: The meaning of dividing it into ten parts (تجزيته عشرة أجزاء) is their purchasing it among ten people, as mentioned in the narration of al-Jawad (peace be upon him), not the division of its meat. Al-Ansibā' (الأنصباء) is the plural of nasīb (نصيب). Al-Fadh (الفذّ) is with the fā' (فاء) and the emphasized dotted dhāl (ذال معجمة مشدّدة). Al-Tu'am (التّوأم) is with the upper doubled tā' (تاء مثنّاة فوقانيّة) and the hamza (همزة). Al-Musbil (المسبل) is like muhsin (مُحسن), with the undotted sīn (سين مهملة) and the single-dotted bā' (باء موحّدة). Al-Nafis (النّافس) is with the nūn (نون), the fā' (فاء), and the undotted sīn (سين مهملة). Al-Hils (الحِلس) is with the kasra on the hā' (كسر الحاءِ), the sukūn on the lām (سكون اللّام), and the undotted sīn (سين مهملة), and it may sometimes be vocalized (يحرّك). Al-Zaqib (الزّقيب) is with the zāy (زاي) and the qāf (قاف) on the pattern of fa'īl (فعيل). Al-Mu'alla (المعلّى) is with the damma on the mīm (ضمّ الميم), the sukūn on the ayn (سكون العين), and the fatha on the lām (فتح اللّام). Al-Safih (السّفيح) is with the undotted sīn (سين مهملة), the fā' (فاء), and the undotted hā' (حاء مهملة) on the pattern of fa'īl (فعيل), like al-Manih (المنيح) with the nūn (نون) and the undotted hā' (حاء مهملة). Al-Waghd (الوغد) is with the wāw (واو), the dotted ghayn (غين معجمة), and the undotted dāl (دال مهملة). It is said that the meaning of seeking to partake بِالْأَزْلامِ (by the divining arrows) is seeking knowledge of what has been allotted to them by the arrows (بالأقداح), meaning the shafts (السّهام). This is because when they intended an action, they would cast three arrows: on one was written, "My Lord commanded me (أمرني ربِّي)," on another, "My Lord forbade me (نهاني ربِّي)," and on the third, "Neglected (غفَل)." If the command came out, they proceeded with that; if the prohibition came out, they avoided it; and if "Neglected" came out, they cast them again.

In some narrations, there is an allusion (إيماء) to this, as will come at the end of the Sūrah. It is possible to reconcile [the interpretations] by generalization. And His Almighty saying: إِلَّا ما ذَكَّيْتُمْ ** (barring that which you slaughter [in time]) refers back to what was previously mentioned of the forbidden things, except for what does not accept ritual slaughter (الذّكاة), such as swine and blood. This is according to al-Majma' from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and al-Ayyashi from al-Rida (peace be upon him): الْمُتَرَدِّيَةُ وَالنَّطِيحَةُ وَما أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ (that which has fallen from a height, and that which has been gored, and that which a beast of prey has eaten)—if you reach it while it is still alive enough for ritual slaughter (ذكاة), then eat it. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): The minimum sign by which ritual slaughter (الذَّكاة) can be performed is that one reaches it while it is moving its ear, or its tail, or blinking its eyes. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the book of Ali (peace be upon him): If the eye blinks, or the leg kicks, or the tail moves, then eat from it, for its ritual slaughter (ذكاة) has been reached. There are other narrations with the same meaning. الْيَوْمَ (Today) means now. يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ دِينِكُمْ ** (the faithless have despaired of your religion) their hope has been cut off regarding your religion, that you would abandon it and return from it to polytheism (الشّرك).

Al-Qummi said: That was when the verse concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was revealed. فَلا تَخْشَوْهُمْ ** (So do not fear them) that they might prevail over the religion of Islam and turn you back from your religion. وَاخْشَوْنِ ** (but fear Me) if you disobey My command, that My punishment may descend upon you. الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلامَ دِيناً ** (Today I have perfected your religion for you, and I have completed My blessing upon you, and I have approved Islam as your religion.) In al-Majma', narrated from them both (peace be upon them): It was only revealed after the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him and his progeny) appointed Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both) as a standard (علماً) for mankind on the Day of Ghadir Khumm, upon his return from the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجّة الوداع). They (peace be upon them) said: And it is the last obligatory duty (فريضة) that Allah revealed, and no obligatory duty was revealed after it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir: One obligatory duty (الفريضة) is revealed after another obligatory duty, and the Guardianship (الولاية) was the last of the obligatory duties. So Allah revealed: الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ ** (Today I have perfected your religion for you). He said: I shall not reveal an obligatory duty after this; I have perfected the obligatory duties for you. And al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrated from Him what is close to it.

I say: The obligatory duties (الفرائض) were only perfected through the Guardianship (الولاية) because the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him and his progeny) conveyed all the knowledge that Allah entrusted to him to Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him), and then to his progeny, the Successors (الأوصياء), one after the other. When He established them in His place, and people were enabled to refer back to them regarding what is lawful (حلال) and what is forbidden (حرام), and this continued with one standing in authority after the other, the religion was perfected and the blessing was completed, if Allah wills. This exact meaning has been narrated from them (peace be upon them), and what is close to it will come in the Sermon of al-Ghadir. فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ (But should someone be compelled) is connected to the mention of the forbidden things, and what is between them is an interjection (اعتراض). The meaning is: فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ ** (But should someone be compelled) to consume something from these forbidden things فِي مَخْمَصَةٍ ** (by hunger) meaning: in a state of starvation (مجاعة), غَيْرَ مُتَجانِفٍ (without being inclined) meaning: not leaning لِإِثْمٍ. (to sin).

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Not intending (متعمّد) لِإِثْمٍ. (to sin).

I say: And that is by eating it for pleasure or by exceeding the limit of the permission (الرخصة). This is like His saying, the Glorified: غَيْرَ باغٍ وَلا عادٍ. (neither desirous nor transgressing). Its interpretation has already passed in Sūrat al-Baqarah. **فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful) He will not hold him accountable for eating it.

(٤) يَسْئَلُونَكَ ما ذا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ ** (4) They ask you what is lawful for them. It is as if, when what was forbidden to them was recited to them, they asked about what was made lawful for them. قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّباتُ ** (Say, 'Lawful for you are the good things') It was said: That which sound natures (الطّباع السّليمة) do not deem foul and are not repelled by. وَما عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوارِحِ ** (and the game caught by the trained hunting animals) meaning: their catch, and they are the beasts of prey (السّباع) and birds that earn the game for their owners. مُكَلِّبِينَ ** (training them) meaning: disciplining them. And al-Mukallib (المكلّب) is the one who disciplines the hunting animals (الجوارح) and incites them to hunt, derived from al-Kalb (الكلب) (dog).

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), in the book of Ali (peace be upon him), regarding the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَما عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ (and the game caught by the trained hunting animals, training them), He said: They are the dogs (الكلاب). And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): If you send forth a falcon (بازاً), a hawk (صقراً), or an eagle (عقاباً), do not eat [the catch] until you reach it and ritually slaughter it (تذكّيه). If it kills it, do not eat it. And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), when He was asked about sending forth a dog and a hawk, He said: As for the hawk, do not eat from its catch until you reach it for ritual slaughter (ذكاة). As for the dog, eat from it if you mentioned the Name of Allah over it, whether the dog ate from it or not. There are many narrations with the same meaning as these two. تُعَلِّمُونَهُنَّ مِمَّا عَلَّمَكُمُ اللهُ ** (teaching them what Allah has taught you) meaning: what He inspired in you of the methods of training. The training (أدبه) was interpreted as: following the game when its owner sends it, being restrained when he restrains it, returning when he calls it, and holding the game for him. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): What the trained hunting animals (الْجَوارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ) kill, and you have mentioned the Name of Allah over it, then eat from their catch. But what the untrained dogs kill before you reach it, do not feed on it.

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said regarding the catch of the dog: If its owner sends it and mentions [the Name of Allah], then let him eat everything it holds for him, even if it kills it, and even if it eats [from it], then eat what remains. And if it was untrained, but he trained it at the moment he sent it, then let him eat from it, for it is considered trained. As for what is other than the dog—such as what leopards (الفهود), hawks (الصّقور), and the like hunt—do not eat from their catch unless you reach it for ritual slaughter (ذكاة), because Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: مُكَلِّبِينَ ** (training them). So, the catch of animals other than dogs is not to be eaten unless its ritual slaughter is reached. As for the narrations that came contrary to this, they are interpreted as being based on dissimulation (التقيّة) because they conform to the doctrines of the general populace (العامّة), as We explained in al-Wafi. فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ.** (So eat of what they catch for you, and mention the Name of Allah over it.)

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the catch of falcons (البزاة) (1), hawks (الصّقور), leopards (الفهود), and dogs. He said: Do not eat except what you ritually slaughter (ذكّيت), except for dogs. It was asked: What if it kills it? He said: Eat, for Allah says: وَما عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ تُعَلِّمُونَهُنَّ مِمَّا عَلَّمَكُمُ اللهُ فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (and the game caught by the trained hunting animals, training them, teaching them what Allah has taught you. So eat of what they catch for you). Then He said: فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (So eat of what they catch for you). Then He said: Every beast of prey holds the game for itself, except for trained dogs, for they hold it for their owner. And He said: If you send forth the trained dog, mention the Name of Allah over it, for that is its ritual slaughter (ذكاة). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (and be wary of Allah) regarding what He has forbidden to you. إِنَّ اللهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ ** (Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning) so He will hold you accountable for what is great and what is small.

(٥) الْيَوْمَ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّباتُ وَطَعامُ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ حِلٌّ لَكُمْ. (5) Today the good things have been made lawful for you. The food of those who were given the Book is lawful for you,

Al-Qummi said: What is meant by their food (طعامهم) here are grains (الحبوب) and fruits (الفَاكهة), excluding the animals they slaughter, for they do not purely mention the Name of Allah over their slaughtered animals. Then he said: By Allah, they do not deem your slaughtered animals lawful, so how can you deem their slaughtered animals lawful? In al-Kafi and other sources, narrated from them both (peace be upon them) in several narrations: That what is meant by it are grains (الحبوب) and vegetables (البقول). In some of them: Do not eat from the slaughtered animals of the Jews and Christians, and do not eat from their vessels. In some of them: Ritual slaughter (الذّبيحة) is by the Name [of Allah], and only the People of Monotheism (أهل التّوحيد) are trusted regarding it. In some of them: If you witness them mentioning the Name of Allah, then eat their slaughtered animals. But if you do not witness them, do not eat. If a Muslim man comes to you and informs you that they mentioned [the Name of Allah], then eat. In some of them: Do not eat it, nor leave it saying that it is forbidden (حرام), but leave it out of avoidance (تنزّهاً) because their vessels contain wine (الخمر) and pork (لحم الخنزير). وَطَعامُكُمْ حِلٌّ لَهُمْ ** (and your food is lawful for them) so there is no blame upon you if you feed them from it or sell it to them. وَالْمُحْصَناتُ ** (and chaste women) And lawful for you is the marriage contract (العقد) with the chaste women (العفائف) مِنَ الْمُؤْمِناتِ. (from among the faithful women).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the Muslim women (المسلمات). وَالْمُحْصَناتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ ** (and chaste women from among those who were given the Book before you). In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the chaste women (العفائف). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the meaning of their chastity (احصانهنّ). He said: They are the chaste women (العفائف) among their women. In al-Kafi, al-Majma', and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That it is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: وَلا تُمْسِكُوا بِعِصَمِ الْكَوافِرِ (and do not hold on to the ties of marriage with faithless women). And al-Majma' added: And by His saying: وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكاتِ. (and do not marry polytheist women). Al-Qummi said: Allah made lawful the marriage of the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) after forbidding it in His saying in Sūrat al-Baqarah: وَلا تَنْكِحُوا (and do not marry) الْمُشْرِكاتِ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَ ** (polytheist women until they have faith). He said: And the marriage of the People of the Book who pay the poll-tax (الجزية) is only lawful; marriage with others is not lawful. I say: This is supported by the Prophetic narration that Sūrat al-Mā'idah is the last Sūrah of the Quran to be revealed, so deem lawful what it made lawful and deem forbidden what it made forbidden.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Hasan ibn al-Jahm, who said: Abu al-Hasan al-Rida (peace be upon him) said to me: "O Aba Muhammad, what do you say about a man who marries a Christian woman while having a Muslim wife?" I said: "May I be made your ransom, what is my saying before you?" He said: "You must say it, for by that My saying will be known." I said: "It is not permissible, it is not permissible [to marry] a Christian woman while having a Muslim wife, nor while having a non-Muslim wife." He said: "Why?" I said: "Because of the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكاتِ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَ ** (and do not marry polytheist women until they have faith)." He said: "Then what do you say about this verse: وَالْمُحْصَناتُ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِناتِ وَالْمُحْصَناتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ ** (and chaste women from among the faithful women, and chaste women from among those who were given the Book before you)?" I said: "His saying, وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكَاتِ (and do not marry polytheist women), abrogated this verse." So He smiled, then remained silent.

In it (al-Kafi) and in al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding the believing man who marries a Christian or Jewish woman, He said: If he has found a Muslim woman, what does he need with a Jewish or Christian woman? It was said: He might have an inclination (الهوى) toward her. He said: If he does that, he must prevent her from drinking wine (الخمر) and eating pork (لحم الخنزير), and know that there is a deficiency (غضاضة) (1) upon him in his religion. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is not proper for a Muslim to marry a Jewish or Christian woman while he can find a free Muslim woman or a slave woman. And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Only the marriage of the simple-minded (البله) (2) among them is lawful. In al-Faqih, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a Muslim man marrying a Magian woman (المجوسيّة). He said: No. But if he has a Magian slave woman, there is no harm in having intercourse with her and practicing azl (عزل) with her, and not seeking a child from her. In a narration: A man should not marry a Jewish or Christian woman while having a Muslim wife, but he may marry a Muslim woman while having a Jewish or Christian wife. In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There is no harm in a man contracting temporary marriage (يتمتّع) with a Jewish or Christian woman while he has a free wife. And in it, there are other narrations regarding the permissibility of temporary marriage with them both and with the Magian woman. إِذا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَ ** (when you have given them their dowries) meaning: their dowries (مهورهن). مُحْصِنِينَ ** (in wedlock) meaning: being chaste through marriage. غَيْرَ مُسافِحِينَ ** (not fornicating openly) meaning: not committing fornication (الزّنا) openly. وَلا مُتَّخِذِي أَخْدانٍ ** (nor taking secret paramours) (1) meaning: committing it secretly. And al-Khidn (الخدن) (paramour/friend) applies to both male and female. وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِالْإِيمانِ ** (And whoever denies the faith) by rejecting the Divine Laws (الشرايع) or by abandoning them. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation: Abandoning the action that he affirmed, such as abandoning the prayer without sickness or preoccupation. In a narration: Abandoning the action until he leaves it entirely. And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it. And he (al-Ayyashi) narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The least thing by which a man leaves Islam is that he holds an opinion contrary to the truth and persists in it. He said: وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِالْإِيمانِ (And whoever denies the faith) means the one who does not act upon what Allah commanded Him and is not pleased with it. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the Guardianship (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi said: Whoever believes and then obeys the People of Polytheism (أهل الشّرك), فَقَدْ حَبِطَ عَمَلُهُ وَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ.** (his work has failed, and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers.)

(٦) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرافِقِ وَامْسَحُوا بِرُؤُسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ (O you who have faith! When you stand up for prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, and wipe a part of your heads and your feet up to the ankles.)

And it was read with the nasb (accusative case) of al-arjul (الأرجل), which is rejected by us, as will follow. And what is meant by "when you stand up" (القيام) is standing up from sleep.

Thus, in al-Tahdhib and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the meaning of إِذا قُمْتُمْ (When you stand up). He said: "When you stand up from sleep." And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked what was meant by it. He said: "It refers to sleep." Thus, we are relieved of the burdens (تكلّفات) and implied meanings (اضماراتهم) of the exegetes (2).

As for the obligation of wudu' (الوضوء) due to an impurity other than the event of sleep, it is derived from the narrations (الأخبار), just as the obligation of ghusl (الغسل) for reasons other than janabah (الجنابة) is derived from another source. Similarly, all other ambiguous parts (مجملات) of the Quran are only clarified by the exegesis (تفسير) of the People of the House (أهل البيت), and they are more knowledgeable than others concerning what was revealed in the House.

The face (الوجه) is that with which one confronts (others). Therefore, it is not obligatory to penetrate thick hair (الشّعر الكثيف)—meaning that through which the skin (البَشرَة) is not visible during conversation—since confrontation (المواجهة) at that time is only with the hair, not with what is beneath it. As narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Everything that the hair encompasses, the servants are not required to seek out or investigate, but the water should flow over it." This was narrated in al-Tahdhib. And in it (al-Tahdhib) and al-Kafi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them): He was asked about a man performing wudu' (يتوضّأ): "Should he wash the inner part (يبطّن) (1) of his beard?" He said: "No."

As for the boundary of the face (حدّ الوجه), in al-Faqih, al-Kafi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The face which Allah commanded to be washed, and upon which no one should add or subtract—if he adds to it, he is not rewarded, and if he subtracts from it, he sins—is what is encompassed by the middle finger (الوسطى) and the thumb (الابهام), from the hairline (قصاص شعر الرأس) down to the chin (الذّقن). And whatever the two fingers pass over on the face in a circular manner is part of the face, and whatever is outside of that is not part of the face." He was asked: "Is the temple (الصّدغ) part of the face?" He said: "No."

As for the rest of the limbs (سائر الأعضاء), it is obligatory to convey the water and wetness to the skin (البشرة) and to penetrate whatever prevents the water from reaching it, as is required by the command for washing (الغسل) and wiping (المسح). Therefore, wiping over the cap (القلنسوة) or over the leather socks (الخفين) is not sufficient. In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Umar ibn al-Khattab gathered the Companions of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), and among them was Ali (peace be upon him). He asked: 'What do you say about wiping over the leather socks (المسح على الخفّين)?' Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah stood up and said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) wiping over the leather socks.' Ali said: 'Was this before al-Ma'idah (المائِدة) or after al-Ma'idah?' He said: 'I do not know.' Ali said: 'The Book preceded the leather socks. Al-Ma'idah was only revealed two or three months before He (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) was taken.'"

أقول I say: This al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah is one of the leaders of the hypocrites (المنافقين) among the people of al-Aqabah (العقبة) and al-Saqifah (السّقيفة), may Allah curse them.

In al-Faqih, A'ishah narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) that He said: "The person with the greatest regret (حسرةً) on the Day of Resurrection is he who sees his wudu' (وضوءه) upon the skin of another."

And it was narrated from her (A'ishah) that she said: "That I wipe over the back of a donkey (عير) in the wilderness (الفلاة) is dearer to me than that I wipe over my leather socks (خفّي)." And no leather sock (خفّ) was known for the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) except the leather sock gifted to Him by the Negus (النّجاشي), and the place of the top of the two feet was split open on it. So the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) wiped His feet while wearing His two leather socks. Then the people said that He wiped over His leather socks. Furthermore, the narration concerning this is not sound in its chain of transmission (الأسناد). The words of al-Faqih end here.

Since the hand (اليد) is applied to what is below the wrist (الزند), to what is below the elbow (المرفق), and to what is below the shoulder (المنكب), Allah, the Glorified, clarified the limit (غاية) of what is to be washed from it, just as you say to your servant: "Dye your hand up to the wrist (الزند)," and to the polisher: "Polish my sword up to the hilt (القبضة)." Thus, there is no indication in the Ayah that the washing must begin at the fingers and end at the elbows, just as there is no indication in these two phrases that the dyeing and polishing must begin at the fingers of the hand and the head of the sword. Therefore, it is ambiguous (مجملة) in this meaning and requires clarification (تبيين) from the People of the House (peace be upon them).

The elbow (المرفق)—with the kasrah on the first letter and fathah on the third, or vice versa—is the joint of the two bones of the forearm (الذّراع) and the upper arm (العضد). There is no indication in the Ayah that it must be included in the washing of the hand, nor that the ankle (الكعب) must be included in the wiping of the feet, because the limit (الغاية) sometimes excludes and sometimes includes (the boundary). Thus, in this meaning, it is ambiguous (مجملة), and it is only clarified by their exegesis (تفسيرهم).

Washing (الغسل) is achieved by pouring water over the limb or immersing it therein, even if one does not rub it. The ba' (الباء) in بِرُؤُسِكُمْ (a part of your heads) is for partiality (للتّبعيض). Likewise, in بِوُجُوهِكُمْ (with your faces), and likewise in the two limbs conjoined to them, meaning أَرْجُلَكُمْ (your feet) and أَيْدِيَكُمْ (your hands). This is narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), as will follow.

The ankle (الكعب) is a bone inclined toward roundness, located at the junction of the shin (الساق) and the foot (القدم), protruding from its back, whose protrusion enters the end of the shin, like that found in the legs of cattle and sheep, and which children sometimes play with. It is sometimes referred to as the joint (المفصل) due to its proximity to it. People only differed concerning it because they did not delve deeply into the words of the linguists (أهل اللّغة) and anatomists (أصحاب التشريح) and because they turned away from reflecting upon the infallible narrations (الأخبار المعصومية).

Since the leg (الرِّجل) is applied to the foot (القدم), to what is below the knee (الركبة), and to what includes the thigh (الفخذ), Allah, the Glorified, clarified the limit (غاية) of what is to be wiped from it. Furthermore, the indication of the Ayah regarding the wiping of the feet (مسح الرجلين) rather than their washing (غسلهما) is clearer than the sun at midday, especially according to the reading with al-jarr (genitive case). For this reason, a large group of those who advocate washing (الغسل) have acknowledged this.

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: امْسَحُوا بِرُؤُسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ (and wipe a part of your heads and your feet up to the ankles)—is it with al-khafd (genitive case) or with al-nasb (accusative case)? He said: "Rather, it is with al-khafd."

أقول I say: Even assuming the reading with al-nasb (accusative case), it still indicates wiping (المسح), because it would then be conjoined (معطوفة) to the place (محلّ) of al-ru'us (الرؤوس), just as you say: "I passed by Zayd and 'Amran (مررت بزيد وعمرواً)," since conjoining it to al-wujuh (الوجوه) is outside the rule of eloquence (الفصاحة), indeed, outside the style of the Arabic language (أسلوب العربية). The general public (العامّة) narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and Ibn Abbas, from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): that He performed wudu' (توضّأ) and wiped over His feet and His sandals (نعليه).

And they also narrated from Ibn Abbas that he said: "Indeed, the Book of Allah dictates wiping (المسح), but the people insist on washing (الغسل)." And that he said: "Wudu' (الوضوء) consists of two washings and two wipings; whoever challenges me, I will challenge him to a mutual imprecation (باهلته)." And that he described the wudu' of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) and He wiped over His legs (رجليه).

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the wiping of the feet (مسح الرّجلين). He said: "It is that which Gabriel (جبرئيل) brought down."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "A man may reach sixty or seventy years, yet Allah has not accepted any prayer (صلوة) from him." He was asked: "How is that?" He said: "Because he washes what Allah commanded to be wiped."

In al-Faqih, narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): "A man may worship Allah for forty years, yet he does not obey Him in wudu' (الوضوء); he washes what Allah commanded to be wiped."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the wudu' (وضوءِ) of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). So He called for a basin (طست) (1) or a small vessel (تور) containing water. He immersed His right hand and scooped up a handful (غرفة) with it and poured it onto His face. Then He immersed His left palm and scooped up a handful with it and poured it onto His right forearm (ذراعه). He washed His forearm with it, from the elbow (المرفق) down to the palm (الكف), not returning it to the elbow. Then He immersed His right palm and poured it onto His left forearm from the elbow, and did with it the same as He did with the right. Then He wiped His head and His feet with the wetness of His palm, without taking new water for them. Then He said: "And he should not put his fingers under the strap (الشِّراك) (2)." Then He said: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: إِذا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ (When you stand up for prayer, wash your faces). And he must not leave anything of his face unwashed. And He commanded the washing of the hands up to the elbows, so he must not leave anything of his hands up to the elbows unwashed, because Allah the Almighty said: فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرافِقِ (wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows). Then He said: وَامْسَحُوا بِرُؤُسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ (and wipe a part of your heads and your feet up to the ankles). So, if he wipes a part of his head, or a part of his feet between the ankles (الكعبين) and the tips of the toes (أطراف الأصابع), it suffices." He was asked: "Where are the ankles (الكعبان)?" He said: "Here," meaning the joint (المفصل), not the bone of the shin (عظم الساق). He was asked: "What is this?" He replied: "This is part of the bone of the shin, and the ankle is lower than that." He was asked: "May Allah keep you well, does one scoop suffice for the face, and one scoop for the forearm?" He said: "Yes, if you are thorough with it, and two scoops cover all of that."

In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Zurarah, who said: I said to Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him): "Will you not inform me from where you knew and said that the wiping is upon a part of the head and a part of the feet?" He (peace be upon him) laughed, then said: "O Zurarah, the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) said it, and the Book came down from Allah with it, because Allah the Almighty says: فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ (wash your faces). So we knew that the entire face should be washed. Then He said: وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرافِقِ (and your hands up to the elbows). So He connected the hands up to the elbows with the face, and we knew that they should be washed up to the elbows. Then He separated the discourse and said: وَامْسَحُوا بِرُؤُسِكُمْ (and wipe a part of your heads). So we knew, when He said 'a part of your heads' (بِرُؤُسِكُمْ), that the wiping is upon a part of the head due to the presence of the ba' (الباءِ). Then He connected the feet (الرّجلين) to the head, just as He connected the hands to the face, and said: وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ (and your feet up to the ankles). So we knew, when He connected them to the head, that the wiping is upon a part of them. Then the Messenger of Allah explained that to the people, but they neglected it." — The narration continues, and its completion will come shortly. And He pointed out with His saying "due to the presence of the ba' (لمكان الباءِ)" that the ba' is for partiality (للتّبعِيض). Thus, there is no basis for Sibawayh's denial of its occurrence for this purpose in seventeen places in his book.

We have only elaborated upon the interpretation of the Ayah of wudu' (آية الوضوءِ) due to the widespread affliction (عموم البلوى) concerning it and the frequency of disagreement regarding it. And praise be to Allah for what He has guided us to through the blessing of the People of the House of His Prophet (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). The complete discussion on this topic should be sought in our book, al-Wafi (الوافي).

وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ جُنُباً فَاطَّهَّرُوا (and if you are junub, purify yourselves.)

This is conjoined (عطف) to the predicate (جزاءِ) of the first condition, meaning فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ (wash your faces). That is, if you stand up from sleep for prayer, then perform wudu' (توضّئوا), and وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ جُنُباً (and if you are junub), then perform ghusl (فاغتسلوُا). This is indicated by His saying, the Almighty: وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ مَرْضى (and if you are sick), for it is certainly included under the condition. If His saying وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ (and if you are) were conjoined to His saying إِذا قُمْتُمْ (When you stand up), or if it were a new independent clause (مستأنفاً), the conjoined elements would not be consistent (يتناسق), and it would necessarily follow that the connection between ghusl (الغسل) and prayer (الصّلوة) is not derived from the Ayah. Furthermore, the word "إن" (in) would not be appropriate; rather, it should have been said: "and if you are junub" (وإذا كنتم جنباً), as is not hidden (1) from one who follows the styles of speech (أساليب الكلام).

This is also indicated by what is in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about a woman whom a man has intercourse with, and she menstruates while she is in the place of ghusl (المغتسل). He said: "That which invalidates the prayer (الصّلوة) has come upon her, so she should not perform ghusl."

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about ghusl (غسل) for janabah (الجنابة). He said: "You begin by washing your two palms (كفّيك), then you pour with your right hand onto your left and wash your private part (فرجك) and your elbows (مرافقك). Then you rinse your mouth (تمضمض) and nose (استنشق), then you wash your body from your crown (قرنك) (1) down to your feet. There is no wudu' (وضوء) after it or before it. And everything you have touched with water, you have purified. And if a man were to immerse himself in water a single immersion (ارتماسة واحدة), that would suffice him, even if he did not rub his body."

In al-Kafi, in a truncated narration (مقطوعاً): If nothing has touched his palm, he immerses it in the water, then begins with his private part and cleanses it with three scoops (غرف). Then he pours three handfuls (أكفّ) onto his head, then pours onto his right shoulder (منكبه الأيمن) twice, and onto his left shoulder (منكبه الأيسر) twice. Whatever water flows over him suffices him.

وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ مَرْضى أَوْ عَلى سَفَرٍ أَوْ جاءَ أَحَدٌ مِنْكُمْ مِنَ الْغائِطِ أَوْ لامَسْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَلَمْ تَجِدُوا ماءً فَتَيَمَّمُوا صَعِيداً طَيِّباً فَامْسَحُوا بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُمْ مِنْهُ (But if you are sick or on a journey, or any of you has come from the toilet, or you have touched women, and you cannot find water, then make tayammum with clean ground: wipe a part of your faces and your hands with it.)

The interpretation of this Ayah has already passed in Surat al-Nisa' (سورة النّساء), so there is no need to repeat it.

In al-Faqih, in the narration of Zurarah mentioned just before, connected to its end, he then said: فَلَمْ تَجِدُوا ماءً فَتَيَمَّمُوا صَعِيداً طَيِّباً فَامْسَحُوا بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُمْ مِنْهُ (and you cannot find water, then make tayammum with clean ground: wipe a part of your faces and your hands with it). When He replaced wudu' (الوضوء) if they could not find water, He established wiping (مسحاً) for a part of the washing (الغسل), because He said: بِوُجُوهِكُمْ (a part of your faces), then He connected to it وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ (and your hands), then He said مِنْهُ (with it), meaning from that tayammum (التّيمّم), because it is known that all of that (الصّعيد) does not flow over the face, since some of that clean ground (الصّعيد) adheres to a part of the palm and does not adhere to another part. His (peace be upon him) saying "from that tayammum (من ذلك التّيمّم)" apparently means the material used for tayammum (المتيمّم به), evidenced by His saying "that" (meaning the clean ground (الصّعيد)) "all of it does not flow over the face." And it is derived from this that the word min (من) in minhu (منه) is for partiality (للتّبعيض), and that the adherence of dust (التّراب) to the palm is stipulated, and that tayammum (التّيمّم) is not permissible with a stone (الحجر) that is not dusty (الغير المغبّر), as its verification has passed.

ما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيَجْعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ حَرَجٍ وَلكِنْ يُرِيدُ لِيُطَهِّرَكُمْ وَلِيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (Allah does not desire to put you to any hardship, but He desires to purify you, and to complete His blessing upon you so that you may be grateful.)

ما يُرِيدُ اللهُ (Allah does not desire)—by ordaining the purifications (الطّهارات)—لِيَجْعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ حَرَجٍ (to put you to any hardship)—meaning difficulty (ضيق)—وَلكِنْ يُرِيدُ لِيُطَهِّرَكُمْ (but He desires to purify you)—from ritual impurities (الأحداث) and sins (الذّنوب), for purification (الطّهارة) is an expiation for sins, just as it removes ritual impurities—وَلِيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمْ (and to complete His blessing upon you)—through this purification—لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (so that you may be grateful)—for His blessing.

(1) The Almighty's saying: وَلا مُتَّخِذِي أَخْدانٍ (nor those who take secret lovers). Meaning: Nor those who single out one prostitute (بغيّة واحدة) whom he takes as a secret lover (خادنها) and she takes him as a secret lover (خادنته), taking her for himself as a friend with whom he commits immorality (يفجر بها).

(2) He said in Majma' al-Bayan: Its meaning is: "If you intend (أردتم) to stand up for prayer while you are not in a state of purity (على غير طهر)." The intention (الإرادة) was omitted because the context indicates it. Similar to this is His saying: فَإِذا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللهِ (So when you recite the Quran, seek the protection of Allah) and وَإِذا كُنْتَ فِيهِمْ فَأَقَمْتَ لَهُمُ الصَّلاةَ (And when you are among them and establish the prayer for them). This is the view of Ibn Abbas and most of the exegetes (المفسّرين).

(1) His saying yubattin (يبطّن)—with the doubling of the ta' (تشديد الطاء)—is derived from battana yabtin (بطّن يبطن), meaning when he introduces water beneath the part that is covered by its hair.

(1) The hesitation (التّرديد) is from the narrator. Al-Tawr (التَّوْر)—with fathah then sukūn—is a small vessel (إناء صغير) made of copper (صفر) or pottery (خزف) from which one drinks, performs wudu' (يتوضأ), and eats. (2) Al-Shirāk (الشِراك)—with kasrah on the shīn (كسر الشّين)—is one of the straps of the sandal (النعل) that is on its surface, by which the foot is secured. From this is the narration: "Do not put your hand under the shirāk," meaning the strap of the sandal (شراك النّعل).

(1) Khafiya (خفي)—like radiya (رضي)—khifā'an (خفاءً), so it is khāfin (خاف) and khafiyy (خفيّ), meaning it did not appear.

(1) Qarnik (قرنك)—with fathah on the qāf (فتح القاف) and sukūn on the rā' (سكون الراء)—meaning the top of your head (أعلى رأسك).

(٧) وَاذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (7) and remember Allah’s blessing upon you (by means of) Islam, so that the Benefactor may remember you and encourage you to thank Him. وَمِيثاقَهُ الَّذِي واثَقَكُمْ بِهِ ** and His covenant which He made with you, It is said that this means (the covenant made) at the time of your acceptance of Islam, that you would obey Allah in whatever He imposes upon you, whether it pleases you or displeases you. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The intent of the covenant (الميثاق) is what was clarified for them during the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجّة الوداع), concerning the prohibition of forbidden things (المحرّمات), the manner of purification (الطّهارة), the obligation of the Guardianship (الولاية), and other matters. I say: And this is included within that. إِذْ قُلْتُمْ سَمِعْنا وَأَطَعْنا. when you said, ‘We hear and obey.’ Al-Qummi said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took the covenant (الميثاق) from them regarding the Guardianship (الولاية), they said, سَمِعْنا وَأَطَعْنا (We hear and obey), then they broke His covenant. وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ في إنساء نعمته ونقض ميثاقه إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ And be wary of Allah; indeed Allah knows best what is in the breasts. (He knows) their hidden aspects, let alone the manifest aspects of your deeds.

footnote (١) The *qarn* (*القرن*) is the side of the head.

(٨) **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ لِلَّهِ شُهَداءَ بِالْقِسْطِ ** (8) O you who have faith! Be maintainers of justice, witnesses for the sake of Allah, even if it should be against yourselves or your parents and relatives. Whether [the person concerned is] rich or poor, Allah is worthier of both [than you are]. So do not follow [your] desires, lest you should be unfair. If you twist [the testimony] or abstain [from giving it], Allah is indeed well aware of what you do.

Its interpretation has passed.

وَلا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ (And let not incite you) — meaning: And let it not carry you — شَنَآنُ قَوْمٍ ** (the hatred of a people) — meaning: the intensity of their enmity and hatred — عَلى أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا ** (to not be just) so that you transgress against them by committing what is unlawful (لا يحلّ), such as mutilation (مُثْلَة), slander (قَذْف), killing women and children (صِبْيَة), and breaking a covenant (نَقْض عَهْد), seeking gratification (تَشَفّياً) for what is in your hearts. اعْدِلُوا ** (Be just) in [dealing with] your allies and your enemies. هُوَ أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوى وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ إِنَّ اللهَ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ ** (It is closer to piety, and be wary of Allah. Indeed, Allah is well aware of what you do.) so He will recompense you. It was said that the repetition of this ruling is either due to the difference in the reason (السّبب), as it was said that the first [Ayah] was revealed concerning the polytheists (المشركين) and this one concerning the Jews (اليهود), or it is for the sake of increased attention to justice (العدل) and emphasis (المبالغة) on extinguishing the flame (نَائِرَة) of rage (الغيظ).

(٩) وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ. (9) Allah has promised those who have faith and do righteous deeds that there will be forgiveness for them and a great reward. (١٠) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَحِيمِ ** (10) But those who are faithless and deny Our signs—they shall be the inmates of the Blaze. He contrasted the promise (الوعد) with the threat (الوعيد) in fulfillment of the right of the call (الدّعوة). (١١) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ هَمَّ قَوْمٌ أَنْ يَبْسُطُوا ** (11) O you who have faith! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you, when a people intended to stretch out They should strike/assault (يبطشوا) إِلَيْكُمْ أَيْدِيَهُمْ ** their hands against you, with killing and destruction (الإِهلاك) فَكَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنْكُمْ ** but He restrained their hands from you. He prevented them from extending them towards you and repelled their harm from you (1). Al-Qummi said: He means the people of Mecca before its conquest, and He restrained their hands (فَكَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ) through the peace treaty on the Day of Al-Hudaybiyyah. وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ Be wary of Allah, وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** and in Allah let the faithful put their trust. For He is the Sufficient One (الكافي) for delivering good and repelling evil (الشَرّ).

(١) Al-Batsh (البطش) is seizing quickly, and seizing with violence and might (سطوة).

(١٢) وَلَقَدْ أَخَذَ اللهُ مِيثاقَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ وَبَعَثْنا مِنْهُمُ اثْنَيْ عَشَرَ نَقِيباً ** (12) And certainly Allah took a pledge from the Children of Israel, and We raised among them twelve chieftains. a guarantor (كفيلاً), trustworthy (أميناً), a witness (شاهداً) from every tribe, who investigates (1) the conditions of his people, searches for them, and knows their merits (مناقبهم).

وَقالَ اللهُ إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ ** And Allah said, 'I am with you: with support (بالنّصرة).

لَئِنْ أَقَمْتُمُ الصَّلاةَ وَآتَيْتُمُ الزَّكاةَ وَآمَنْتُمْ بِرُسُلِي ** If you establish the prayer and give the zakat and believe in My apostles and confirm them (وصدّقتموهم).

وَعَزَّرْتُمُوهُمْ ** and assist them and support them (ونصرتموهم) and strengthen them (وقوّيتموهم).

وَأَقْرَضْتُمُ اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً ** and lend Allah a good loan, by spending in His way (بالانفاق في سبيله).

لَأُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْكُمْ سَيِّئاتِكُمْ وَلَأُدْخِلَنَّكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ فَمَنْ كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ مِنْكُمْ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَواءَ السَّبِيلِ I will surely absolve you of your misdeeds, and I will admit you into gardens with streams running in them. But whoever of you disbelieves after that has certainly strayed from the right way.'

It is said that Allah commanded the Children of Israel, after the destruction of Pharaoh in Egypt, to march to Jericho (أريحا) in the land of Syria (الشّام), which was inhabited by the giants (الجبابرة). He said: "I have decreed it for you as a dwelling (قراراً)." And He commanded Musa (peace be upon him) to take from every tribe a chieftain (نَقِيباً) who would be a guarantor (كفيلاً) over his people for fulfilling what they were commanded: marching out against the giants (الجبابرة) and engaging in Jihad (الجهاد), and who would be a leader (قائداً) and chief (رئيساً) for them. So, he chose the chieftains (النّقباء), took the covenant (الميثاق) from the Children of Israel, and guaranteed (تَكَفَّلَ) it for them. He marched with them, and when he drew near their land, he sent the chieftains (النّقباء) to spy (يتجسّسون). They saw enormous bodies (اجراماً عظاماً) and great strength (وقوّة). They returned and informed Musa (peace be upon him) of this, so he commanded them to conceal it. However, they spoke of it to their people, except for Kalib ibn Yufanna (كالب بن يوفنَّا) from the tribe of Judah (يهودا) and Yusha' ibn Nun (يوشع بن نون) from the tribe of Ephraim (افرائيم) son of Yusuf, and both of them were among the chieftains (النقباء).

(١٣) فَبِما نَقْضِهِمْ مِيثاقَهُمْ لَعَنَّاهُمْ ** (13) So, for their breaking their covenant, We cursed them We expelled them from Our mercy. وَجَعَلْنا قُلُوبَهُمْ قاسِيَةً ** and made their hearts hard. They are not affected by the signs (الآيات) and the warnings (النّذر). And it was recited (قُرِئَ) as (قَسِيَّة) for emphasis (المبالغة). يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ عَنْ مَواضِعِهِ وَنَسُوا حَظًّا ** They distort the words from their meanings, and have forgotten a part They abandoned an ample share (نصيباً وافِراً). مِمَّا ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ وَلا تَزالُ تَطَّلِعُ عَلى خائِنَةٍ مِنْهُمْ ** of what they were reminded of. You will not cease to come across treachery from them, Treachery (خيانة) or a treacherous group (فرقة خائنة). إِلَّا قَلِيلاً مِنْهُمْ لم يخونوا except for a few of them who did not commit treachery. فَاعْفُ عَنْهُمْ وَاصْفَحْ إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** except a few of them. Yet forgive them and forbear. Indeed, Allah loves the virtuous. This is an encouragement (حث) towards forbearance (الصّفح). Al-Qummi said it is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ**. Then kill the polytheists.

(١٤) وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ قالُوا إِنَّا نَصارى ** (14) And from those who say, 'We are Christians,' They claimed the support (نصرة) of Allah by marking themselves (بالاتِّسام) with this name, أَخَذْنا مِيثاقَهُمْ We took their covenant, just as We took [covenant] from those before them among the Children of Israel فَنَسُوا حَظًّا مِمَّا ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ فَأَغْرَيْنا ** then they forgot a part of what they were reminded of. So We stirred up meaning, We made binding بَيْنَهُمُ الْعَداوَةَ ** among them enmity through actions (بالأفعال) وَالْبَغْضاءَ ** and hatred through hearts (بالقلوب) إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ وَسَوْفَ يُنَبِّئُهُمُ اللهُ بِما كانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ ** until the Day of Resurrection. And soon Allah will inform them of what they have been doing. through recompense (بالجزاء) and punishment (والعقاب).

footnote (١) It is said *naqqabū* (*نَقَّبُوا*) in the lands, meaning they entered its *nuqūb* (*نُقُوب*), that is, its paths (*طُرُق*), seeking escape. And the *naqīb* (*نَقِيب*) of the people is like the guarantor (*الكفيل*) and the surety (*الضّمين*). He searches (*يُنَقِّب*) for secrets (*الأسرار*) and hidden intentions (*مكنون الأضمار*). He was only called *naqīb* (*نَقِيب*) because he knows the inner workings (*دَخِيلَة*) of the people's affairs and knows the way to understand their matters.

(15) يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ قَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ كَثِيراً مِمَّا كُنْتُمْ تُخْفُونَ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** (O People of the Book! Our Apostle has certainly come to you, clarifying for you much of what you used to conceal of the Book) [This includes] the description (نعت) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, the verse of stoning (آية الرّجم) in the Torah (التّورية), and the glad tidings (بشارة) of Jesus concerning Ahmad صلى الله عليه وآله in the Gospel (الإنجيل). وَيَعْفُوا عَنْ كَثِيرٍ** (and excusing much) of what you conceal, which He does not inform about.

Al-Qummi said: The Prophet clarifies much of what you concealed, which is in the Torah concerning His news, and He leaves much that He does not clarify.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السّلام, concerning the interpretation of يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ لا يَحْزُنْكَ الَّذِينَ يُسارِعُونَ فِي الْكُفْرِ ** (O Apostle! Do not grieve for those who are active in disbelief) from this Surah: A woman from Khaybar, who was of high standing among them, committed adultery with a man from their nobles, and both were married (محصنان). They disliked stoning them (رجمهما), so they sent [messengers] to the Jews of Medina and wrote to them to ask the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله about this, hoping that He would provide them with a concession (رخصة). A group of them departed, including Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf, Ka'b ibn Usayd, Shu'bah ibn 'Amr, Malik ibn al-Dayf, Kinanah ibn Abi al-Huqayq, and others. They said: "O Muhammad, inform us about the adulterer and the adulteress, if they are married (أحصنا), what is their prescribed punishment (حدّهما)?" He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Will you accept My judgment concerning that?" They said: "Yes." Then Gabriel descended with the command of stoning (الرّجم). He informed them of this, but they refused to accept it. Gabriel عليه السلام then said: "Appoint Ibn Suriya as a judge between you and Him," and described him to Him. The Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله said: "Do you know a beardless (أمرد), white, one-eyed young man who resides in Fadak, called Ibn Suriya?" They said: "Yes." He said: "What kind of man is he among you?" They said: "He is the most knowledgeable Jew remaining on the face of the earth concerning what Allah revealed to Moses." He said: "Then send for him." They did so, and 'Abdullah ibn Suriya came to them. The Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله said to him: "I adjure you by Allah, there is no god but He, Who revealed the Torah (التَّوراة) to Moses, split the sea for you and saved you, drowned the family of Pharaoh, shaded you with the clouds, and sent down upon you the manna and the quails (المنّ والسّلوى): Do you find in your Book the stoning (الرّجم) for the married person (من أحصن)?" Ibn Suriya said: "Yes, by Him Whom you reminded me of! Were it not for the fear that the Lord of the Torah would burn me if I lied or altered [the truth], I would not have confessed to you. But inform me, how is it in your Book, O Muhammad?" He صلىّ الله عليه وآله said: "If four trustworthy men (رهط عدول) testify that he has inserted it into her, just as the kohl stick (الميل) enters the kohl container (المكحلة), stoning becomes obligatory upon him." Ibn Suriya said: "Thus did Allah reveal it in the Torah to Moses عليه السّلام." The Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله then said to him: "What was the first thing you made a concession (ترخّصتم) regarding Allah's command?" He said: "When a noble person committed adultery, we would leave him, but when we apprehended a weak person, we would carry out the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) upon him. Adultery became widespread among our nobles until the cousin of one of our kings committed adultery, and we did not stone him. Then another man committed adultery, and the king wanted to stone him. His people said to him: 'No, not until you stone so-and-so,' meaning his cousin. So we said: 'Let us gather and establish something less than stoning, which applies to both the noble and the commoner.' So we established lashing (الجلد) and blackening (التّحميم) (1), which is that he is lashed forty lashes, then their faces are blackened, then they are mounted on two donkeys with their faces turned toward the donkey's rear, and they are paraded around. They made this a substitute for stoning." The Jews said to Ibn Suriya: "How quickly you informed Him! You were not worthy of the praise we gave you, but you were absent, and we disliked backbiting you." He said: "He adjured me by the Torah, and were it not for that, I would not have informed Him." So the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله commanded that they be stoned at the door of His mosque, and He said: "I am the first to revive your command when they caused it to die." Then Allah, the Glorified, revealed concerning this: يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ قَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ كَثِيراً مِمَّا كُنْتُمْ تُخْفُونَ مِنَ الْكِتابِ وَيَعْفُوا عَنْ كَثِيرٍ ** (O People of the Book! Our Apostle has certainly come to you, clarifying for you much of what you used to conceal of the Book, and excusing much)

Ibn Suriya then stood up and placed his hand on the knees of the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله, then said: "This is the position of one seeking refuge with Allah and with you, that you mention to us the much that you were commanded to excuse." The Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله turned away from that. قَدْ جاءَكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ نُورٌ وَكِتابٌ مُبِينٌ ** (There has certainly come to you a light from Allah, and a manifest Book) It was said that the Light (النّور) is Muhammad صلىّ الله عليه وآله and the Book (الكتاب) is the Quran. And it was said that both of them are the Quran, and this is supported by the singular pronoun in به (in it/by it). Al-Qummi said: He means by the Light (النّور) the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams عليهم السّلام.

(١٦) يَهْدِي بِهِ اللهُ مَنِ اتَّبَعَ رِضْوانَهُ سُبُلَ السَّلامِ ** the paths of safety from the punishment, وَيُخْرِجُهُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ the types of disbelief (الكفر), إِلَى النُّورِ through Islam (بالإِسلام), بِإِذْنِهِ ** by His Will (بإرادته) and His granting of success (توفيقه), **وَيَهْدِيهِمْ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** a path which is the nearest of paths to Allah and to His Paradise.

(16) By it Allah guides those who follow His pleasure to the ways of peace, and brings them out of darkness into light by His leave, and guides them to a straight path.

(١٧) لَقَدْ كَفَرَ الَّذِينَ قالُوا إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ قُلْ فَمَنْ يَمْلِكُ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً ** (17) Certainly they have disbelieved who say, 'Allah is the Messiah, son of Mary.' Say, 'Who can withhold anything from Allah, Who can prevent anything from His power and His will (إرادته)? إِنْ أَرادَ أَنْ يُهْلِكَ الْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ.** if He wishes to destroy the Messiah, son of Mary, and his mother, and everyone on the earth?' To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them. He creates whatever He wishes, and Allah has power over all things.

footnote (١). حمّم رأسه إذا اسودّ بعد الحلق وحممت الرّجل سخّمت وجهه بالفحم. (1). حَمَّمَ رَأْسَهُ (He blackened his head) means when it turns black after shaving, and حَمَّمْتُ الرَّجُلَ (I blackened the man) means I blackened his face with charcoal (بالفحم).

(18) وَقالَتِ الْيَهُودُ وَالنَّصارى نَحْنُ أَبْناءُ اللهِ وَأَحِبَّاؤُهُ ** (The Jews and the Christians say, 'We are the sons of Allah and His beloved ones.') (They are) the followers (أشياع) of His two alleged sons, Uzayr and the Messiah. Say: 'Why then does He punish you for your sins?' in this world by killing, captivity, and metamorphosis (المسخ), and in the Hereafter by the Fire for a number of days (أيّاماً معدودة). بَلْ أَنْتُمْ بَشَرٌ مِمَّنْ خَلَقَ يَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (No, you are humans from among those He has created. He forgives whomever He wishes, and punishes whomever He wishes.) And they are those who disbelieved. The meaning is that He treats you with the same treatment as the rest of mankind; you have no distinction (مزيّة) over them. وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما ** (To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them,) all of it is equal in being His creation (خلقاً) and His dominion (ملكاً). وَإِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ ** (and toward Him is the ultimate return.) So He will recompense each one of you for what he earned (بما كسَبَ).

(١٩) يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ قَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ ** (19) O People of the Book! Our Apostle has certainly come to you, clarifying for you what requires clarification عَلى فَتْرَةٍ مِنَ الرُّسُلِ upon a cessation (فُتُور) of sending [messengers] and an interruption (انْقِطَاع) of revelation (الوَحْي). Al-Saduq (may his resting place be pleasant) said in Ikmal al-Din: The meaning of the gap (الفَتْرَة) is that there is no Prophet (نَبِيّ) or Successor (وَصِيّ) who is manifest and well-known (ظَاهِر مَشْهُور). And indeed, between our Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله and Jesus عليه السّلام, there were Prophets (أَنْبِيَاء) and Imams (أَئِمَّة) who were concealed (مَسْتُورُون) and fearful (خَائِفُون). Among them was Khalid ibn Sinan al-Absi (خَالِد بْن سِنَان الْعَبْسِي), whom no one can refute (يَدْفَعُهُ دَافِع) or deny (يُنْكِرُهُ مُنْكِر). And there were fifty years between his mission (مَبْعَثه) and the mission (مَبْعَث) of our Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله. I say: The confirmation of this is the saying of the Commander of the Faithful عليه السّلام: "The earth is never devoid of one who stands for Allah with a proof (حُجَّة), either manifest and well-known (ظَاهِر مَشْهُور) or fearful and concealed (خَائِف مَغْمُور)." أَنْ تَقُولُوا ما جاءَنا مِنْ بَشِيرٍ وَلا نَذِيرٍ ** lest you should say, 'There did not come to us any bearer of good news or warner.' [This was done] lest you should say that and use it as an excuse (تَعْتَذِرُوا بِهِ). فَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ بَشِيرٌ وَنَذِيرٌ So, a bearer of good news and a warner has certainly come to you, so do not offer excuses. وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ and Allah is capable of all things. It has passed in Surat An-Nisa (Chapter of Women) regarding His saying: فَكَيْفَ إِذا جِئْنا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ ** (How will it be when We bring a witness from every nation) that the nations on the Day of Resurrection (يَوْم الْقِيَامَة) will deny the delivery of the messages (رِسَالَات) of their Apostles and will say: ما جاءَنا مِنْ بَشِيرٍ وَلا نَذِيرٍ ** (There did not come to us any bearer of good news or warner). And the Apostles will call upon our Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله to bear witness, so our Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله will say to every nation: "Yes, فَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ بَشِيرٌ وَنَذِيرٌ وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** (a bearer of good news and a warner has certainly come to you, and Allah is capable of all things)." Meaning: He is powerful (مُقْتَدِر) over the testimony (شَهَادَة) of your limbs (جَوَارِح) against you regarding the Apostles having delivered their messages (رِسَالَاتِهِم) to you.

(٢٠) وَإِذْ قالَ مُوسى لِقَوْمِهِ يا قَوْمِ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ جَعَلَ فِيكُمْ أَنْبِياءَ وَجَعَلَكُمْ مُلُوكاً وَآتاكُمْ ما لَمْ يُؤْتِ أَحَداً مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ ** (20) And when Moses said to his people, 'O my people, remember Allah's blessing upon you, when He appointed prophets among you, and made you kings, and gave you what He has not given to anyone in the world.) (This includes) the splitting of the sea, the shading of the clouds, the sending down of the mann and the salwa (المنّ والسّلوى), and other things besides that.

(21) يا قَوْمِ ادْخُلُوا الْأَرْضَ الْمُقَدَّسَةَ. (O my people! Enter the holy land) Al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means al-Sham (الشّام). الَّتِي كَتَبَ اللهُ لَكُمْ ** (which Allah has ordained for you) that it be a dwelling place for you. Al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah said to the Children of Israel: "Enter the holy land (الْأَرْضَ الْمُقَدَّسَةَ)," but they did not enter it until He forbade it upon them and upon their prophets, and only the sons of their sons entered it. And narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): He ordained it for them, then He erased it. وَلا تَرْتَدُّوا عَلى أَدْبارِكُمْ ** (and do not turn your backs)—meaning, do not return retreating—فَتَنْقَلِبُوا خاسِرِينَ (lest you turn back as losers) of the reward of both abodes (الدَّارين).

(٢٢) قالُوا يا مُوسى إِنَّ فِيها قَوْماً جَبَّارِينَ ** (22) They said, 'O Moses, there are a tyrannical people in it. severe in striking (البطش), despair (اليأس), and physique (الخلق); their resistance is not possible for us. وَإِنَّا لَنْ نَدْخُلَها حَتَّى يَخْرُجُوا مِنْها فَإِنْ يَخْرُجُوا مِنْها فَإِنَّا داخِلُونَ ** and we will not enter it until they leave it. If they leave it, then we will enter it.' since we have no capacity for them.

(٢٣) قالَ رَجُلانِ ** (23) Two men said They are Yusha' ibn Nun and Kalib ibn Yufanna, and they were cousins. This is according to what is narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), reported by al-Ayyashi. مِنَ الَّذِينَ يَخافُونَ ** from those who fear Meaning, they fear Allah and are pious (يَتَّقُونَهُ). Allah bestowed His favor upon them both with faith (الإيمان) and steadfastness (التثبيت). ادْخُلُوا عَلَيْهِمُ الْبابَ ** enter upon them through the gate The gate of their town; meaning: surprise them, press them in the narrow passage (المضيق), and prevent them from reaching the open ground (الإصحار) (1). فَإِذا دَخَلْتُمُوهُ فَإِنَّكُمْ غالِبُونَ ** for when you enter it, you will be victors Due to the difficulty of maneuvering (الكرّ) for them in the narrow passages (المضايق) because of the size of their bodies, and because they are bodies without hearts [i.e., lacking courage]. وَعَلَى اللهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوا ** and put your trust in Allah In His help against the tyrants (الجبّارين). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** if you are faithful In Him and believing in His promise.

(٢٤) قالُوا يا مُوسى إِنَّا لَنْ نَدْخُلَها أَبَداً ما دامُوا فِيها فَاذْهَبْ أَنْتَ وَرَبُّكَ فَقاتِلا إِنَّا هاهُنا قاعِدُونَ ** (24) They said, 'O Moses, we will never enter it as long as they are in it. Go, you and your Lord, and fight! We will wait here.' They said this out of contempt (استهانة) for Allah and His Messenger, and a lack of concern (عدم مبالاة) for both of Them. (٢٥) قالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي لا أَمْلِكُ إِلَّا نَفْسِي وَأَخِي فَافْرُقْ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَ الْقَوْمِ الْفاسِقِينَ. (25) He said, 'My Lord, I have control only over myself and my brother. So separate us from the transgressing people.' (٢٦) قالَ فَإِنَّها مُحَرَّمَةٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (26) He said, 'It shall be forbidden them for forty years, during which they shall wander in the land. So do not grieve for the transgressing people.' (It shall be forbidden them) meaning: they shall not enter it nor possess it due to their disobedience, أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً يَتِيهُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ (for forty years, during which they shall wander in the land), meaning: they will travel in it bewildered (متحيّرين), seeing no path. فَلا تَأْسَ عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْفاسِقِينَ ** (So do not grieve for the transgressing people), because they deserve that (أحقّاء بذلك) due to their transgression (لفسقهم).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By Him in Whose hand is my soul, you shall certainly follow the ways (سنن) of those who were before you, exactly like one sandal follows the other, and one feather follows the other (حذوا النعل بالنعل والقذّة بالقذّة), until you do not miss their path, nor does the way (سنة) of the Children of Israel miss you." Then Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: Moses said to his people: يا قَوْمِ ادْخُلُوا الْأَرْضَ الْمُقَدَّسَةَ الَّتِي كَتَبَ اللهُ لَكُمْ ** (O my people, enter the holy land which Allah has ordained for you). But they rejected him, and they were six hundred thousand. قالُوا يا مُوسى إِنَّ فِيها قَوْماً جَبَّارِينَ ** (They said, 'O Moses, there are a tyrannical people in it) — the verses. He said: So all disobeyed except forty thousand, and Harun and his two sons, Joshua son of Nun (يشوع بن نون), and Caleb son of Yufanna (كالب بن يوفنا) submitted. So Allah named them transgressors (فاسقين) and said: فَلا تَأْسَ عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْفاسِقِينَ ** (So do not grieve for the transgressing people). So they wandered for forty years because they disobeyed. This was exactly like one sandal follows the other (حذو النّعل بالنّعل): when the Messenger of Allah was taken (died), no one remained upon the command of Allah except Ali, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them), Salman, al-Miqdad, and Abu Dharr. So they remained for forty (years) until Ali arose and fought those who opposed him.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "The best land is Syria (الشّام), and the worst people are its inhabitants. And the worst land is Egypt (مصر). Indeed, it is the prison of those upon whom Allah is wrathful. The entry of the Children of Israel was nothing but an act of disobedience (معصية) on their part toward Allah, because Allah said: ادْخُلُوا الْأَرْضَ الْمُقَدَّسَةَ الَّتِي كَتَبَ اللهُ لَكُمْ (enter the holy land which Allah has ordained for you), meaning Syria (الشّام).
