But they refused to enter it, so they wandered in the land for forty years, in Egypt (مصر) and in it (فيها). Then they entered it after forty years." He said: "Their departure from Egypt and their entry into Syria was only after their repentance and Allah's satisfaction (رضاءِ اللهِ) with them."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), mentioning Moses and their saying: فَاذْهَبْ أَنْتَ وَرَبُّكَ فَقاتِلا إِنَّا هاهُنا قاعِدُونَ ** (Go, you and your Lord, and fight! We will wait here). He said: So Allah forbade it to them for forty years and caused them to wander (وتيّههم). When it was evening (العشاء) and they began to travel, they would call out: "Travel! Travel! Haste (الوحا)! Haste (الوحا)!" (1) They continued like this until the sun set. When they had departed and the land was level beneath them, Allah the Almighty said to the earth: "Turn them around (ديري بهم)." They continued like this until they reached the time before dawn (أسحروا) and morning approached. They would say: "You have reached this water, so dismount." But when morning came, behold, it was their place of wandering (تيههم) (2) and the dwellings they had been in yesterday. Some of them would say to others: "O people, you have certainly gone astray (ضلّلتم) and missed the path." They continued like this until Allah permitted them, and they entered it, though He had already ordained it for them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: Moses, the Speaker of Allah (كليم الله), died in the wandering (في التّيه). A caller cried out from the heaven: "Moses has died, and what soul does not die?"

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Harun died before Moses, and they both died in the wandering (في التّيه). And in al-Qummi: When Moses (peace be upon him) intended to leave them, they became alarmed (فزعوا) and said: "If Moses leaves us, punishment will descend upon us." So they rushed to him (ففزعوا إليه) and asked him to stay with them and to ask Allah to accept their repentance.

(٢٧) وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ ** (27) Recite to them the account of Adam’s two sons Qabil and Habil بِالْحَقِ ** (with truth) meaning, with veracity (بالصدق). إِذْ قَرَّبا قُرْباناً (when they offered a sacrifice). The qurban (القربان) is that by which one seeks nearness (يتقرب) to Allah, whether it be a sacrificial animal (ذبيحة) or otherwise. فَتُقُبِّلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِما ** (It was accepted from one of them) because he was content with the judgment of Allah, purified his intention (النّية) for Allah, and aimed for the best of what he possessed, and that was Habil. وَلَمْ يُتَقَبَّلْ مِنَ الْآخِرِ ** (and not accepted from the other.) because he was displeased with the judgment of Allah, did not purify his intention (النّية) in his sacrifice (قربانه), and intended the worst of what he possessed, and that was Qabil. قالَ لَأَقْتُلَنَّكَ ** (He said, 'I will surely kill you.') (1) He threatened him with murder due to the excessiveness of his envy (حسده) towards him for the acceptance of his sacrifice (قربانه). قالَ إِنَّما يَتَقَبَّلُ اللهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ ** (He said, 'Allah accepts only from the Godwary.') Meaning: You were afflicted only from your own side by abandoning piety (التقوى), not from my side. It is said that in this there is an indication that the envious person (الحاسد) ought to see his deprivation as stemming from his own shortcoming (تقصيره) and strive to attain that by which the envied person (المحسود) became fortunate, except regarding the removal of his fortune, for that is something that harms him and does not benefit him. And that obedience (الطاعة) is not accepted except from a believing, God-fearing person (مؤمن متق).

(٢٨) لَئِنْ بَسَطْتَ إِلَيَّ يَدَكَ لِتَقْتُلَنِي ما أَنَا بِباسِطٍ يَدِيَ ** (28) Should you stretch out your hand toward me to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand toward you to kill you. وقرء يدي بإسكان الياءِ إِلَيْكَ لِأَقْتُلَكَ إِنِّي أَخافُ اللهَ رَبَّ الْعالَمِينَ. And it was recited يدي (my hand) with the sukun (إسكان) on the yā’ (الياءِ). إِلَيْكَ لِأَقْتُلَكَ إِنِّي أَخافُ اللهَ رَبَّ الْعالَمِينَ. (28) ...to kill you. Indeed, I fear Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.

(٢٩) إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ ** (29) I desire that you should be laden أن ترجع بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ فَتَكُونَ مِنْ أَصْحابِ النَّارِ وَذلِكَ جَزاءُ الظَّالِمِينَ ** that you return (أن ترجع) بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ فَتَكُونَ مِنْ أَصْحابِ النَّارِ وَذلِكَ جَزاءُ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (29) ...with my sin and your sin, and thus become an inhabitant of the Fire, and that is the requital of the wrongdoers. Perhaps his primary intention (غرضه بالذات) was that this (sin) should not be for him (the speaker), not that it should be for his brother.

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Whoever kills a believer, Allah establishes all the sins upon his killer and absolves the slain person from them. And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ فَتَكُونَ مِنْ أَصْحابِ النَّارِ. (I desire that you should be laden with my sin and your sin, and thus become an inhabitant of the Fire.)"

(٣٠) فَطَوَّعَتْ لَهُ نَفْسُهُ قَتْلَ أَخِيهِ فَقَتَلَهُ فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ (30) Then his soul prompted him (2) [meaning: it made easy for him] the killing of his brother and he killed him, and became one of the losers in religion and in the world, since he remained for the duration of his life expelled, sorrowful, regretful.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: that Hawwa', the wife of Adam, used to give birth in every pregnancy to a boy and a girl. So, in the first pregnancy, she gave birth to Qabil (and it is said Qabin) and his twin sister Iqlima, daughter of Adam. And in the second pregnancy, Habil and his twin sister Lyudha. When they all reached maturity, Allah the Almighty commanded Adam to marry Qabil to Habil's sister, and Habil to Qabil's sister. Habil agreed, but Qabil refused because his sister was the more beautiful of the two. And he said: "Allah did not command this, rather this is from your own opinion." So, He commanded them both to offer a sacrifice (قرباناً), and they agreed to that. Habil, who was a shepherd, proceeded and took the best of his sheep, butter, and milk. And Qabil, who was a farmer, took the worst of his crops. Then they ascended and placed the two sacrifices on the mountain. Then the fire came and consumed Habil's sacrifice and avoided Qabil's sacrifice. Adam was absent in Mecca, having gone there to visit the House (البيت) by the command of his Lord. Qabil said: "May you not live, O Habil, in this world, since your sacrifice was accepted and my sacrifice was not accepted. And you want to take my beautiful sister, while I take your ugly sister." Habil said to him what Allah the Almighty narrated [in the Quran]. Then he smashed him with a stone and killed him.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, in a tradition whose beginning preceded in the first part of Surat al-Nisa' (Chapter of Women): It was said to him: "They claim that Qabil only killed Habil because they were jealous over their sister." He said: "Do you say this? Do you not feel ashamed to narrate this against the Prophet of Allah, Adam?" So it was asked: "Then why did Qabil kill Habil?" He replied: "Because of the trusteeship (الوصية)." Then he said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed to Adam that he should hand over the trusteeship (الوصية) and the Greatest Name of Allah (اسم الله الأعظم) to Habil." Qabil was the elder, and when this reached Qabil, he became angry and said: "I am more deserving of the honor (الكرامة) and the trusteeship (الوصية)." So, He commanded them both to offer a sacrifice (قرباناً) by way of revelation from Allah to Him (Adam). They did so, and Allah accepted Habil's sacrifice. Qabil envied him and killed him.

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: After mentioning their two sacrifices, [he said]: The sacrifice, when accepted, would be consumed by fire. Qabil proceeded and built a house for it (the fire), and he was the first person to build houses for fire. And he said: "I shall worship this fire until my sacrifice is accepted." Then, the enemy of Allah, Iblis, said to Qabil: "Habil's sacrifice has been accepted, but your sacrifice has not been accepted. If you leave him, he will have descendants who will boast over your descendants." So Qabil killed him. When Adam returned, he said to him: "O Qabil, where is Habil?" He replied: "I do not know. You did not send me as a guardian over him." Adam went and found Habil killed. He said: "Cursed be the land that accepted the blood of Habil." Adam wept over Habil for forty nights. And in al-Kafi, narrated from him عليه السلام: what is similar in meaning.

footnote (١) There is an omission and estimation in the speech, meaning: The one whose sacrifice was not accepted said to the one whose sacrifice was accepted: "I will surely kill you." So he said to him: "Why will you kill me?" He replied: "Because your sacrifice was accepted and my sacrifice was not accepted." He said to him: "What is my sin? **إِنَّما يَتَقَبَّلُ اللهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ.** (Indeed, Allah accepts only from the Godwary.)"

footnote (٢) Meaning: it encouraged him and beautified it for him. It is also said: it permitted it and made it easy for him, derived from (*أطاع له المرتع*) (the pasture became easy for him) when it was vast.

فَبَعَثَ اللهُ غُراباً يَبْحَثُ فِي الْأَرْضِ لِيُرِيَهُ كَيْفَ يُوارِي سَوْأَةَ أَخِيهِ (١) (31) Then Allah sent a raven, scratching the earth, to show him how to conceal the corpse of his brother. قالَ يا وَيْلَتى أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هذَا الْغُرابِ فَأُوارِيَ سَوْأَةَ أَخِي فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ (٢) concerning his killing of him. He said, 'Woe to me! Was I unable to be like this raven and conceal the corpse of my brother?' So he became one of the remorseful.

Al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), after mentioning their sacrifices, said: He did not know how to kill him until Iblis came and taught him. He said: "Place his head between two stones, then crush it." When he killed him, he did not know what to do with him. Then two ravens came and fought until one of them killed its companion. Then the one that remained scratched the earth with its claws and buried its companion in it. Qabil said: يا وَيْلَتى ** (Woe to me!)—the verse—so he dug a pit for him and buried him in it. This became the tradition (سنة) by which the dead are buried. Qabil returned to his father, and Adam did not see Habil with him. Adam said to him: "Where did you leave my son?" Qabil said to him: "Did you send me as a shepherd over him?" Adam said: "Come with me to the place of the sacrifice." And Adam's heart sensed (أَوْجَسَ) (3) what Qabil had done. When they reached the place of the sacrifice, the killing became manifest. Adam cursed the earth that accepted Habil's blood, and Adam was commanded to curse Qabil. Qabil was called out to from the heaven: "You are cursed, just as you killed your brother." That is why the earth does not absorb blood. Adam departed and wept over Habil for forty days and nights. When he grieved over him, he complained of that to Allah. So, He revealed to him: "I shall grant you a male child who will be a successor (خلفاً) to Habil." Hawwa' gave birth to a blessed boy. When the seventh day arrived, Allah revealed to him: "O Adam, this boy is a gift (هبة) from Me to you, so name him Hibbat Allah (هبة الله)." So he named him Hibbat Allah (هبة الله).

In al-Majma', the general populace (العامّة) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Qabil killed Habil and left him in the open ground (بالعراءِ) (4), not knowing what to do with him. The wild beasts intended him, so he carried him in a sack (جراب) (5) on his back until he began to stink (أروح) (6). The birds and wild beasts gathered (عكفت) (7) around him, waiting for him to discard him so they could eat him. Then Allah sent two ravens, and they fought. One of them killed its companion, then it dug for it with its beak and its legs, then it threw it into the pit and concealed it, while Qabil was watching it. So he buried his brother.

Al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Qabil, the son of Adam, is suspended by his horns in the eye of the sun, revolving with it wherever it revolves, in its extreme cold (زمهريرها) and its scorching heat (حميمها) until the Day of Resurrection. When the Day of Resurrection comes, He will consign him to the Fire.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The killer son of Adam was mentioned, and he was asked: "What is his state? Is he among the people of the Fire?" He said: "Glory be to Allah! Allah is more just than that, that He should combine upon him the punishment of this world and the punishment of the Hereafter."

In al-Ihtijaj, Tawus al-Yamani said to Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him): "Do you know on which day one-third of mankind died?" He said: "O servant of Allah, one-third of mankind never died. Rather, you meant one-fourth of mankind." He asked: "How is that?" He said: "There were Adam, Hawwa', Qabil, and Habil. That is one-fourth." He said: "You have spoken the truth." Abu Ja'far said: "Do you know what was done to Qabil?" He said: "No." He said: "He is suspended by the sun, being sprinkled (يَنْضَحُ) (1) with hot water until the Hour is established."

(1) سَوْأَةَ أَخِيهِ ** means his private parts (عورته) and that which is not permissible to be uncovered from his body. And it is said: it means his corpse (جيفته). Al-Sū' (السّوء) is the ugly state (الخالة القبيحة). And يا وَيْلَتى ** is a word of torment (عذاب). It is said: Wailun lahu (ويل له) and Wailahu (ويله), and its meaning is a supplication for destruction (الدعاء بالإِهلاك).

(2) His saying فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ ** (So he became one of the remorseful) concerning his killing of him, but he did not regret it in a manner that constitutes repentance (توبة), like one who regrets drinking because it causes him a headache. This is the nature of his remorse, according to Al-Jubba'i. And it is said: مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ ** (one of the remorseful) concerning his carrying (the body), not concerning his killing. And it is said: مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ ** (one of the remorseful) concerning the death of his brother, not concerning the denial of the sin (الذّنب).

(3) Al-Wajs (الوجس), like al-Wa'd (الوعد), is the fear (الفزع) that falls upon the heart or the hearing from a sound or otherwise, like al-Wajasan (الوَجَسان) and the hidden sound (الصّوت الخفي). And the Almighty's saying فَأَوْجَسَ فِي نَفْسِهِ ** means he sensed (أحسّ) and concealed (أضمَرَ).

(4) Al-'Ara' (العراء), with the long vowel (بالمد), is an open space (فضاء) where no tree or anything else conceals (the person). And it is said: Al-'Ara' (العراء) is the surface of the earth (وجه الأرض).

(5) Al-Jirab (الجراب), with a kasra (بالكسر), is a container (وعاب) made from the skin (إهاب) of a sheep, in which grain (الحبّ) and flour (الدّقيق) and the like are stored.

(6) Arwaha (أَرْوَحَ) means its smell became foul (أنتن ريحه).

(7) 'Akafat 'alayhi al-tayr (عكفت عليه الطير) means the birds gathered (اجتمعت) around him.

(32) مِنْ أَجْلِ ذلِكَ كَتَبْنا عَلى بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** (That is why We decreed for the Children of Israel) Al-Qummi said: The wording of the Ayah is specific to the Children of Israel, but its meaning applies to all people. أَنَّهُ مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْساً بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ ** (that whoever kills a soul, unless it be for murder) without the killing of a soul that necessitates retribution (الاقتصاص). أَوْ فَسادٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (or for corruption in the land) or without corruption (فَسادٍ) therein, such as polytheism (الشرك) and highway robbery (قطع الطريق), فَكَأَنَّما قَتَلَ النَّاسَ جَمِيعاً (it is as if he had killed all mankind), due to his violating the sanctity of blood, establishing the precedent (سنّة) of murder, and emboldening people to commit it.

In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "There is a valley (واد) in Hell (جهنّم). If a person were to kill all mankind, he would only enter that place." It was asked: "What if he kills another?" He replied: "It will be multiplied for him."

And in another narration from him (peace be upon him): "There is a seat (مقعد) in the Fire (النّار). If a person were to kill all mankind, he would not receive more than that seat."

And al-Ayyashi contains what is close to these two narrations. وَمَنْ أَحْياها فَكَأَنَّما أَحْيَا النَّاسَ جَمِيعاً ** (and whoever saves a life, it is as if he had saved all mankind.) And whoever causes the continuation of that life, whether through pardon (عفو) or preventing murder or rescuing from some causes of destruction, it is as if he had done that for all mankind. Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] whoever rescues it from burning (حرق) or drowning (غرق) or collapse (هدم) or a beast (سبع), or supports it until it becomes self-sufficient (يستغني), or brings it out of poverty (فقر) into wealth (غنىً). And better than that is whoever brings it out of error (ضلال) into guidance (هدىً).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation, he said: "[It refers to rescuing] from burning (حرق) or drowning (غرق)." It was asked: "What about whoever brings it out of error (ضلال) into guidance (هدىً)?" He replied: "That is its greatest interpretation (تأويلها الأعظم)."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, the like of it is narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

And from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever brings it out of error (ضلال) into guidance (هدىً) is as if he has revived it, and whoever brings it out of guidance (هدىً) into error (ضلال) has surely killed it." And from him (peace be upon him): "Its greatest interpretation (تأويلها الأعظم) is that he calls it [to guidance] and it responds to him."

And in al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever gives water to drink (سقى الماء) in a place where water is found is like one who frees a slave (أعتق رقبة). And whoever gives water to drink in a place where it is not found is like one who revives a soul (أحيا نفساً). And whoever revives a soul, فَكَأَنَّما أَحْيَا النَّاسَ جَمِيعاً (it is as if he had saved all mankind)." وَلَقَدْ جاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنا بِالْبَيِّناتِ (Certainly Our apostles brought them manifest proofs) the clear ones, after We decreed this great severity upon them, as an affirmation of the command and a renewal of the covenant, so that they might abstain (1) from such crimes (الجنايات). ثُمَّ إِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنْهُمْ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ فِي الْأَرْضِ لَمُسْرِفُونَ ** (Yet, subsequent to that, many of them commit excesses on the earth.) transgressing beyond the truth (الحقّ). In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The excessive ones (المسرفون) are those who deem unlawful things lawful (يستحِلّون المحارم) and shed blood (يسفكون الدّماء).

(1) النّضح is sprinkling (الرشّ). نضحت الثوب نضحاً (I sprinkled the garment, نضحاً being the مصدر from the verb forms ضرب and نفع) means I sprinkled it with water.

(٣٣) **إِنَّما جَزاءُ الَّذِينَ يُحارِبُونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَيَسْعَوْنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَساداً أَنْ يُقَتَّلُوا أَوْ يُصَلَّبُوا أَوْ تُقَطَّعَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُمْ مِنْ خِلافٍ أَوْ يُنْفَوْا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ ** (33) The requital of those who wage war against Allah and His Apostle, and try to bring about corruption in the land, is that they shall be killed or crucified, or have their hands and feet cut off from opposite sides, or be banished from the land.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A group of people from Banu Dhabbah came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while they were sick. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "Stay with me, and when you recover, I will send you on an expedition (سريّة)." They said: "Take us out of the city." So, He sent them to the camels of al-Sadaqah (charity), where they drank from their urine and ate from their milk. When they recovered and became strong, they killed three of the men who were with the camels and drove the camels away. The news reached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so He sent Ali (peace be upon him) after them. They were in a valley where they had become bewildered, unable to leave it, near the land of Yemen. He captured them and brought them to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then this verse was revealed to Him. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم chose the cutting (القطع), so He cut off their hands and feet from opposite sides.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) that He was asked about this verse, and He said: "That is up to the Imam (الإِمام); He does with him what He wills." It was asked: "Is that delegated (مفوّض) to Him?" He said: "No, but according to the nature of the crime (الجناية)." And in another narration: "It is not that He does whatever He wills, but rather He deals with them according to the extent of their crimes (جناياتهم) of highway robbery (قطع الطريق): If he killed and took the property, his hand and foot are cut off and he is crucified. If he committed highway robbery and killed but did not take the property, he is killed. If he committed highway robbery and took the property but did not kill, his hand and foot are cut off. If he committed highway robbery and neither took property nor killed, he is banished (نفي) from the land." There are other narrations with the same meaning.

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): That which is close to it (the previous ruling), and He was asked: "How is he banished (ينفى) and what is the limit (حدّ) of his banishment?" He said: "He is banished from the city (المصر) where he committed the act to another city (مصر) other than it. A letter is written to the people of that city stating that he is banished (منفيّ), so do not sit with him, nor trade with him, nor intermarry with him, nor eat with him, nor drink with him. This is done to him for one year. If he leaves that city for another, a similar letter is written to them until the year is completed." And in another narration: "He will repent before that, while he is humiliated (صاغر)." It was asked: "What if he heads toward the land of the people of polytheism (أهل الشرك) to enter it?" He said: "If he heads toward the land of polytheism (أرض الشرك) to enter it, its people are fought."

I say: Their people are only fought if they intend to annex him to themselves and refuse to hand him over to the Muslims so that they may kill him. This is the meaning of His saying: "its people are fought."

And in another narration by al-Ayyashi: "His neck is struck," He said, "if he intends to enter the land of polytheism (أرض الشرك)." And in a narration by him (al-Ayyashi) from al-Jawad (peace be upon him) concerning a group who committed highway robbery (قطعوا الطريق): He said: "If they only intimidated the path (أخافوا السبيل) and did not kill anyone and did not take property, He orders them to be placed in prison (الحبس), for that is the meaning of their banishment (نفيهم) from the land."

And in a narration in al-Kafi: The meaning of the banishment (نفي) of the warrior (المحارب) is that he is cast into the sea, so that it may be equivalent (عدلاً) to killing and crucifixion.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever carries a weapon at night is a warrior (محارب), unless he is a man who is not among the people of suspicion (أهل الرّيبة).

ذلِكَ لَهُمْ خِزْيٌ فِي الدُّنْيا ** That is a disgrace for them in this world, [meaning] humiliation (ذلّ) and scandal (فضيحة). وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ ** and there is a great punishment for them in the Hereafter, due to the magnitude of their sins (ذنوبهم).

footnote (١) *حاميت عنه محاماة* and *حماءً* means I prevented (*منعت*) it from him.

(٣٤) لَّا الَّذِينَ تابُوا مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَقْدِرُوا عَلَيْهِمْ فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (34) except those who repent before you seize them. Know that Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. It was said that the exception is specific to what constitutes the right of Allah. As for killing as retribution (قصاصاً), then it belongs to the guardians (الأولياء). Its obligation (وجوبه) is dropped by repentance, but not its permissibility (جوازه). And repentance after his capture only drops the punishment (العذاب) but not the prescribed penalty (الحدّ), unless it is concerning polytheism (الشرك).

(35) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَابْتَغُوا إِلَيْهِ الْوَسِيلَةَ ** (O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and seek the means of recourse to Him) That by which you seek recourse to Him to attain His reward and proximity from Him, such as performing acts of obedience and abandoning sins, after knowing the Imam (الإِمام) and following him. This is derived from (وَسَّلَ إِلَى كَذَا) when one seeks proximity to something. Al-Qummi said: Seek proximity to Him through the Imam (peace be upon him). And in al-Uyun, narrated from the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: The Imams (الأئمّة) from the progeny of Al-Husayn (peace be upon them)—whoever obeys them has obeyed Allah, and whoever disobeys them has disobeyed Allah. They are the firmest handle (العروة الوثقى) and the means of recourse (الوسيلة) to Allah. And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the sermon of al-Wasila (الوسيلة): It is the highest degree in Paradise. Then He described it with an extensive discourse; whoever desires it should refer back to it. وَجاهِدُوا فِي سَبِيلِهِ ** (and wage jihad in His way) The way of Allah, by fighting His apparent and hidden enemies, لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (so that you may be felicitous) by reaching Allah and attaining victory through His generosity.

(٣٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ أَنَّ لَهُمْ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (36) Indeed, those who are faithless—if they had all that is in the earth, of various kinds of wealth, جَمِيعاً وَمِثْلَهُ مَعَهُ لِيَفْتَدُوا بِهِ ** and as much more besides, so that they might ransom themselves with it so that they might make it a ransom for their souls from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection, it would not be accepted from them. This is a representation (تمثيل) of the permanence of the punishment for them, and that there is no way for them to escape from it. وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. and there is a painful punishment for them.

(٣٧) يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا مِنَ النَّارِ وَما هُمْ بِخارِجِينَ مِنْها وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ مُقِيمٌ ** (37) They desire to come out of the Fire, but they will not come out of it, and there is a lasting punishment for them. Al-Ayyashi narrated from them both (peace be upon them) that they are the enemies of Ali (peace be upon him).

(٣٨) وَالسَّارِقُ وَالسَّارِقَةُ فَاقْطَعُوا أَيْدِيَهُما ** (38) As for the man and the woman who steal, cut off their hands Al-Sariqah (السرقة) is taking the property of another in secret. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked regarding the amount for which the thief (السَّارق) is to be cut. He said: "For a quarter of a dinar (دينار)." It was said: "For two dirhams (درهمين)?" He said: "For a quarter of a dinar (دينار), whatever the dinar (الدِّينار) may reach." It was said: "What do You say about one who steals less than a quarter of a dinar (دينار)? Does the name of 'thief' (السارق) apply to him when he steals, and is he a thief (سارق) in the sight of Allah in that state?" He replied: "Everyone who steals something from a Muslim that he has possessed and secured, the name of 'thief' (السَّارق) applies to him, and he is a thief (سارق) in the sight of Allah. However, the hand is not cut except for a quarter of a dinar (دينار) or more. If the hands of thieves (السرَّاق) were cut for what is less than a quarter of a dinar (دينار), you would find the majority of people with severed hands." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The cutting is from the middle of the palm (الكفّ), and the thumb (الإِبهام) is not cut. And if the foot (الرّجل) is cut, the heel (العقب) is left and not cut. In a narration: The four fingers (الأربع أصابع) are cut, and the thumb (الإِبهام) is left, upon which he relies during the prayer (الصّلوة) and with which he washes his face for the prayer (الصّلوة). There are other narrations with the same meaning as these two. And al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): When He would cut the hand of the thief (السّارق), He would leave him the thumb (الإِبهام) and the palm (الرّاحة). It was said to Him: "O Commander of the Faithful, You have left most of his hand." He said: "If he repents, with what will he perform ablution (يتوضّأ)? Allah says: فَمَنْ تابَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ظُلْمِهِ وَأَصْلَحَ فَإِنَّ اللهَ يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِ إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (39) But whoever repents after his wrongdoing and reforms, Allah shall surely relent to him. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful." And narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): The cutting must be from the joint of the base of the fingers (أصول الأصابع), so the palm (الكفّ) is left. The proof (الحجّة) for this is the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Prostration (السجود) is upon seven limbs: the face, the two hands, the two knees, and the two feet." If his hand were cut from the wrist (الكوع) (١) or the elbow (المرفق), he would not have a hand left upon which to prostrate. And Allah the Almighty said: وَأَنَّ الْمَساجِدَ لِلَّهِ ** (18) and that the places of worship belong to Allah, meaning by this these seven limbs upon which one prostrates, "so do not invoke anyone along with Allah." And whatever belongs to Allah is not to be cut. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) judged concerning the thief (السارق): If he steals, his right hand (يمينه) is cut. If he steals another time, his left foot (رجله اليسرى) is cut. Then, if he steals yet another time, He imprisons him and leaves his right foot (رجله اليمنى) for him to walk upon to relieve himself (إلى الغائط), and his left hand (يده اليسرى) for him to eat with and to cleanse himself (يستنجي بها) with. He said: "I am ashamed before Allah to leave him unable to benefit from anything, but I will imprison him until he dies in prison." He said: "The Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم did not cut anything from a thief after his hand and his foot." And al-Ayyashi narrated what is close to this, and there are many narrations with the same meaning. جَزاءً بِما كَسَبا نَكالاً مِنَ اللهِ ** (as a requital for what they have earned, a deterrent from Allah.) meaning: a punishment (عقوبة) from Him. وَاللهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ. (And Allah is all-mighty, all-wise.)

(٣٩) فَمَنْ تابَ ** refers to the thieves (السراق) مِنْ بَعْدِ ظُلْمِهِ ** meaning, after his theft وَأَصْلَحَ ** meaning, reforms his affair by returning the property and freeing himself from the consequences (التبعات) فَإِنَّ اللهَ يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِ إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** meaning, He accepts his repentance, so he is not cut off (his hand is not cut) nor is he punished in the Hereafter, unless his repentance occurs after he falls into the hands of the Imam (الإِمام). In that case, the cutting (القطع) is not waived, even if the owner forgives him.

(39) But whoever repents after his wrongdoing and reforms, Allah shall surely relent to him. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

In al-Kafi, narrated from one of the two (Imams) عليهما السلام (peace be upon them both), concerning a man who stole, or drank wine (الخمر), or committed fornication (زنا), and this was not known about him, and he was not apprehended until he repented and reformed. He (the Imam) said: "If he reforms and a beautiful matter (أمر جميل) is known from him, the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) shall not be carried out against him."

And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام (peace be upon him): "Whoever apprehends a thief and forgives him, that is his right. But if he is referred to the Imam (الإِمام), He (the Imam) shall cut him (his hand). If the one from whom the theft occurred says, 'I grant it (the property) to him,' the Imam shall not leave him until He cuts him (his hand) once the matter has been referred to the Imam. The granting (الهبة) is only valid before the matter is referred to the Imam. That is the meaning of Allah's saying: وَالْحافِظُونَ لِحُدُودِ اللهِ ** (and those who maintain Allah’s bounds). When the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) reaches the Imam, it is not for anyone to abandon it."

And from Him عليه السلام (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who apprehends a thief (الّلصّ): should he refer him (to the authority) or leave him? He said: "Indeed, Safwan ibn Umayyah (صفوان بن أميّة) was reclining in the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام), and he placed his cloak (رداءه). It was stolen. When he returned to it, he said, 'Who took my cloak?' He went seeking it and apprehended the culprit, whom he referred to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). He (the Prophet) said: 'Cut off his hand.' Safwan said: 'His hand is cut off because of my cloak, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'Yes.' Safwan said: 'Then I grant it (the cloak) to him.' The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'Why was this not done before you referred him to Me?'" It was asked: "Is the Imam (الإِمام) in the same position when the matter is referred to him?" He said: "Yes."

footnote (١) The *الكوع* (with a *ḍamma* on the *kāf*) is the end of the forearm bone (*الزّند*) that is next to the thumb (*الإِبهام*). The plural is *أكواع*. According to al-Azhari, the *الكوع* is the end of the forearm bone (*الزّند*) that is next to the wrist (*رسغ اليد*), adjacent to the thumb (*الإِبهام*). They are two bones joined together in the forearm (*السّاعد*), one of them being thinner than the other. Their ends meet at the joint of the palm (*مفصل الكف*). The one next to the little finger (*الخنصر*) is called the *الكرسوع*, and the one next to the thumb (*الإِبهام*) is called the *الكوع*. They are the two forearm bones (*عظما ساعدي الذّراع*).

(٤٠) أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللهَ لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. (40) Do you not know that to Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth? He punishes whomever He wishes, and forgives whomever He wishes, and Allah is capable of all things. (٤١) يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ لا يَحْزُنْكَ الَّذِينَ يُسارِعُونَ فِي الْكُفْرِ في إظهاره إذا وجدوا منه فرصة مِنَ الَّذِينَ قالُوا آمَنَّا بِأَفْواهِهِمْ وَلَمْ تُؤْمِنْ قُلُوبُهُمْ يعني المنافقين وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ هادُوا سَمَّاعُونَ لِلْكَذِبِ (١) قائلون له أو سماَّعون كلامك ليكذبوا عليك سَمَّاعُونَ لِقَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ لَمْ يَأْتُوكَ أي لجمع آخر من اليهود لم يحضروا مجلسك وتجافوا عنك تكبّراً وافراطاً في البغضاءِ يعني مصغون لهم قائلون كلامهم أو سماَّعون منك لأجلهم وللإِنهاءِ إليهم يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَواضِعِهِ يميلونه عن مواضعه التي وضعه الله فيها بتغييره وحمله على غير المراد واجرائه في غير مورده أو إهماله يَقُولُونَ إِنْ أُوتِيتُمْ هذا فَخُذُوهُ ان أوتيتم هذا المحرف فاقبلوه واعملوا به وَإِنْ لَمْ تُؤْتَوْهُ بل أفتاكم محمّد صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم بخلافه فَاحْذَرُوا قبول ما أفتاكم به قيل كان سبب نزول هذه الآية ما مر في تفسير قَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ كَثِيراً مِمَّا كُنْتُمْ تُخْفُونَ من هذه السورة من قصة ابن صوريا ومحاكمته بين نبيّنا صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم واليهود. (41) O Apostle! Do not grieve for those who are active in disbelief (in manifesting it when they find an opportunity for it)—among those who say with their mouths, ‘We believe,’ but their hearts do not believe (meaning the hypocrites (المنافقين)). And among the Jews are those who listen closely to falsehood (لِلْكَذِبِ) (1), saying it, or listening closely to your speech so that they may lie about you. سَمَّاعُونَ لِقَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ لَمْ يَأْتُوكَ ** (They listen closely for the sake of another group who have not come to you) (meaning, for another group of Jews who did not attend your assembly and kept away from you out of arrogance and excessive hatred; meaning, they are attentive to them, repeating their words, or they listen closely to you for their sake and to convey [the information] to them). يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَواضِعِهِ ** (They distort the words from their contexts) (They incline it away from the contexts in which Allah placed it, by changing it, interpreting it contrary to the intended meaning, applying it in an inappropriate situation, or neglecting it). يَقُولُونَ إِنْ أُوتِيتُمْ هذا فَخُذُوهُ ** (They say: ‘If you are given this, take it’) (If you are given this distorted [ruling], accept it and act upon it). وَإِنْ لَمْ تُؤْتَوْهُ ** (But if you are not given it) (rather, Muhammad صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم gives you a ruling contrary to it), فَاحْذَرُوا ** (‘then beware’) (of accepting what He has ruled for you). It was said that the reason for the revelation of this Ayah was what passed in the exegesis of قَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ كَثِيراً مِمَّا كُنْتُمْ تُخْفُونَ ** (Our Apostle has certainly come to you, clarifying for you much of what you used to conceal) from this Surah, concerning the story of Ibn Suriya and his arbitration between our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Jews.

والقمّيّ : كان سبب نزولها إنّه كان في المدينة بطنان من اليهود من بني هرون وهم النّضير وقريظة وكانت قريظة سبعمائة والنّضير ألفاً وكانت النّضير أكثر مالاً وأحسن حالاً من قريظة وكانوا حُلَفاء لعبد الله بن أبيّ فكان إذا وقع بين قريظة والنّضير قتل وكان القتيل من بني النّضير قالوا لبني قريظة لا نرضى أن يكون قتيل منّا بقتيل منكم فجرى بينهم في ذلك مخاطبات كثيرة حتّى كادوا أن يقتلوا حتّى رضيت قريظة وكتبوا بينهم كتاباً على أنّه أيّ رجل من اليهود من النّضير قتل رجلاً من بني قريظة أن يحنّب (١) ويحمّم والتحنيب أن يقعد على جمل ويولى وجهه الى ذنب الجمل ويلطخ وجهه بالحمأة (٢) ويدفع نصف الدية وأيّما رجل قتل رجلاً من النّضير أن يدفع إليه الدّية كاملة ويقتل به فلمّا هاجر رسول الله صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم ودخل الأوس والخزرج في الإِسلام ضعف أمر اليهود فقتل رجل من بني قريظة رجلاً من بني النّضير فبعثوا إليهم بني النّضير ابعثوا إلينا بدية المقتول وبالقاتل حتّى نقتله فقالت قريظة ليس هذا حكم التوراة وانّما هو شيء غلبتمونا عليه فاما الدّية وإِمّا القتل والّا فهذا محمّد صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم بيننا وبينكم فهلموا نتحاكم إليه فمشت بنو النّضير الى عبد الله من أبي فقالوا سل محمّداً أن لا ينقض شرطنا في هذا الحكم الذي بيننا وبين قريظة في القتل فقال عبد الله بن أُبيّ ابعثوا رجلاً يسمع كلامي وكلامه فان حكم لكم بما تريدون والا فلا ترضوا به فبعثوا معه رجلا فجاء إلى رسول الله صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم فقال يا رسول الله إنّ هؤلاءِ القوم قريظة والنّضير قد كتبوا بينهم كتاباً وعهداً وثيقاً تراضوا به والآن في قدومك يريدون نقضه وقد رضوا بحكمك فيهم فلا تنقض كتابهم وشرطهم فانّ النّضير لهم القوّة والسّلاح والكراع (١) ونحن نخاف الدّوائر فاغتم رسول الله صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم من ذلك ولم يجبه بشيء فنزل عليه جبرئيل بهذه الآيات قال يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَواضِعِهِ يعني عبد الله بن ابيّ وبني النّضير وَإِنْ لَمْ تُؤْتَوْهُ فَاحْذَرُوا يعني عبد الله قال لبني النّضير لم يحكم بما تريدونه فلا تقبلوا وَمَنْ يُرِدِ اللهُ فِتْنَتَهُ اختباره ليفضح فَلَنْ تَمْلِكَ لَهُ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً فلن تستطيع له من الله شيئاً في دفعها أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يُرِدِ اللهُ أَنْ يُطَهِّرَ قُلُوبَهُمْ من العقوبات المرتّبة على الكفر كالختم والطّبع والضّيق لَهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيا خِزْيٌ هوان بالزام الجزية على اليهود واجلاءِ بني النّضير منهم وإظهار كذبهم في كتمان الحقّ وظهور كفر المنافقين وخوفهم جميعاً من المؤمنين وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ وهو الخلود في النار.

And al-Qummi [said]: The reason for its revelation was that there were two clans of Jews in Medina from the Children of Aaron (بني هرون): Banu al-Nadir (النّضير) and Banu Qurayzah (قريظة). Qurayzah numbered seven hundred, and al-Nadir a thousand. Al-Nadir were wealthier and in better condition than Qurayzah, and they were allies of Abdullah ibn Ubayy. If a killing occurred between Qurayzah and al-Nadir, and the slain person was from Banu al-Nadir, they would say to Banu Qurayzah: "We are not satisfied that a slain person from us should be equal to a slain person from you." Many discussions took place between them regarding this, until they almost fought, until Qurayzah agreed. They wrote a document between them stating that if any man from the Jews of al-Nadir killed a man from Banu Qurayzah, he should be yuḥannab (يُحَنَّب) (1) and yuḥammam (يُحَمَّم). Al-Taḥnīb (التحنيب) is that he is seated upon a camel, his face turned toward the camel's tail, and his face is smeared with mud (الحمأة) (2), and he pays half the blood-money (الدية). But if any man killed a man from al-Nadir, he should pay the full blood-money and be killed in retaliation. When the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم emigrated and the Aws (الأوس) and Khazraj (الخزرج) entered Islam, the power of the Jews weakened. Then a man from Banu Qurayzah killed a man from Banu al-Nadir. Banu al-Nadir sent to them [saying]: "Send us the blood-money of the slain person and the killer so that we may execute him." Qurayzah said: "This is not the ruling of the Torah (التوراة); rather, it is something you imposed upon us by force. So, either the blood-money or the execution. Otherwise, Muhammad صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم is between us and you, so let us go and seek judgment from Him." Banu al-Nadir went to Abdullah ibn Ubayy and said: "Ask Muhammad not to annul our condition regarding this ruling concerning killing that is between us and Qurayzah." Abdullah ibn Ubayy said: "Send a man who will listen to my words and His words. If He rules for you what you desire, [accept it], otherwise, do not be satisfied with it." So they sent a man with him. He came to the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, these people, Qurayzah and al-Nadir, have written a document and a firm covenant between them with which they were satisfied. Now, with your arrival, they wish to annul it. They have agreed to your judgment concerning them, so do not annul their document and their condition, for al-Nadir possess strength, weapons, and mounts (الكراع) (1), and we fear misfortunes (الدّوائر)." The Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم was distressed by this and did not answer him with anything. Then Gabriel descended upon Him with these Ayahs. He said: يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَواضِعِهِ ** (They distort the words from their contexts) means Abdullah ibn Ubayy and Banu al-Nadir. وَإِنْ لَمْ تُؤْتَوْهُ فَاحْذَرُوا ** (But if you are not given it, then beware) means Abdullah said to Banu al-Nadir: "If He does not rule according to what you desire, do not accept it." وَمَنْ يُرِدِ اللهُ فِتْنَتَهُ ** (And whomever Allah wishes to test) (His testing him in order to expose him) فَلَنْ تَمْلِكَ لَهُ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً ** (you cannot avail him anything against Allah). (You will not be able to do anything for him against Allah in repelling it.) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يُرِدِ اللهُ أَنْ يُطَهِّرَ قُلُوبَهُمْ ** (They are the ones whose hearts Allah has not willed to purify) from the punishments resulting from disbelief, such as sealing (الختم), stamping (الطّبع), and constriction (الضّيق). لَهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيا خِزْيٌ ** (For them is disgrace in this world) (humiliation by imposing the Jizyah (الجزية) upon the Jews, the expulsion of Banu al-Nadir from among them, the exposure of their lies in concealing the truth, the manifestation of the hypocrites' disbelief, and the fear of all of them from the believers) **وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ ** (and for them there is a great punishment in the Hereafter) (which is eternal dwelling in the Fire).

footnote (١) على تضمين السّماع معنى القبُول حتّى يصحّ استعماله مع اللام.

footnote (١) حنّب تحنيباً نكّس.

footnote (٢) الحمأة الطّين الأسود المنتن.

(٤٢) سَمَّاعُونَ لِلْكَذِبِ ** (42) [They are] listeners to falsehood] He repeated it for emphasis. أَكَّالُونَ لِلسُّحْتِ ** [eaters of the unlawful gain (السُّحْتِ)] meaning the forbidden (الحرام), derived from سَحَتَهُ (he uprooted it/destroyed it) when he eradicated it, because its blessing (البركة) is destroyed (مسحوت). And it was recited with two ḍammahs (i.e., السُّحُت).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about السُّحْتَ ** [the unlawful gain (السُّحْتَ)], and He said: "It is bribery (الرّشا) in judgment."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): السُّحْتَ ** [The unlawful gain (السُّحْتَ)] is the price of carrion (الميتَة), the price of a dog, the price of wine (الخمر), the dowry of a prostitute (مهر البغي), bribery (الرّشوة), and the wage of a soothsayer (أجر الكاهن). And in one narration: the price of a dog that does not hunt.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Everything that is embezzled (غلّ) from the Imam (الإِمام) is سحت (unlawful gain), and consuming the property of the orphan (مال اليتيم) and its like is سحت. And السُّحْتَ (unlawful gain) has many types, among them are the wages of immoral women (أجور الفواجر), the price of wine (الخمر) and intoxicating date-wine (النّبيذ المسكر), and usury (الرّبا) after clear evidence. As for bribery (الرّشا) in judgment, that is disbelief (الكفر) in Allah, the Magnificent, and His Messenger.

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a judge between two parties who takes sustenance (الرّزق) from the ruler (السّلطان) for judging. He said: "That is السُّحْتَ ** [the unlawful gain (السُّحْتَ)]."

And in al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: أَكَّالُونَ لِلسُّحْتِ ** [eaters of the unlawful gain (السُّحْتِ)]. He said: "It is the man who fulfills a need for his brother, and then accepts his gift (هديّته)."

And al-Qummi said: السُّحْتَ ** [The unlawful gain (السُّحْتَ)] is between the lawful (الحلال) and the forbidden (الحرام). It is when a man hires himself out to carry intoxicants (المسكر) or pork (لحم الخنزير) or to set up musical instruments (واتّخاذ الملاهي). His hiring himself out is lawful (حلال), but from the perspective of what he carries and does, it is سحت (unlawful gain).

فَإِنْ جاؤُكَ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ أَوْ أَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ ** (42) So if they come to you, judge between them, or disregard them. This is a choice (تخيير) for Him. In al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): If the people of the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإِنجيل) come to the ruler (الحاكم) seeking judgment from him, the matter is up to him: if He wills, He judges between them, and if He wills, He leaves them. وَإِنْ تُعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ فَلَنْ يَضُرُّوكَ شَيْئاً (and if you disregard them, they will not harm you in any way) by showing enmity toward you because of your disregard for them, for Allah will protect you (يعصمك) from the people. وَإِنْ حَكَمْتَ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْقِسْطِ ** (But if you judge, judge between them with justice (بِالْقِسْطِ))—with the justice (العدل) that Allah has commanded. إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُقْسِطِينَ (Indeed Allah loves the just ones.)

footnote (١) *Al-Kura'* (*الكراع*) is a name specifically for a group of horses. The Almighty's saying: **أَنْ تُصِيبَنا دائِرَةٌ ** (that a turn of fortune may befall us) means one of the turns (*دوائر*) of time (*الزّمان*), meaning its vicissitudes (*صروفه*) which revolve and encompass man, sometimes with good and sometimes with evil.

(٤٣) **وَكَيْفَ يُحَكِّمُونَكَ وَعِنْدَهُمُ التَّوْراةُ فِيها حُكْمُ اللهِ ** (43) How will they make you the judge, while they have the Torah containing Allah’s judgment?

Astonishment at their making him (the Prophet) the judge, whom they do not believe in, while the situation is that the judgment is explicitly stated (منصوص) in the Book which they possess. And in this is an alert (تنبيه) that they did not intend by seeking judgment (التحكيم) the knowledge of the truth (معرفة الحقّ) and the establishment of the Law (إقامة الشّرع), but rather they sought through it what would be easier (أهون) for them, even if it was not the judgment of Allah in their claim (زعمهم).

**ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّوْنَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ ** (43) Then they turn away after that.

Then they turn away (يعرضون) from your judgment, which conforms to their Book, after seeking judgment (التحكيم).

**وَما أُولئِكَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (43) And these are not believers.

In their Book, because of their turning away (إِعراضهم) from it first, and from what conforms to it second.

(44) إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنَا التَّوْراةَ فِيها هُدىً وَنُورٌ يَحْكُمُ بِهَا النَّبِيُّونَ الَّذِينَ أَسْلَمُوا لِلَّذِينَ هادُوا وَالرَّبَّانِيُّونَ وَالْأَحْبارُ بِمَا اسْتُحْفِظُوا مِنْ كِتابِ اللهِ وَكانُوا عَلَيْهِ شُهَداءَ ** (Indeed, We sent down the Torah, in which was guidance and light. The prophets who submitted [to Allah] judged by it for the Jews, and so did the masters and the rabbis, because they were entrusted with preserving the Book of Allah, and were witnesses to it.) a clarification of the truth, and light which reveals what is obscure concerning the rulings. The prophets who submitted [to Allah]—they submitted to Allah. It is said that He described them with al-Islam because it is the religion of Allah—judged by it for the Jews—they judge for them—and the masters and the rabbis. And the masters and the rabbis judge by it, because they were entrusted with preserving the Book of Allah, and were witnesses to it. (1)

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace and blessings be upon him): الرَّبَّانِيُّونَ ** (the masters) are the Imams, distinct from the Prophets, who nurture (يَرُبُّونَ) the people with their knowledge. وَالْأَحْبارُ ** (and the rabbis) are the scholars, distinct from the masters (الربانيين). He said: Then He informed about them, saying: بِمَا اسْتُحْفِظُوا مِنْ كِتابِ اللهِ وَكانُوا عَلَيْهِ شُهَداءَ ** (because they were entrusted with preserving the Book of Allah, and were witnesses to it), and He did not say: "because they carried (حَمَلُوا) from it."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This verse was revealed concerning us. فَلا تَخْشَوُا النَّاسَ وَاخْشَوْنِ ** (So do not fear the people, but fear Me). It is said that this is a prohibition for judges against fearing anyone other than Allah in their judgments and being lenient therein. وَلا تَشْتَرُوا بِآياتِي ** (and do not sell My signs)—meaning: do not exchange My rulings which I have revealed—ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً ** (for a paltry gain)—such as a bribe or status—وَمَنْ لَمْ يَحْكُمْ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْكافِرُونَ.** (And whoever does not judge by what Allah has sent down—it is they who are the faithless.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Household): Whoever judges unjustly concerning two dirhams, and then enforces that judgment, is among the people referred to in this verse.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) and Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever judges concerning two dirhams by other than what Allah has revealed, among those who possess a whip (سَوْط) or a staff (عَصاً), he is faithless (كافر) in what Allah revealed to Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Household).

(1) Meaning: كانُوا ** (they were) witnesses to the ruling of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Household) concerning stoning (الرّجم), that it was established in the Torah. شُهَداءَ ** (witnesses). This is according to Ibn Abbas. And it is said: كانُوا عَلَيْهِ شُهَداءَ ** (and were witnesses to it) concerning the Book, that it is from Allah. (عطاء)

(٤٥) وَكَتَبْنا عَلَيْهِمْ And We made obligatory upon the Jews فِيها in the Torah: أَنَّ النَّفْسَ بِالنَّفْسِ (١) meaning, it is killed by it وَالْعَيْنَ بِالْعَيْنِ (٢) meaning, it is gouged out by it وَالْأَنْفَ بِالْأَنْفِ meaning, it is mutilated by it وَالْأُذُنَ بِالْأُذُنِ (٣) meaning, it is severed by it وَالسِّنَّ بِالسِّنِ meaning, it is extracted by it وَالْجُرُوحَ قِصاصٌ meaning, possessing retaliation (قصاص). And it was recited with raf' (nominative case) in the five (body parts) and with the lightening (تخفيف) of al-udhun (الأذن).

القمّيّ (Al-Qummi) said: It is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْقِصاصُ فِي الْقَتْلى الْحُرُّ بِالْحُرِّ وَالْعَبْدُ بِالْعَبْدِ وَالْأُنْثى بِالْأُنْثى (Prescribed for you is retaliation concerning the slain: the free for the free, the slave for the slave, and the female for the female). And His saying **الْجُرُوحَ قِصاصٌ ** (retaliation for [all] injuries) is not abrogated.

فَمَنْ تَصَدَّقَ بِهِ meaning, with the retaliation (القصاص), that is, he pardoned it فَهُوَ كَفَّارَةٌ لَهُ. (45) But whoever remits it as a charity, it will be an atonement for him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It atones for his sins to the extent of what he pardoned, whether it be injuries (جراح) or otherwise. And in al-Faqih, there is a similar narration, except that it states: what he pardoned concerning intentional (العمد) acts.

وَمَنْ لَمْ يَحْكُمْ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ including retaliation (القصاص) and other matters فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ (45) Those who do not judge by what Allah has sent down—it is they who are the wrongdoers.

(٤٦) وَقَفَّيْنا عَلى آثارِهِمْ ** (46) And We sent after them واتبعنا على آثار النبيين الّذين أسلموا بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ And We followed in the footsteps of the prophets who submitted (أسلموا) with بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ Jesus son of Mary, مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ التَّوْراةِ وَآتَيْناهُ الْإِنْجِيلَ فِيهِ هُدىً وَنُورٌ وَمُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ التَّوْراةِ وَهُدىً وَمَوْعِظَةً لِلْمُتَّقِينَ ** confirming what was before him of the Torah, and We gave him the Evangel, in which was guidance and light, confirming what was before him of the Torah, and a guidance and an admonition for the Godwary. إنّما خصّهم بالذكر مع عموم الموعظة لأنّهم اختصوا بالانتفاع به. He only specified them (the Godwary - المتقين) in mention, despite the generality of the admonition (الموعظة), because they are specifically distinguished by benefiting from it.

footnote (١) معناه إذا قتلت نفس نفساً اخرى عمداً فانّه يستحق عليه العَوَد إذا كان القاتل عاقلاً مميّزاً أو كان المقتول مكافئاً للقاتل. (1) Its meaning is that if a soul kills another soul intentionally, then the return (العَوَد) is deserved against him, provided that the killer is sane (عاقلاً) and discerning (مميزاً), or that the slain person is equal (مكافئاً) to the killer.

footnote (٢) قال العلماء كلّ شخصين جرى القصاص بينهما في العين والأنف والأُذن والسّن وجميع الأطراف إذا تماثلا في السّلامة والشّلل وإذا امتنع القصاص في النفس امتنع أيضاً في الأطراف. (2) The scholars (العلماء) said: Qisas (القصاص) applies between any two persons regarding the eye, the nose, the ear, the tooth, and all limbs, provided they are similar (تماثلا) in soundness (السّلامة) and paralysis (الشّلل). If Qisas is prevented regarding the soul (النفس), it is also prevented regarding the limbs (الأطراف).

footnote (٣) الاصطلام الاستيصال وهو افتعال من الصّلم وهو القطع المستأصل. (3) Al-Istilam (الاصطلام) is eradication (الاستيصال), and it is derived from the form ifti'āl (افتعال) from al-Ṣalm (الصّلم), which is the cutting that eradicates (القطع المستأصل).

(٤٧) وَلْيَحْكُمْ ** And it was read with the kasrah on the lām and the fatḥah on the mīm. أَهْلُ الْإِنْجِيلِ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ فِيهِ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَحْكُمْ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ **(٤٧) (Let the People of the Evangel judge by what Allah has sent down in it. And whoever does not judge by what Allah has sent down—it is they who are the transgressors.)

(٤٨) وَأَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِ ** meaning the Quran مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** meaning from the category (من جنس) of the revealed Books (الكتُب المنزلة). وَمُهَيْمِناً عَلَيْهِ ** and a guardian (رقيباً) over the rest of the Books, protecting it from alteration (التغيير) and testifying to its soundness (بالصحة) and permanence (والثبات). فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ ** meaning what He sent down to you وَلا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْواءَهُمْ عَمَّا جاءَكَ مِنَ الْحَقِ ** by deviating (بالانحراف) from it to what they desire (ما يشتهونه). لِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنا مِنْكُمْ ** O people (أيّها النَّاس) شِرْعَةً ** meaning a divine law (شريعةً), and it is the path (الطريقة) to water (الماء); the religion (الدين) was likened to it because it is a path to that which is the cause (سبب) of eternal life (الحياة الأبدية). وَمِنْهاجاً ** and a clear path (وطريقاً واضحاً), derived from نَهَج الأمر (the matter became clear) when it was made manifest (إذا أوضح).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a ḥadīth: So when every prophet was answered by those of his people who believed, We appointed for each of them شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهاجاً . And the شِرْعَة and the مِنْهاج are a way (سبيل) and a practice (سنّة). And every prophet was commanded to adopt the way (السّبيل) and the practice (السنّة). And among the way (السّبيل) and the practice (السنّة) which Allah commanded Moses was that He appointed the Sabbath (السّبت) upon them.

وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** meaning a community (جماعة) united (متفقة) upon a single religion (عَلَى دين واحد). وَلكِنْ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِي ما آتاكُمْ ** meaning regarding the differing divine laws (من الشرائع المختلفة) appropriate for every age and generation (عصر وقرن). Do you act upon them, affirming the existence of wisdom (الحكمة) in their difference? فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْراتِ ** So hasten to them (فابتدروها), seizing (انتهازاً) the opportunity (للفرصة) and acquiring (وحيازةً) the prize of precedence (قصب السّبق) and advancement (والتقدّم). إِلَى اللهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعاً ** This is a promise and a threat (وعد ووعيد) for those who hasten (للمبادرين) and those who fall short (والمقصرين). فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ ** by means of the decisive recompense (بالجزاءِ الفاصل) between the truthful (المحق) and the false (المبطل), and the hastener (المبادر) and the one who falls short (والمقصر).

وَأَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِ مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ الْكِتابِ وَمُهَيْمِناً عَلَيْهِ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ وَلا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْواءَهُمْ عَمَّا جاءَكَ مِنَ الْحَقِ لِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنا مِنْكُمْ شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهاجاً وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً وَلكِنْ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِي ما آتاكُمْ فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْراتِ إِلَى اللهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعاً فَيُنَبِّئْكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (٤٨) (And We have sent down to you the Book with the truth, confirming what was before it of the Book, and sovereign over it. So judge between them by what Allah has sent down, and do not follow their desires, deviating from the truth that has come to you. For each of you We have appointed a divine law and a manifest way. Had Allah wished, He would have made you one nation, but that He may test you in respect to what He has given you. So compete with one another in good deeds. To Allah shall be your return, all together, whereat He will inform you concerning that about which you used to differ.)

(٤٩) وَأَنِ احْكُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ ** (49) and judge between them by what Allah has sent down It was said that it is conjoined (عطف) to الْكِتابَ ** (the Book), meaning: We sent down to you the Book and the judgment (الحكم), or conjoined to الْحَقِ ** (the truth), meaning: We sent it down with the truth and with the command that I judge (بِأَنْ أَحْكُم). It is also permissible for it to be a new sentence (الاستيناف) with the implied meaning: And We commanded that I judge. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The command to judge between them was only repeated because they were two judgments that He commanded entirely, since they sought his judgment regarding a killing that had occurred between them. وَلا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْواءَهُمْ وَاحْذَرْهُمْ أَنْ يَفْتِنُوكَ ** and do not follow their desires, and beware of them lest they should tempt you away meaning: lest they mislead you and turn you away عَنْ بَعْضِ ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ إِلَيْكَ (from some of what Allah has sent down to you). فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا (Then if they turn away) from the revealed judgment and desire something else, فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ أَنْ يُصِيبَهُمْ بِبَعْضِ ذُنُوبِهِمْ (know that Allah desires to inflict them with some of their sins). In this, there is an alert that they have many sins, and turning away from the judgment of Allah, despite its greatness, is one of them, counted among their totality. وَإِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنَ النَّاسِ لَفاسِقُونَ ** and indeed many of the people are transgressors. This is a consolation for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding the refusal of the people to acknowledge his prophethood and hasten to respond to his call, [by informing him] that the people of faith are few and the people of transgression (الفسق) are many, so this should not weigh heavily upon you.

(٥٠) أَفَحُكْمَ الْجاهِلِيَّةِ يَبْغُونَ ** (Do they seek the judgment of [the Age of] Ignorance?) This is a denunciation of their turning away from the judgment of Allah. And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتّاءِ). وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ مِنَ اللهِ حُكْماً لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (And who is better than Allah in judgment for a people who are certain?) Meaning, this interrogation is for a people who are certain (يوقنون), for they are the ones who reflect upon matters (الأمور) and ascertain things (الأشياء) through their insights (بأنظارهم), thus knowing that there is no judgment better than the judgment of Allah.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq, from the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both): Judgment (الحكم) is two types of judgment: the judgment of Allah and the judgment of al-Jahiliyyah (الجاهلية). So, whoever errs in the judgment of Allah judges according to the judgment of al-Jahiliyyah. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said: وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ مِنَ اللهِ حُكْماً لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (And who is better than Allah in judgment for a people who are certain?). And I bear witness against Zayd ibn Thabit that he certainly judged concerning the obligatory shares (الفرائض) according to the judgment of al-Jahiliyyah.

(٥١) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَّخِذُوا الْيَهُودَ وَالنَّصارى أَوْلِياءَ (51) O you who have faith! Do not take the Jews and the Christians as allies.

Do not rely upon gaining victory through them, showing affection towards them, nor associate with them with the association of beloved friends. بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِياءُ بَعْضٍ (They are) allies of one another. in aid and support, and their hand is united against you, and they are those who are agreed upon opposing you. وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ And whoever of you takes them as allies, he is indeed one of them. Whoever seeks victory through them is a disbeliever (كافر) like them.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever takes as allies (تولىَّ) the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and prefers them over all people, due to the precedence granted to them by their kinship to the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, then he is from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) in the rank (بمنزلة) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all. Not that he is one of the people (القوم) themselves (بأعيانهم), but rather he is one of them through his alliance (توليه) to them and his following of them. And likewise is the ruling of Allah in His Book: وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ (And whoever of you takes them as allies, he is indeed one of them)."

And the saying of Abraham: فَمَنْ تَبِعَنِي فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ (36) So whoever follows me, he is indeed one of me, (51) Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. those who wronged themselves and the believers by taking the disbelievers (الكفّار) as allies (بموالاة).

(٥٢) فَتَرَى الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ ** (Then you see those in whose hearts is a sickness) like Ibn Ubayy and his likes (أضرابه) يُسارِعُونَ فِيهِمْ ** (hastening towards them,) by befriending them (بموالاتهم) and assisting them (ومعاونتهم) يَقُولُونَ نَخْشى أَنْ تُصِيبَنا دائِرَةٌ ** (saying, 'We fear that a turn of fortune may befall us.') They apologize by saying that they fear that a turn (دائرة) from the turns of fortune (الدّوائر) may befall them, meaning that the matter might turn around and the dominance (الدولة) might belong to the disbelievers. It is narrated that 'Ubadah ibn al-Samit said to the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, I have many allies (موالي) among the Jews in number, and I disavow (أبرء) before Allah and His Messenger their alliance (ولايتهم), and I ally myself with Allah and His Messenger." Then Ibn Ubayy said: "I am a man who fears the turns of fortune (الدواير); I do not disavow the alliance (ولاية) of my allies (مواليّ)." So, [the following Ayah] was revealed: فَعَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ بِالْفَتْحِ ** (Maybe Allah will bring about the victory) for His Messenger أَوْ أَمْرٍ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ ** (or some command from Him,) in which there is the honoring (إعزاز) of the believers, the humiliation (إذلال) of the polytheists, and the triumph (ظهور) of Islam فَيُصْبِحُوا ** (whereupon they will become) meaning these hypocrites عَلى ما أَسَرُّوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (regretful for what they kept secret in their hearts.) of hypocrisy and doubt concerning the affair of the Messenger, regretful (نادِمِينَ).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding the interpretation (تأويل) of this Ayah, He (peace be upon him) permitted (١) the destruction (هلاك) of Banu Umayyah seven days after the burning of Zayd.

(٥٣) وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (And those who believe will say) some of them to others, or to the Jews. And it was read without the waw of conjunction (واو العطف) and in the accusative case (بالنّصب), as an object (عطفاً) of يأتي (referring to the preceding verb). أَهؤُلاءِ الَّذِينَ أَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ إِنَّهُمْ لَمَعَكُمْ ** (Are these the ones who swore by Allah their strongest oaths that they are indeed with you?) in astonishment at the state of the hypocrites and boasting (2) about the sincerity (الإِخلاص) with which Allah favored them. And جهد الأيمان (the strongest oaths) means the most severe (أغلظها) of them. حَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا خاسِرِينَ (Their works have failed, and they have become losers) Either from the perspective of what is said [by the believers] or from the saying of Allah, as a testimony for them [the believers]. And in it is the meaning of astonishment, as if it were said: How futile are their works! How great is their loss!

(٥٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَنْ يَرْتَدَّ مِنْكُمْ عَنْ دِينِهِ ** (54) O you who have faith! Should any of you revert from his religion, It was read (يَرْتَدِد) with two dāls (دالين). Its answer (جوابه) is omitted, meaning: he will not harm the religion of Allah in any way, for Allah will not leave His religion without supporters (أنصار) who protect it. Al-Qummi said: This is an address to the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم who usurped the right of the Family of Muhammad صلوات الله عليهم and reverted from the religion of Allah. فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ ** Allah loves them and they love Allah. The meaning of love from Allah and from the servants has already preceded. أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (١) merciful toward them, derived from al-dhill (الذِّلِّ) with a kasrah (بالكسر), which is gentleness (اللينّ), not from al-dhull (الذُّل) with a ḍammah (بالضّمِ), which is humiliation (الهوان). أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ ** harsh and severe toward them, derived from ‘azzahu (عَزَّه) when he overcame him (غلبه). يُجاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** by fighting for the exaltation of the word (كلمة) of Allah and the strengthening (إعزاز) of His religion. وَلا يَخافُونَ لَوْمَةَ لائِمٍ ** in what they undertake of jihad (الجهاد) and obedience (الطاعة). In al-Majma‘, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام and al-Sadiq عليه السلام: They are the Commander of the Faithful and his companions, until he fought those who fought him among the oath-breakers (الناكثين), the unjust (القاسطين), and the renegades (المارقين). He said: What supports this is that the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم described him with these attributes when He called upon him for the conquest of Khaybar, after the standard-bearer had been repelled from it back to Him time after time, while he was deeming the people cowardly and they were deeming him cowardly. [He said]: "Tomorrow I shall certainly give the standard to a man who loves Allah and His Messenger, and Whom Allah and His Messenger love, a repeated attacker (كرّاراً), not a fugitive (فرّار). He will not return until Allah grants victory through his hands." Then He gave it to him. As for the description of gentleness toward the people of faith, severity toward the disbelievers, and jihad in the way of Allah, along with the fact that he does not fear the blame of any blamer—this is something that cannot prevent Ali from deserving it, due to what was manifest of his severity toward the people of polytheism (الشرك) and disbelief (الكفر), his striking them, his famous stances in strengthening the religion (الملّة) and supporting the faith (الدين), and his compassion (الرأفة) toward the faithful. And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام that he said on the Day of Basra: "By Allah, the people of this verse have not been fought until today," and he recited this verse. And narrated from the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: "A group (رهط) of my Companions will come to me on the Day of Resurrection, and they will be driven away (٢) from the Pond (الحوض). So I will say: 'O Lord, my Companions! My Companions!' It will be said: 'You have no knowledge of what they innovated after you. Indeed, they reverted upon their heels, retreating backward (القهقرى)." Al-Qummi said that it was revealed concerning the Mahdi of the Ummah (الأمة) and his companions عليهم السلام, and its beginning is an address to those who wronged the Family of Muhammad صلوات الله عليهم, killed them, and usurped their right. And in al-Majma‘: It is possible to support this by noting that His saying, the Glorified, فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ ** necessitates that those people were not present at the time the address was revealed. Thus, it includes whoever comes after them possessing this attribute until the Hour (السَّاعة) is established. I say: There is no contradiction between the two narrations, based on what we established in the introductions regarding the permissibility of generalization (التعميم). ذلِكَ فَضْلُ اللهِ ** meaning: their love for Allah, the Glorified, their gentleness toward the faithful, and their severity toward the disbelievers is a favor (تفِضّل) from Allah, a success (توفيق), a kindness (لطف) from Him, and a bounty (منّة) from His side. يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** He grants it to whomever He knows is worthy of it. وَاللهُ واسِعٌ ** generous (جواد) Who does not fear the depletion (نفاد) of what He possesses, عَلِيمٌ ** all-knowing, of the place of His generosity (جوده) and His giving (عطائه).

(١) Meaning, just as Allah permitted their destruction. It was mentioned due to the suitability of His saying: Allah may bring about a victory or a command from Him.

(٢) Al-Bajaḥ (البَجَحْ) with a fatḥah (محرّكة) is joy (الفَرَح).

(١) Ibn Abbas said: You see them toward the faithful like a child toward his parent, and like a servant toward his master, and in severity toward the disbelievers they are like a beast of prey toward its quarry.

(٢) Ḥala’tu (حلأت) the camels, with tashdīd (بالتّشديد), taḥli’atan (تحلئةً) and taḥlā (تحلاً), means I drove them away from it and prevented them from seeing it. The same applies to things other than camels.

(٥٥) إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَهُمْ راكِعُونَ. (55) Your guardian (walī) is only Allah, His Apostle, and the faithful who maintain the prayer and give the zakāt while bowing down.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding the interpretation of this verse: It means the most worthy of you (أولى بكم), that is, the most entitled to you and your affairs, even more than yourselves and your wealth. اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (Allah, His Apostle, and the faithful) means Ali and his children, the Imams, until the Day of Resurrection. Then Allah described them, saying: الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَهُمْ راكِعُونَ ** (who maintain the prayer and give the zakāt while bowing down). The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was performing the noon prayer (صلاة الظهر). He had completed two rak'ahs and was bowing (راكع). He was wearing a garment (حلة) worth a thousand dinars, which the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had given him, and which the Negus (النجاشي) had gifted to Him. A beggar came and said: "Peace be upon you, O guardian (وليّ) of Allah and the one most worthy of the believers than their own selves. Give charity (تصدّق) to a poor person." So he threw the garment (الحلّة) to him and gestured with his hand for him to take it. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed this verse concerning him, and He made the blessing (نعمة) of his children dependent on His blessing (نعمته). Thus, every one of his children who reaches the station of Imamate (مبلغ الإمامة) is like him in this blessing, and they give charity while they are bowing (راكعون). The beggar who asked the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was one of the angels, and those who ask the Imams among his children are also from the angels.

And narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq), from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them all), regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: يَعْرِفُونَ نِعْمَتَ اللهِ ثُمَّ يُنْكِرُونَها ** (They recognize the blessing of Allah, then they deny it). He said: When the verse إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ ** was revealed, a group of the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم gathered in the Mosque of Medina. Some of them said: "If we disbelieve in this verse, we disbelieve in the rest of them. But if we believe, then this is humiliation (ذلّ) when Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) is given authority (يسلّط) over us." So they said: "We know that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is truthful in what He says, but we will take him as our guardian (نتولّاه) and not obey Ali in what he commands us." He said: Then this verse was revealed: يَعْرِفُونَ نِعْمَتَ اللهِ ثُمَّ يُنْكِرُونَها ** (They recognize the blessing of Allah, then they deny it), meaning the Guardianship (ولاية) of Ali, وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ الْكافِرُونَ (and most of them are faithless) regarding the Guardianship (الولاية).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked whether obedience to the Successors (الأوصياء) is obligatory (مفروضة). He replied: "Yes, they are those concerning whom Allah said: أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ (Obey Allah, and obey the Apostle and those vested with authority among you). And they are those concerning whom Allah said: إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا —the verse."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith: The hypocrites (المنافقون) said: "Is there anything else remaining for your Lord to obligate upon us after what He has already obligated, that you might mention it so that our souls may be reassured that nothing else remains?" So Allah revealed concerning that: قُلْ إِنَّما أَعِظُكُمْ بِواحِدَةٍ ** (Say: 'I advise you concerning only one thing'), meaning the Guardianship (الولاية). Then Allah revealed: إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ (Your guardian is only Allah, His Apostle)—the verse. And there is no disagreement among the Ummah that no one among them gave the zakāt while bowing (راكع) on that day except for one man. Had his name been mentioned in the Book, it would have been omitted along with what was omitted.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in a hadith, regarding His saying, the Glorified: يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ (O Apostle! Communicate what has been sent down to you from your Lord). He said: "And I shall explain to you the reason for the revelation of this verse. Indeed, Gabriel descended to me repeatedly, commanding me on behalf of my Lord, the Peace (السّلام), Who is the Peace (السّلام), that I should stand in this gathering and inform every white and black person that Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him) is my brother, my successor (وصييّ), my Caliph (خليفتي), and the Imam after me, and he is your guardian (وليّكم) after Allah and His Apostle. And Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, has revealed to me concerning this a verse from His Book: إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ —the verse. And Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) maintained the prayer and gave the zakāt while he was bowing (راكع), seeking Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in every state."

In al-Khisal, concerning the argument (احتجاج) of Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) against Abu Bakr, he said: "I adjure you by Allah, is the Guardianship (الولاية) from Allah, along with the guardianship of His Apostle, in the verse concerning the zakāt of the ring, for me or for you?" He replied: "Rather, it is for you." And in it (al-Khisal), concerning the merits (مناقب) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and their enumeration, he said: "As for the sixty-fifth, I was praying in the Mosque, and a beggar came while I was bowing (راكع). I handed him my ring from my finger, and Allah, the Exalted, revealed: إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ (Your guardian is only Allah, His Apostle)—the verse."

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): While the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was sitting, and with Him was a group of Jews, including Abdullah ibn Salam, this verse was revealed to Him. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then went out to the Mosque, and a beggar met Him. He asked: "Has anyone given you anything?" He replied: "Yes, that worshipper (المصليّ)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم came, and behold, it was the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

The narrations reported by both the general public (العامّة) and the select (الخاصّة) concerning the revelation of this verse regarding the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) are extremely numerous. It is reported in al-Majma' from the majority (جمهور) of the exegetes (المفسّرين) that it was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) when he gave his ring in charity while bowing (ركوعه), and his story is mentioned through Ibn Abbas and others. It is possible to reconcile what was narrated in al-Kafi—that the item given in charity was a garment (حلة)—with what others narrated and what is famous among the select (الخاصّة) and the general public (العامّة)—that it was a ring (خاتماً). This reconciliation is that perhaps he gave charity while bowing (ركوعه) once with the garment (الحلّة) and another time with the ring (الخاتم), and the verse was revealed after the second instance.
