And in His saying, the Exalted, **وَيُؤْتُونَ ** (and give), there is an indication of this, as it implies repetition and renewal, just as it also indicates the action of his children.

(٥٦) **وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللهِ هُمُ الْغالِبُونَ ** (56) Whoever takes Allah, His Apostle and the faithful as his guardians, [should know that] Allah’s party are indeed the victorious.

The apparent (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (المضمر) in هُمُ الْغالِبُونَ ** as an alert to the proof concerning it. And it is as if it was said: "For they are the party of Allah, and فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللهِ هُمُ الْغالِبُونَ " (1)—and to exalt their mention (تنويهاً بذكرهم), magnify their status, and honor them with this name, and as an allusion to whoever takes others besides these as allies, that he is the party of Satan (حزب الشيطان). The origin of al-Hizb (الحزب) is the people who gather for a matter that unites them (حزبهم).

In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ (the Ayah). He said: "Indeed, a group (رهطاً) of Jews embraced Islam, among them were Abdullah ibn Salam, Asad, Tha'labah, Ibn Amin, and Ibn Suriya. They came to the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: 'O Prophet of Allah, Musa entrusted his will to Yusha' ibn Nun, so who is your successor (وصيّك), O Messenger of Allah, and who is our guardian (وليّنا) after you?' So this Ayah was revealed: **إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ ** (the Ayah)."

The Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Stand up." So they stood up and came to the mosque. Suddenly, a beggar (سائل) was leaving. He (the Prophet) said: "O beggar, did anyone give you anything?" He said: "Yes, this ring." He asked: "Who gave it to you?" He replied: "That man who is praying gave it to me." He asked: "In what state did He give it to you?" He replied: "He was bowing (راكعاً)." The Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم pronounced the Takbir (كبّر), and the people of the mosque pronounced the Takbir. The Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) is your guardian after me." They said: "We are content with Allah as Lord, with Islam as religion, with Muhammad صلىّ الله عليه واله وسلم as Prophet, and with Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the prayers and peace of Allah be upon him) as guardian (وليّاً)." Then Allah revealed: وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللهِ هُمُ الْغالِبُونَ.

And it is narrated from Umar ibn al-Khattab that he said: "By Allah, I gave forty rings in charity while I was bowing (راكع) so that what was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib might be revealed concerning me, but it was not revealed."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (and the faithful) in this context are those entrusted with the creation (المؤتمنون على الخلائق)—the proofs (الحجَج) and the successors (الأَوصياء)—in every age following another.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله will come on the Day of Resurrection holding onto the waistband (حجزة) (1) of his Lord, and we will be holding onto the waistband (حجزة) of our Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله, and our followers (شيعتنا) will be holding onto our waistband (حجزتنا). Thus, we and our followers are the party of Allah (حزب الله). And the party of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله will come holding onto the religion of Allah, and we will come holding onto the religion of our Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله, and our followers will come holding onto our religion.

footnote (١) It is said: *Nawwahtu bi-ismihi* (*نوّهت باسمه*) with the doubling (*بالتّشديد*) when you raise his mention (*رفعت ذكره*). And *nawwahtuhu tanwihan* (*نوّهته تنويهاً*) when you raise him. And *nāha al-shay' yanūhu* (*ناه الشيء ينوه*) when it rises, so it is *na'yuhu* (*نأيه*). Al-Jazari said this.

**(٥٧) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَّخِذُوا الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا دِينَكُمْ هُزُواً وَلَعِباً مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ وَالْكُفَّارَ أَوْلِياءَ ** (57) O you who have faith! Do not take as friends those who take your religion for a mockery and a game, from among those who were given the Book before you, and the faithless.

And al-Kuffār (الْكُفَّارَ) was read with the jarr (genitive case), so that the prohibition against befriending them is based upon their taking their religion as mockery and play. This is hinting at the reason (العلّة) and alerting that whoever is of this nature is far from being befriended (الموالاة), deserving of enmity (المعاداة). It is said that it was revealed concerning Rifā‘ah ibn Zayd and Suwayd ibn al-Hārith, who outwardly showed Islam (الإِسلام) then became hypocrites (نافقا). And there were men among the Muslims who befriended them. He specified the hypocrites (المنافقين) with the name al-Kuffār (الْكُفَّارَ), even though it encompasses the People of the Book (اهل الكتاب), due to the doubling of their disbelief (كفرهم). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ. (And be wary of Allah, should you be faithful.)

(٥٨) وَإِذا نادَيْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ اتَّخَذُوها هُزُواً وَلَعِباً ** (58) And when you call to prayer, they take it as a mockery and a game. They took the prayer (الصّلوة) and the call (المناداة) as a laughingstock (مضحكة). It was narrated: A Christian in Medina, when he heard the caller to prayer (المؤذّن) say: "I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله," he would say: "May Allah burn the liar." Then, one night, his servant entered with fire while his family was asleep, and a spark flew into the house, burning him and his family. ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لا يَعْقِلُونَ ** That is because they are a people who do not apply reason. For foolishness (السّفه) leads to ignorance of the truth (الجهل بالحقّ) and mockery (الهزء) (2) of it, while reason (العقل) prevents it.

footnote (١) In a *hadith* of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "Hold fast to the waist-wrapper (*حجزة*) of this bald man (*الأنزع*)," meaning Ali عليه السلام, "for he is the Greatest Truthful One (*الصِّدّيق الأكبر*) and the Greatest Distinguisher (*الفاروق الأعظم*), distinguishing between truth and falsehood." The word *الحجزة* (*al-ḥujzah*), with the *ḥā* (*الحاء*) undotted (*المهملة*) being *ḍammah* (*ضمّ*), the *jīm* (*الجيم*) being *sukūn* (*إسكان*), and the *zāy* (*الزاي*), is the knotting place of the lower garment (*الإزار*). Then the lower garment (*الإزار*) itself was called *حجزة* (*ḥujzah*) due to proximity (*للمجاورة*). The plural is *حجز* (*ḥujaz*), like *غرفة* (*ghurfah*) and *غرف* (*ghuraf*). Taking hold of the *حجزة* (*al-ḥujzah*) has been metaphorically used (*استعير*) for adherence (*التمسّك*) and holding fast (*الاعتصام*), meaning: adhere to Him and hold fast to Him.

footnote (٢) *الهزو* (*al-hazw*) and *الهزء* (*al-haz’*) mean ridicule (*السّخرية*) and contempt (*الاستخفاف*). It is transitive using the preposition *bā’* (*الباء*), so one says *هزأت به* (*haz’tu bihi*) and *استهزأت به* (*istahza’tu bihi*), meaning: I ridiculed him (*سخرت به*). It is also said *هزأت منه* (*haz’tu minhu*).

(٥٩) قُلْ يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ هَلْ تَنْقِمُونَ مِنَّا Do you deny (تُنْكِرُونَ) us and find fault (تَعِيبُونَ) with us إِلَّا أَنْ آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْنا وَما أُنْزِلَ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** Say, 'O People of the Book! Do you fault us except because we believe in Allah and what has been sent down to us and what was sent down before? [We believe] in all the revealed Books (بالكتب المنزلة كلّها). وَأَنَّ أَكْثَرَكُمْ فاسِقُونَ ** And that most of you are transgressors?' And because most of you are deviating (خَارِجُونَ) from the command (أَمْر) of Allah, seeking leadership (طَلَباً لِلرّيَاسَة) and out of envy (حَسَداً) for the station of prophethood (مَنْزِلَة النّبُوَّة).

(٦٠) قُلْ هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِشَرٍّ مِنْ ذلِكَ ** (60) Say, 'Shall I inform you of something worse than that?' The denounced (المنقوم). Meaning: If that is evil in your view, then I shall inform you of something worse than it. مَثُوبَةً ** (as a recompense) A fixed recompense عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (with Allah) And المثوبة (recompense) is specific to good, just as العقوبة (punishment) is specific to evil. It was placed here in its position following the style of His saying, glory be to Him: فَبَشِّرْهُمْ بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ (So give them the good news of a painful punishment) مَنْ لَعَنَهُ اللهُ ** (those whom Allah has cursed) He removed him far from His mercy وَغَضِبَ عَلَيْهِ (and incurred His wrath upon him) and He was displeased with him due to his disbelief and his immersion in sins after the clarity of the signs (الآيات). وَجَعَلَ مِنْهُمُ الْقِرَدَةَ وَالْخَنازِيرَ ** (and turned some of them into apes and swine) He transformed them (مسخهم). وَعَبَدَ الطَّاغُوتَ ** (and who worshipped the Tāghūt) And it was recited (1) with a dammah on the bā’ and a kasrah on the tā’ [referring to وَعُبِدَ الطَّاغُوتِ]. And الطَّاغُوتَ is Satan, and everyone worshipped besides Allah. It was said that those made into apes (الْقِرَدَةَ) are the People of the Sabbath (أصحاب السّبت), and the swine (الْخَنازِيرَ) are the disbelievers among the People of the Table of Jesus, upon our Prophet and him be peace. And it was said that both groups are the People of the Sabbath: their young men were transformed into apes and their old men into swine. And those who worshipped الطَّاغُوتَ are the People of the Calf (أصحاب العجل). And what has been narrated concerning that will come later in this sūrah. أُولئِكَ ** (They) Those cursed ones شَرٌّ مَكاناً وَأَضَلُّ عَنْ سَواءِ السَّبِيلِ (are in a worse plight and further astray from the right path.) Further astray from the right path (سَواءِ السَّبِيلِ), meaning: from the straight path (قصد الطّريق) which is the middle way between the extremism (غلوّ) of the Christians and the extremism (غلوّ) of the Jews. And the intent behind the two forms of elative (صيغتي التّفضيل*) is absolute increase, not in comparison to the believers.

(٦١) وَإِذا جاؤُكُمْ قالُوا آمَنَّا ** (61) And when they come to you, they say, 'We believe,' Al-Qummi said it was revealed concerning 'Abd Allah ibn Ubayy. وَقَدْ دَخَلُوا بِالْكُفْرِ وَهُمْ قَدْ خَرَجُوا بِهِ ** though they entered with disbelief, and they departed with it. They depart from you just as they entered; what they heard from you does not affect them. وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما كانُوا يَكْتُمُونَ ** and Allah knows best what they used to conceal. of disbelief; in this is a warning (وعيد) for them.

(٦٢) وَتَرى كَثِيراً مِنْهُمْ يُسارِعُونَ فِي الْإِثْمِ ** the disobedience (المعصية) وَالْعُدْوانِ ** transgressing the limits of Allah (تعدّي حدود الله) وَأَكْلِهِمُ السُّحْتَ ** the forbidden (الحرام), such as bribery (كالرشوة) لَبِئْسَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ.** (62) And you see many of them vying with one another in sin and aggression, and their consuming the unlawful. Evil indeed is what they have been doing.

footnote (١) That is, Hamzah alone read (it). Abu Ali said: His argument (*حجّته*) for his reading is that He carries it upon what was acted upon in it (*جعل*), as if He made (*جعل*) among them the worshipper (*عبد*) of the *Taghut* (*الطاغوت*). And the meaning of *ja‘ala* (*جعل*) is created (*خلق*), like His saying: **وَجَعَلَ الظُّلُماتِ وَالنُّورَ ** (and He made the darkness and the light) and **جَعَلَ مِنْها زَوْجَها* ** (and He made its spouse from it). And *‘abd* (*عبد*) is not a plural in wording, because there is nothing among the structures of plurals (*ابنية الجموع*) built upon this pattern, but rather it is a singular intended to mean plurality. Do you not see that among the singular nouns (*الأسماء المفردة*) added to definite nouns (*المعارف*) there is that whose wording is singular but whose meaning is plural, as in His saying: **وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللهِ لا تُحْصُوها ** (And if you enumerate Allah’s blessings, you will not be able to count them)? And because the pattern *fa‘ul* (*فَعُل*) is intended for emphasis (*المبالغة*) and plurality, such as *yaqiz* (*يقظ*) and *nadas* (*ندس*). So its estimation (*تقديره*) is that he has gone to every extent in the worship of the *Taghut* (*الطّاغوت*), and that has been repeated for him. And whoever vocalized with a *fatḥah* (*فتح*) and said *wa ‘abada al-ṭāghūta* (*وَعَبَدَ الطَّاغُوتَ*), then he is conjugating it upon the past tense structure (*بناء الماضي*) which is in the relative clause (*الصلة*), which is His saying: **لَعَنَهُ اللهُ ** (Allah cursed him). And the pronoun in *‘abada* (*عبد*) was made singular, even though the meaning implies plurality, because the discourse is carried upon its wording (*لفظه*) not its meaning (*معناه*). And its agent (*فاعله*) is the pronoun referring to *man* (*من*), just as the agent (*فاعله*) of the examples (*الأمثلة*) for the conjoined element (*المعطوف عليه*) is the pronoun referring to *man* (*من*). So it was made singular to carry all of that upon the wording. And if the whole were carried upon the meaning, or some upon the meaning, or some upon the wording and some upon the meaning, it would have been correct (*مستقيماً*).

(٦٣) لَوْ لَا يَنْهَاهُمُ الرَّبَّانِيُّونَ وَالْأَحْبَارُ عَن قَوْلِهِمُ الْإِثْمَ وَأَكْلِهِمُ السُّحْتَ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ (63) Why do not the masters and the rabbis forbid them from uttering sinful words and eating illicit gain? Evil indeed is what they have been working.

لَوْ لا يَنْهاهُمُ الرَّبَّانِيُّونَ وَالْأَحْبارُ ** — their scholars (علماؤهم) — عَنْ قَوْلِهِمُ الْإِثْمَ ** (from uttering sinful words) — such as lying (الكذب) and the word of polytheism (كلمة الشّرك), like [saying] 'Uzayr is the son of Allah (عزيز بن الله), وَأَكْلِهِمُ السُّحْتَ (and eating illicit gain). It was said that لَوْ لَا (why not) when it precedes a past tense verb (الماضي) conveys reproach (التّوبيخ), and when it precedes a future tense verb (المستقبل) it conveys specification (التّخْصيص). لَبِئْسَ ما كانُوا يَصْنُونَ ** (Evil indeed is what they have been working) — He censured them with a censure more severe than the censure of those who commit major sins (مرتكبي الكبائر), because every doer (عامل) is not called a worker (صانعاً) until he masters his work (عمله) and becomes skilled (يتمهّر). And the reason (الوجه) for this is that abandoning a good deed (ترك الحسنة) is uglier than committing a sin (مواقعة المعصية), because the soul finds pleasure (تلتذّ) in the sin and inclines towards it (تميل اليها), but abandoning the denunciation (ترك الإِنكار) of it is not like that. Narrated from Ibn 'Abbas (ابن عبّاس): This is the most severe verse (آية) in the Quran.

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (may the prayers of Allah be upon him) in one of His sermons (خطبة): "Indeed, those who were before you perished when they committed acts of disobedience (المعاصي) and the masters (الرّبانيّون) and the rabbis (الأحبار) did not forbid them from that. And when they persisted (تمادوا) in acts of disobedience and the masters and the rabbis did not forbid them from that, the punishments (العقوبات) descended upon them. So, command the good (المعروف) and forbid the evil (المنكر)." — the Hadith.

And in another saying of His, in a narrative (حديث) related by Ibn Shu'bah in Tuhaf al-Uqul, He said: "Take heed, O people, by what Allah admonished His friends (أولياءه) with, concerning His severe condemnation (سوء ثنائه) of the rabbis (الأحبار). He says: لَوْ لَا يَنْهَاهُمُ الرَّبَّانِيُّونَ وَالْأَحْبَارُ عَن قَوْلِهِمُ الْإِثْمَ (Why do not the masters and the rabbis forbid them from uttering sinful words?). And He said: لُعِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ ** (Cursed were those of the Children of Israel who were faithless) up to His saying: لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ (Evil indeed was what they used to do). Other reports concerning this have already passed in Surat Āl ‘Imrān at the Almighty's saying: وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أُمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ.** (And let there be a nation among you who call to good)."

(٦٤) وَقالَتِ الْيَهُودُ يَدُ اللهِ مَغْلُولَةٌ ** (64) The Jews say, 'Allah's hand is tied up.' It is said that the tying (غلّ) (1) of the hand is a metaphor (كناية) for stinginess (البخل), and the stretching of it is a metaphor for generosity (الجود). And al-Qummi said: They said that Allah has finished the matter (الأمر); Allah does not bring forth anything other than what He decreed in the first decree (التّقدير الأوّل). So Allah refuted them and said: بَلْ يَداهُ مَبْسُوطَتانِ يُنْفِقُ كَيْفَ يَشاءُ ** Nay, His hands are outstretched: He spends as He wishes. Meaning: He advances and delays, increases and decreases, and to Him belongs al-Bada' (البداء) and the Will (المشيّة). In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: They did not mean that He is physically like this, but rather they said that He has finished the matter (الأمر), so He neither increases nor decreases. Allah, majestic is His glory, said, in refutation of their saying: غُلَّتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَلُعِنُوا بِما قالُوا بَلْ يَداهُ مَبْسُوطَتانِ يُنْفِقُ كَيْفَ يَشاءُ (Their hands shall be tied up, and they shall be cursed for what they said. Nay, His hands are outstretched: He spends as He wishes). Have you not heard the Almighty Allah say: يَمْحُوا اللهُ ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ Allah effaces and confirms whatever He wishes, and with Him is the Mother of the Book. In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a discourse of His: regarding the affirmation of al-Bada' (البداء) with Sulayman al-Marwazi, who used to deny it. He (peace be upon him) said: "I reckon you resemble the Jews in this matter (الباب)." He said: "I seek refuge in Allah from that! And what did the Jews say?" He (peace be upon him) said: قالَتِ الْيَهُودُ يَدُ اللهِ مَغْلُولَةٌ ** (The Jews say, 'Allah's hand is tied up.'), meaning that Allah has finished the matter (الأمر) and does not bring forth anything new—the rest of the narration (الحديث). And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They mean that He has finished what was (ممّا هو كان). غُلَّتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَلُعِنُوا بِما قالُوا ** (Their hands shall be tied up, and they shall be cursed for what they said) is a supplication (دعاء) against them. بَلْ يَداهُ مَبْسُوطَتانِ (Nay, His hands are outstretched). The dual form (تثنية) (1) of the hand (اليد) is an indication of the correspondence (تقابل) of His glorious Names (أسمائه) and a metaphor (كناية) for the utmost generosity (غاية الجود), for the extremely generous person (الجواد في الغاية) gives with both His hands together. يُنْفِقُ كَيْفَ يَشاءُ ** He spends as He wishes. according to what wisdom (الحكمة) and righteousness (الصّلاح) necessitate. وَلَيَزِيدَنَّ كَثِيراً مِنْهُمْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ طُغْياناً وَكُفْراً What has been sent down to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in rebellion and disbelief. upon their rebellion (طغيانهم) and disbelief (كفرهم), just as a sick person increases in sickness from consuming the food of healthy people (الأصحّاء). وَأَلْقَيْنا بَيْنَهُمُ الْعَداوَةَ وَالْبَغْضاءَ إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ ** We have cast enmity and hatred among them until the Day of Resurrection. Thus, their words (كلماتهم) are divergent and their hearts are scattered (شتّى), so no agreement (موافقة) occurs between them (2). كُلَّما أَوْقَدُوا ناراً لِلْحَرْبِ أَطْفَأَهَا اللهُ ** Whenever they ignite the fire of war, Allah extinguishes it. Whenever they intended to wage war, they were defeated (غلبوا). It is said that they were in the strongest might (أشدّ بأس) and the most fortified dwelling (امنع دار), so much so that Quraysh used to rely on them, and the Aws and the Khazraj used to gain strength through their support (مظاهرتهم). Then they were humiliated (ذلّوا) and subdued (قهروا). The Prophet killed Banu Qurayzah, expelled Banu al-Nadir, conquered Khaybar and Fadak, and Allah eradicated their root (شأفتهم) (3), such that today you find the Jews in every town (بلدة) to be the most humiliated of people. وَيَسْعَوْنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَساداً ** and they try to wreak corruption on the earth. —for the purpose of corruption (للفساد) by disobeying the command of Allah and striving to erase the mention of the Messenger from their books. It is said that when they violated the ruling (حكم) of the Torah (التّورية), Allah empowered Nebuchadnezzar (بخت نصّر) over them. Then they committed corruption (أفسدوا), so He empowered Photorus the Roman (فطرس الرّومي) over them. Then they committed corruption, so He empowered the Magians (المجوس) over them. Then they committed corruption, so He empowered the Muslims over them. وَاللهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** And Allah does not love the corruptors. so He does not recompense them except with evil (شرّاً).

(٦٥) **وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ آمَنُوا ** (65) And if the People of the Book had believed in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and in what He brought,

وَاتَّقَوْا لَكَفَّرْنا عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ (and been Godwary, We would surely have absolved them of their misdeeds) which they committed, and We would not hold them accountable for them,

**وَلَأَدْخَلْناهُمْ جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ ** (and admitted them into gardens of bliss.) For Islam wipes out what preceded it, even if it was great.

(٦٦) وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ أَقامُوا التَّوْراةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ ** (66) And had they maintained the Torah and the Evangel by publicizing what is in them and upholding their rulings, وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ and what has been sent down to them from their Lord, In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the Wilayah (الولاية). لَأَكَلُوا مِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ وَمِنْ تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِهِمْ ** they would surely have eaten from above them and from beneath their feet. He would have expanded their provisions for them and poured upon them blessings from the heaven and the earth. Al-Qummi said: مِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ ** (from above them) means the rain (المطر), and وَمِنْ تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِهِمْ ** (from beneath their feet) means the vegetation (النّبات). مِنْهُمْ أُمَّةٌ مُقْتَصِدَةٌ ** Among them is a moderate community, who have entered Islam. Al-Qummi said: They are a group (قوم) from the Jews who entered Islam, and Allah named them مُقْتَصِدَةٌ (moderate). وَكَثِيرٌ مِنْهُمْ ساءَ ما يَعْمَلُونَ ** and many of them—evil is what they do. And in this [phrase] is the meaning of astonishment (التعجّب), meaning: how evil is their deed! And they are those who persisted in denial (الجحود) and disbelief (الكفر).

(67) يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (O Messenger! Communicate what has been sent down to you from your Lord) meaning concerning Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him). It is narrated from them (peace be upon them) that it was revealed thus: وَإِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلْ فَما بَلَّغْتَ رِسالَتَهُ (١) (And if you do not, you have not communicated His message.) (1) If you abandon communicating what was revealed to you concerning the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) and conceal it, it would be as if you had not communicated anything from the messages, thereby deserving punishment. And risālatuhu (رِسالَتَهُ) was read with al-Tawhid (التَّوحيد) (singular form). وَاللهُ يَعْصِمُكَ مِنَ النَّاسِ ** (and Allah shall protect you from the people.) He will prevent them from harming you. إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْكافِرِينَ (Indeed Allah does not guide the faithless lot.) In al-Jawami’, narrated from Ibn Abbas and Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him): Indeed, Allah the Almighty commanded His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to appoint Ali (peace be upon him) for the people and inform them of his Wilayah (ولايته). The Prophet feared that they might say he favored his cousin, and that this would be difficult for a group of his companions. So this verse was revealed. Then he took his hand on the Day of Ghadir Khumm and said صلى الله عليه وآله: "Of whomsoever I am the Mawla (مولاه), Ali is his Mawla (مولاه)." And al-Ayyashi narrated from both of them (may Allah be pleased with them both) what is similar in meaning. And it was narrated in al-Majma’ from al-Tha’labi, al-Haskani, and others from the ‘Ammah (العامّة). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a Hadith: Then the Wilayah (الولاية) was revealed. This came to Him on Friday at Arafah, when Allah the Almighty revealed: الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي ** (This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My blessing upon you.) The perfection of the religion was through the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him). Thereupon, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "My community has recently emerged from the Age of Ignorance (الجاهليّة). If I inform them of this concerning my cousin, one speaker will say something, and another speaker will say something else." So I said to myself, without my tongue uttering it, then a decisive decree (عزيمة) from Allah came to me, absolute (1), threatening me with punishment if I did not communicate it. Then the verse was revealed: O Messenger! Communicate... the verse. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله took Ali (peace be upon him) by the hand and said: "O people! There was no Prophet among the Prophets before me except that Allah granted him life, then called him, and he responded. Soon I shall be called and I shall respond. I am accountable, and you are accountable. So what will you say?" They said: "We bear witness that you have communicated, advised sincerely, and fulfilled your duty. May Allah reward you with the best reward of the Messengers." He said: "O Allah, bear witness," three times. Then he said: "O assembly of Muslims! This is your Wali (وليّكم) after me. Let the present among you inform the absent." Abu Ja’far (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, he was Allah's Trustee (أمين الله) over His creation, His unseen, and His religion which He approved for Himself." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded His Messenger concerning the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon them both) and revealed to you: Your guardian is only Allah, His Messenger... the verse. And He made the Wilayah (ولاية) of the holders of authority (أولي الأمر) obligatory, but they did not know what it was. So Allah commanded Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله to explain the Wilayah (الولاية) to them, just as He explained the Prayer (الصّلاة), the Zakat (الزّكاة), the Fast (الصّوم), and the Hajj (الحجّ) to them. When this command came to him from Allah, the breast of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله became constrained by it, and he feared that they would apostatize from their religion and deny him. So his breast became constrained, and he consulted his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic. Then Allah the Almighty revealed to Him: O Messenger!... the verse. And He proclaimed the command of Allah, exalted be His remembrance. So he stood up for the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) on the Day of Ghadir Khumm, and called out: "Prayer is comprehensive (الصلاة جامعةً)," and commanded the people that the present should inform the absent (1). He (peace be upon him) said: The obligatory duties (الفرائض) were revealed one after the other, and the Wilayah (الولاية) was the last of the obligatory duties. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My blessing upon you. He said: Allah the Almighty, the Mighty and Majestic, says: "I shall not reveal an obligatory duty to you after this, for I have perfected the obligatory duties for you." And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him) that he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم performed Hajj from Medina, having already communicated all the laws (الشرايع) to his people except for the Hajj and the Wilayah (الولاية). Then Gabriel (peace be upon him) came to Him and said: "O Muhammad, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, conveys His greetings to you and says to you: 'I have not taken any Prophet from My Prophets nor any Messenger from My Messengers except after perfecting My religion and confirming My proof (حجتي). Two obligatory duties remain for you to communicate to your people: the obligatory duty of Hajj, and the obligatory duty of Wilayah (الولاية) and the Caliphate (الخلافة) after you. For I have not left My earth without a Proof (حجة) and I shall never leave it without one. Indeed, Allah commands you to communicate the Hajj to your people: you shall perform Hajj, and everyone who can afford the journey, from the settled people, the surrounding areas, and the Bedouins, shall perform Hajj with you. You shall teach them concerning their Hajj just as you taught them concerning their prayers (صلواتهم), their Zakat (زكوتهم), and their fasting (صيامهم), and you shall establish them in that, according to the example upon which you established them in all the laws (الشرايع) you communicated to them.'" Then a caller from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم announced among the people: "Behold! The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم intends to perform Hajj and to teach you concerning it, just as He taught you concerning the laws (شرايع) of your religion, and to establish you in that, according to what He established you in concerning other matters." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم departed, and the people departed with Him, listening intently to see what He would do so that they might do likewise. He performed Hajj with them, and those who performed Hajj with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from the people of Medina, the surrounding areas, and the Bedouins reached seventy thousand people or more, similar to the number of the companions of Moses—seventy thousand—from whom the oath of allegiance (بيعة) to Aaron was taken, but they broke it and followed the Calf and the Samiri (السّامريّ). Likewise, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took the oath of allegiance (البيعة) for Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) for the Caliphate (الخلافة), corresponding to the number of the companions of Moses, but they broke the oath (البيعَة) and followed the Calf, tradition by tradition (سنّة بسنّة) and example by example (مثلاً بمثل). And the Talbiyah (التّلبية) continued between Mecca and Medina. When He stood at the appointed place (بالموقوفِ), Gabriel came to Him from Allah the Almighty and said: "O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, Allah the Almighty conveys His greetings to you and says to you that your term and your appointed time have drawn near, and I am bringing you forward to that which is inevitable and from which there is no escape. So make your covenant (1), present your testament (وصيتك), and proceed to what you possess of knowledge, the inheritance of the sciences of the Prophets before you, the weapons, the Ark (التّابوت), and all the signs (آيات) of the Prophets that you have, and hand them over to your successor (وصيّك) and Caliph (خَليفتك) after you, My ultimate Proof (حجّتي البالغة) over My creation, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). So establish him for the people as a sign (علماً), renew his covenant, his solemn pledge (ميثاقه), and his oath of allegiance (بيعته), and remind them of the oath of allegiance (بيعتي) and My solemn pledge (ميثاقي) that I took from them, and My covenant (عهدي) that I entrusted to them concerning the Wilayah (ولاية) of My Wali (وليّ) and their Mawla (مولاهم), and the Mawla (مولى) of every believing man and woman, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). For I have not taken any Prophet from the Prophets except after perfecting My religion and completing My blessing through the Wilayah (ولاية) of My Awliya’ (أوليائي) and the enmity toward My enemies. That is the perfection of My Monotheism (توحيدي) and My religion, and the completion of My blessing upon My creation is through following My Wali (وليّي) and obeying him. This is because I do not leave My earth without a Guardian (قيّم) to be a Proof (حجّة) for Me over My creation. So This day I have perfected for you your religion... the verse, through the Wilayah (ولاية) of My Wali (وليي) and the Mawla (مولى) of every believing man and woman, Ali, My servant, the successor (وصي) of My Prophet, and the Caliph after Him, and My ultimate Proof (حجتي البالغة) over My creation. His obedience is coupled with the obedience of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, My Prophet, and his obedience, along with the obedience of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, is coupled with My obedience. Whoever obeys him has obeyed Me, and whoever disobeys him has disobeyed Me. I have made him a sign (علماً) between Me and My creation. Whoever recognizes him is a believer, and whoever denies him is a disbeliever, and whoever associates partners with his oath of allegiance (بيعته) is a polytheist (مشرك). Whoever meets Me with his Wilayah (بولايته) shall enter Paradise, and whoever meets Me with enmity toward him shall enter the Fire. So establish Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both), O Muhammad, as a sign (عليماً), take the oath of allegiance (البيعة) from them for him, and renew My covenant and My solemn pledge (ميثاقي) for them, upon which I bound them, for I am taking you back to Me and bringing you forward to Me." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم feared his people and the people of hypocrisy (النِّفاق) and dissension (الشّقاق) that they might disperse and return to the Age of Ignorance (جاهليّةً), due to what He knew of their enmity and the hatred (البغضة) (1) that their souls harbored toward Ali (peace be upon him). He asked Gabriel to ask his Lord for protection (العصمة) from the people. He waited for Gabriel to bring Him the protection from the people from Allah, Glorious is His Name, but this was delayed until He reached the Mosque of al-Khayf (2). Gabriel came to Him in the Mosque of al-Khayf and commanded Him to make His covenant and establish Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) for the people, but he did not bring Him the protection from Allah, Glorious is His Majesty, that He desired. This continued until He reached Kura’ al-Ghamim, between Mecca and Medina. Gabriel (peace be upon him) came to Him there and commanded Him with what He had been commanded by Allah before, but he did not bring Him the protection from Allah, Glorious is His Majesty, that He desired. So He said: "O Gabriel, I fear my people will deny me and will not accept my word concerning Ali (peace be upon him)." He traveled on. When He reached Ghadir Khumm, three miles before al-Juhfah, Gabriel came to Him five hours into the day, with rebuke and reprimand (3), and protection (العصمة) from the people. He said: "O Muhammad, Allah the Almighty conveys His greetings to you and says to you: O Messenger! Communicate what has been sent down to you from your Lord concerning Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him), and if you do not, you have not communicated His message, and Allah shall protect you from the people." The first of them were close to al-Juhfah (4). So He commanded that those who had gone ahead should be brought back, and those who lagged behind should be held in that place, so that He might establish Ali (peace be upon him) for the people and communicate to them what Allah the Almighty had revealed concerning Ali (peace be upon him). And He informed Him that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had protected Him from the people. When the protection (العصمة) came to Him, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded a caller to announce among the people: "Prayer is comprehensive (الصّلوة جامعةً)," and to bring back those who had gone ahead and hold back those who lagged behind. He moved away from the right side of the road to the side of the Mosque of al-Ghadir. Gabriel commanded Him to do this on behalf of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. In that place there were acacia trees (سلمات) (5). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that what was beneath them should be swept (6) and that stones should be set up for Him in the form of a pulpit (المنبر) so that He could overlook the people. So the people returned, and the last of them were held in that place, remaining there. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم stood upon those stones, then praised Allah the Almighty and lauded Him, and said صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي عَلَا فيِ تَوَحُّدِهِ وَدَنَا فيِ تَفَرُّدِهِ وَجَلَّ فيِ سَلْطَنَتِهِ وَعَظُمَ فيِ أَرْكَانِهِ وَأَحَاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلْماً وَهُوَ فيِ مَكَانِهِ وَقَهَرَ جَمِيعَ الْخَلْقِ بِقُدْرَتِهِ وَبُرْهَانِهِ مَجِيداً لَمْ يَزَلْ مَحْمُوداً لَا يَزَالُ بَارِئُ المَسْمُوكاتِ وَدَاحِي الْمَدْخوَّاتِ وَجَبَّارُ اْلأَرْضِيِنَ وَالسَّموَاتِ سُبُّوحٌ قُدُّوسٌ رَبُّ الْمَلَائِكَةِ وَالرُّوحِ مُتَفَضِّلٌ عَلَى جَمِيعِ مَنْ بَرَاهُ مُتَطَوِّلٌ عَلَى جَمِيعِ مَنْ أَنْشَأَهُ يَلْحَظُ كُلَّ عَيْنٍ وَالْعُيُونُ لَا تَرَاهُ كَرِيمٌ حَلِيمٌ ذُو أَنَاةٍ قَدْ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَيْءِ بِرَحْمَتِهِ وَمَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ بِنِعْمَتِهِ لَا يَعْجَلُ بِانْتِقَامِهِ وَلَا يُبَادِرُ إِلَيْهِمْ بِمَا اسْتَحَقُّوا مِنْ عَذَابِهِ قَدْ فَهِمَ السَرَّائِرَ وَعَلِمَ الضَّمَآئِرَ وَلَا يَخْفَى عَلَيْهِ الْمَكْنُونَاتُ وَلَا اشْتَبَهَتْ عَلَيْهِ الْخَفِيَّاتُ لَهُ الإِحَاطَةُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَالْغَلَبَةُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَالْقُوَّةُ فيِ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَالْقُدْرَةُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْء وَهُوَ مُنْشِئُ الشّيْءِ حِينَ لَا شَيْءَ دَآئِمٌ قَآئِمٌ بِالْقِسْطِ لَا الهَ إلَّا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ جَلَّ عَنْ أَنْ تُدْرِكَهُ اْلأَبْصَارُ وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ اْلأَبْصَارَ وَهُوَ اللَّطيفُ الْخَبِيرُ لَا يَلْحَقُ أَحَدٌ وَصْفَهُ مِنْ مُعَايَنَةٍ وَلَا يَجِدُ أَحَدٌ كَيْفَ هُوَ مِنْ سِرِّ وَعَلَانِيَةٍ إلَّا بِمَا دَلَّ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ وَاشْهَدُ بِأَنَّهُ اللهُ الَّذِي مَلَأَ الدَّهْرَ قُدْسُهُ وَالَّذِي يُغْشِي اْلأَبَدَ نُورُهُ وَالَّذِي يَنْفُذُ أمْرُهُ بِلَا مُشَاوَرَةِ مُشِيرٍ وَلَا مَعَهُ شَرِيكٌ فيِ تَقْدِيرٍ وَلَا تَفَاوُتٌ فيِ تَدْبِيرٍ صَوَّرَ مَا أَبْدَعَ عَلَى غَيْرِ مِثَالٍ وَخَلَقَ مَا خَلَقَ بِلَا مَعُونَةٍ مِنْ أَحَدٍ وَلَا تَكَلّفٍ وَلَا احْتِيَالٍ أنْشَاهَا فَكَانَتْ وَبَرأَهَا فَبَانَتْ وَهُوَ اللهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إلَّا هُوَ الْمُتْقِنُ الصَّنْعَةِ الْحَسَنُ الصَّنيعَةِ الْعَدْلُ الَّذِي لَا يَجُورُ وَاْلأَكْرَمُ الَّذِي تُرْجَعُ إِلَيْهِ اْلأُمُورُ وَاشْهَدُ أَنَّهُ الَّذِي تَوَاضَعَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ لِقُدْرَتِهِ وَخَضَعَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ لَهِيْبَتِهِ مَالِكُ اْلأَمْلَاكِ وَمُفَلِّكُ اْلأَفْلَاكِ وَمُسَخِّرُ الشَّمْسِ وَالقَمَرِ كُّلٌ يَجْرِي لِأَجَلٍ مُسَمّىً يُكَوِّرُ اللَّيْلَ عَلَى النَّهَارِ وَيُكَوِّرُ النَّهَارَ عَلَى اللَّيْلِ يَطْلُبُهُ حَثِيثاً قَاصِمُ كُلِّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ وَمُهْلِكُ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ مَريد لَمْ يَكُنْ مَعَهُ ضِدٌّ وَلَا نِدٌّ أَحَدٌ صَمَدٌ لَمْ يَلْدِ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُواً أحَدٌ إلَهٌ وَاحِدٌ وَرَبٌّ مَاجِدٌ يَشَآءُ فَيَمْضِي وَيُرِيدُ فَيَقْضِي وَيَعْلَمُ وَيُحْصِي وَيُمِيتُ وَيُحْيي وَيُفْقِرُ وَيُغْني وَيُضْحِكُ وَيُبْكِي وَيُدْني وَيُقْصِي وَيَمْنَعُ وَيُعْطي لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ بِيَدِهِ الْخَيْرُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فيِ النَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهَارَ فيِ اللَّيْلِ لَا إلَهَ إلَّا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْغَفَّارُ مُسْتَجِيبُ الدُّعَآءِ وَمُجْزلُ الْعَطآءِ محْصِي اْلأَنْفَاسِ وَرَبُّ الْجَنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ لَا يُشْكِلُ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَلَا يُضْجِرُهُ صرُاخُ الْمُسْتَصْرِخِينَ وَلَا يُبْرِمُهُ الْحَاحُ الْمُلِحِّينَ العَاصِمُ لِلصَّالِحِينَ وَالْمُوَفِّقُ لِلْمُفْلِحِينَ وَمَوْلَى الْعَالَمِينَ الَّذِي اسْتَحَقَّ مِنْ كُلِّ مَنْ خَلَقَ أَنْ يَشْكُرَهُ وَيَحْمِدَهُ عَلَى السرَّاءِ وَالضَرَّآءِ وَالشِّدَّةِ وَالرَّخَآءِ وَأُومِنْ بِهِ وَبَمِلآئِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ أَسْمَعُ أَمْرَهُ وَأُطِيعُ وَأُبَادِرُ إلى كُلِّ مَا يَرْضَاهُ وَاسْتَسلِمُ لِقَضَائِهِ رَغْبَةً فيِ طَاعَتِهِ وَخَوْفاً مِنْ عُقُوبَتِهِ لِأنَّهُ الَّذِي لَا يُؤْمَنُ مَكْرُهُ وَلَا يُخَافَ جَوْرُهُ أُقرُّ عَلَى نَفْسِي بِالْعُبُودِيَّةِ وَأشْهَدُ لَهُ بِالرُّبُوبِيَّةِ وَأُؤَدِّي مَا أَوْحَى إِليَّ حَذَراً مِنْ أَنْ لَا أَفْعَلَ فَيَحِلَّ بِي مِنْهُ قَارِعَةٌ لَا يَدْفَعُهَا عَنِّي أَحَدٌ وَإنْ عَظُمَتْ حِيلَتُهُ لَا إلَهَ إلَّا هُوَ لِإنَّهُ قَدْ أعْلَمَنِي أَنّي إنْ لَمْ أُبَلِّغْ مَا أنْزَلَ إلَيَّ فَمَا بَلّغَتُ رِسَالَتَهُ فَقَدْ ضَمِنَ ليِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى الْعِصْمَةَ وَهُوَ اللهُ الْكَافيِ الكَرِيمُ فَأَوْحَى اللهُ إِلَيَّ بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم (All praise belongs to Allah, Who is Exalted in His Oneness (تَوَحُّدِهِ) and Near in His Singularity (تَفَرُّدِهِ), Majestic in His Sovereignty (سَلْطَنَتِهِ), and Great in His Pillars (أَرْكَانِهِ). He encompasses everything with knowledge while He is in His place. He subjugates all creation by His Power and His Proof (بُرْهَانِهِ). Glorious (مَجِيداً) He has ever been, Praised (مَحْمُوداً) He shall ever remain. The Originator of the high heavens (المَسْمُوكاتِ) and the Spreader of the flat earths (الْمَدْخوَّاتِ), the Compeller (جَبَّارُ) of the earths and the heavens. Holy (سُبُّوحٌ), Pure (قُدُّوسٌ), the Lord of the angels and the Spirit (الرُّوحِ). He is Bountiful (مُتَفَضِّلٌ) toward all whom He created, and Generous (مُتَطَوِّلٌ) toward all whom He originated. He observes every eye, yet the eyes do not see Him. He is Generous (كَرِيمٌ), Clement (حَلِيمٌ), Possessor of Deliberation (ذُو أَنَاةٍ). He has encompassed everything with His Mercy and conferred His blessing upon them with His favor. He does not hasten His retribution, nor does He rush upon them with the punishment they deserve. He has understood the secrets (السَرَّائِرَ) and known the innermost thoughts (الضَّمَآئِرَ). The hidden things (الْمَكْنُونَاتُ) are not concealed from Him, nor are the secret matters (الْخَفِيَّاتُ) confused for Him. To Him belongs the encompassing of everything, the dominance over everything, the power in everything, and the capability over everything. Nothing is like Him, and He is the Originator of the thing when there was no thing. Everlasting (دَآئِمٌ), Upholding Justice (قَآئِمٌ بِالْقِسْطِ). There is no god except Him, the All-mighty (الْعَزِيزُ), the All-wise (الْحَكِيمُ). He is too Exalted for the sights to perceive Him, yet He perceives the sights, and He is the All-subtle (اللَّطيفُ), the All-aware (الْخَبِيرُ). No one can attain His description through observation, nor can anyone find out how He is, secretly or openly, except by what He, the Mighty and Majestic, has indicated concerning Himself. I bear witness that He is Allah, Whose sanctity (قُدْسُهُ) has filled eternity (الدَّهْرَ), Whose light (نُورُهُ) covers everlastingness (اْلأَبَدَ), Whose command (أمْرُهُ) is executed without the consultation of an advisor, and there is no partner with Him in decree (تَقْدِيرٍ), nor any discrepancy (تَفَاوُتٌ) in management (تَدْبِيرٍ). He fashioned what He originated without a prior example, and He created what He created without the assistance of anyone, without effort (تَكَلّفٍ) or contrivance (احْتِيَالٍ). He brought them into existence, and they were; He created them, and they became manifest. He is Allah, there is no god except Him, the Perfecter of creation (الْمُتْقِنُ الصَّنْعَةِ), the Excellent in Deed (الصَّنيعَةِ), the Just (الْعَدْلُ) Who does not oppress, and the Most Generous (اْلأَكْرَمُ) to Whom all affairs are returned. I bear witness that He is the One before Whose Power everything humbles itself, and before Whose Awe everything submits. The Owner of kingdoms (اْلأَمْلَاكِ), the Revolver of the spheres (اْلأَفْلَاكِ), and the Subjugator of the sun and the moon—each runs for a specified term. He wraps the night around the day, and wraps the day around the night, seeking it swiftly. The Destroyer (قَاصِمُ) of every obstinate tyrant (جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ) and the Annihilator (مُهْلِكُ) of every rebellious devil (شَيْطَانٍ مَريد). There was no opponent (ضِدٌّ) with Him, nor any equal (نِدٌّ). He is One (أَحَدٌ), the Eternal Refuge (صَمَدٌ). He neither begets nor is begotten, nor has He any equal (كُفُواً أحَدٌ). A Single God (إلَهٌ وَاحِدٌ), a Glorious Lord (رَبٌّ مَاجِدٌ). He wills, and it comes to pass; He desires, and He decrees. He knows and enumerates. He causes death and gives life. He impoverishes and enriches. He causes laughter and weeping. He brings near and distances. He withholds and gives. To Him belongs the Dominion (الْمُلْكُ), and to Him belongs the Praise (الْحَمْدُ). In His Hand is the good, and He is Powerful over everything. He makes the night pass into the day, and He makes the day pass into the night. There is no god except Him, the All-mighty (الْعَزِيزُ), the All-forgiving (الْغَفَّارُ). The Responder of supplication (مُسْتَجِيبُ الدُّعَآءِ), the Bestower of great gifts (مُجْزلُ الْعَطآءِ), the Enumerator of breaths (اْلأَنْفَاسِ), and the Lord of Paradise and mankind. Nothing is difficult for Him, nor is He wearied by the cries of those seeking help, nor is He annoyed by the persistence of the insistent. The Protector (العَاصِمُ) of the righteous, the Granter of success (الْمُوَفِّقُ) to the prosperous, and the Master (مَوْلَى) of the worlds, Who deserves that everyone He created should thank Him and praise Him in ease and hardship, in severity and prosperity. I believe in Him, in His angels, His Books, and His Messengers. I hear His command and I obey. I hasten to everything that pleases Him and I submit to His decree, out of desire for His obedience and fear of His punishment, because He is the One Whose cunning (مَكْرُهُ) is not safe from, and Whose injustice (جَوْرُهُ) is not feared. I affirm for myself servitude (الْعُبُودِيَّةِ) and I bear witness to His Lordship (الرُّبُوبِيَّةِ). I convey what He revealed to me, fearing that if I do not do so, a calamity (قَارِعَةٌ) from Him will befall me, which no one can avert from me, even if his stratagem (حِيلَتُهُ) is great. There is no god except Him, because He has informed me that if I do not communicate what He revealed to me, I have not communicated His message. Indeed, He, Blessed and Exalted, has guaranteed Me protection (الْعِصْمَةَ), and He is Allah, the Sufficient (الْكَافيِ), the Generous (الكَرِيمُ). So Allah revealed to me: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.) يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (O Messenger! Communicate what has been sent down to you from your Lord) concerning Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him), وَإِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلْ فَما بَلَّغْتَ رِسالَتَهُ وَاللهُ يَعْصِمُكَ مِنَ النَّاسِ ** (And if you do not, you have not communicated His message, and Allah shall protect you from the people.) مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ مَا قَصَّرْتُ فيِ تَبْلِيغِ مَا أنْزَلَهُ وَأَنَا مُبَيِّنٌ لَكُمْ سَبَبَ هذِهِ الآيَةِ إنَّ جَبْرِئِيلَ هَبَطَ الَيَّ مِرَاراً يَاْمُرُني عَنِ السَّلَامِ رَبِّي وَهُوَ السَّلامَ انْ أَقُومَ فيِ هذَا الْمَشْهَدِ فَاعْلِمَ كُلَّ ابْيضَ وَأسْوَدَ أَنَّ عَليَّ بِنْ أبي طالِبٍ عليه السلام أخِي وَوَصِيّي وَخَلِيفَتي وَالإمَامُ مِنْ بَعْدِي الَّذِي مَحَلَّهُ مِنّي مَحَلُّ هرُونَ مِنْ مُوسَى إلَّا أَنَّهُ لَا نَبِيَّ بَعْدِي وَهُوَ وَلِيُّكُمْ بَعْدِ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَقَدْ أنْزَلَ اللهُ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالى عَلَيَّ بِذلِكَ آيَةً مِنْ كِتَابِهِ إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَهُمْ راكِعُونَ ** (O assembly of people!) I have not fallen short in communicating what He revealed, and I am explaining to you the reason for this verse. Gabriel descended to me repeatedly, commanding me on behalf of my Lord, the Peace (السَّلامِ), and He is the Peace (السَّلامَ), that I should stand in this gathering (الْمَشْهَدِ) and inform every white and black person that Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) is my brother, my successor (وَصِيّي), my Caliph (خَلِيفَتي), and the Imam (الإمَامُ) after me, whose position to me is the position of Aaron to Moses, except that there is no Prophet after me. He is your Wali (وَلِيُّكُمْ) after Allah and His Messenger. And Allah, Blessed and Exalted, has revealed a verse concerning this in His Book: Your guardian is only Allah, His Messenger, and the faithful who maintain the prayer and give the zakat while bowing down. And Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) maintained the Prayer (الصَّلوةَ) and gave the Zakat (الزَّكْوَة) while bowing (رَاكِعٌ), seeking Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in every state. I asked Gabriel (peace be upon him) to intercede for me to be excused from communicating this to you, O people, due to my knowledge of the scarcity of the pious, the multitude of the hypocrites (المُنَافِقِينَ), the deceit (أدغَالِ) (1) of the sinners (اْلآثِمينَ), and the trickery (خَتَل) (2) of those who mock Islam, whom Allah the Almighty described in His Book as those who say with their mouths what is not in their hearts, and you suppose it to be a light matter, while it is a grave matter with Allah. And the frequency of their harming me more than once, until they called me an ear (أُذُناً) and claimed that I was such, due to his constant adherence to me and my turning toward him. Until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed concerning that: And among them are those who abuse the Prophet and say, 'He is all ear.' Say: 'An ear against those who claim that He is an ear of good for you...' the verse. If I wished to name them by their names, I would name them. If I wished to point to them by their persons, I would point. And if I wished to guide to them, I would guide. But by Allah, I have been generous (تَكَرَّمْتُ) regarding their affairs. Yet, Allah is not pleased with me in all of this unless I communicate what was revealed to me. Then He recited: O Messenger! Communicate what has been sent down to you from your Lord concerning Ali, And if you do not, you have not communicated His message, and Allah shall protect you from the people. فاعْلَمُوا مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ أَنَّ اللهَ قَدْ نَصَبَهُ لَكُمْ وَلِيّاً وَإمَاماً مُفْتَرَضاً طَاعَتُهُ عَلَى الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَاْلأنْصَارِ وَعَلَى التَّابِعِينَ لَهُمْ بِإحْسَانٍ وَعَلَى البَادِي وَالحَاضِرِ وَعَلَى الأَعْجَمِيّ وَالْعَرَبِيّ والْحُرِّ وَالمَمْلُوكِ والصَّغِيرِ وَالكَبِيرِ وَعَلَى اْلأَبْيَضِ وَاْلأَسْوَدِ وَعَلَى كلِّ مُوَحِّدٍ مَاضٍ حُكْمُهُ جَايِزٌ قَوْلُهُ نَافِذٌ أَمْرُهُ مَلْعُونٌ مَنْ خَالَفَهُ مَرْحُومٌ مَنْ تَبِعَهُ وَمَنْ صَدَّقَهُ فَقَدْ غَفَرَ اللهُ لَهُ وَلِمَنْ سَمِعَ مِنْهُ وَأطَاعَ لَهُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنَّهُ آخِرُ مَقامٍ أقُومُهُ فيِ هذا الْمَشْهَدِ فَاسْمَعُوا وَأطِيعُوا وَانْقادُوا لِأَمْرِ رَبِّكُمْ فَإنَّ اللهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ هُوَ رَبُّكُمْ وَوَلِيُّكُمْ وَإلهكُمْ ثُمَّ مِنْ دُونِهِ رَسُولُهُ مُحَمَّدٌ صَلَيّ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَم وَلِيُّكُمْ الْقَآئِمُ الْمُخَاطِبُ لَكُمْ ثُمَّ مِنْ بَعْدي عَليُّ صَلَواتُ اللهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ وَلِيُّكُمْ وَإمَامُكُمْ بِأَمْرِ اللهِ رَبِّكُمْ ثُمَّ اْلإِمَامَةُ فيِ ذُرِّيَتِي مِنْ وُلْدِهِ إلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ لَا حَلَالَ إلَا مَا أَحَلَّهُ اللهُ وَلَا حَرَامَ إلَّا مَا حَرَّمَهُ اللهُ عَرَّفَنِي الحَلَالَ والحَرَامَ وَأنَا أفْضَيْتُ (١) بِمَا عَلَّمَنِيِ رَبّي مِنْ كِتَابِهِ وَحَلَالِهِ وَحَرَامِهِ إلَيْهِ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ مَا مِنْ عِلْمٍ إلَّا وَقَدْ أحْصَاهُ اللهُ فيِ كُلّ عِلْمٍ عَلِمْتُهُ فَقَدْ أحْصَيْتُهُ فيِ عَليِّ إمَامِ الْمُتَّقِينَ وَمَا مِنْ عِلْمٍ إلَّا وَقَدْ عَلَّمْتُهُ عَلِيَّاً وَهُوَ الإمَامُ الْمُبِينَ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ لَا تَضِلُّوا عَنْهُ وَلَا تَنْفِرُوا مِنْهُ وَلَا تَسْتنْكفُوا مِنْ وِلَايَتِهِ فَهُوَ الَّذِي يَهْدِي إلىَ الْحَقِّ وَيَعْمَلُ بِهِ وَيزْهَقُ الْبَاطِلَ وَيَنْهَى عَنْهُ وَلَا تَأَخُذُهُ في اللهِ لَوْمَةُ لآئِمٍ ثُمَّ إِنَّهُ أَوَّلُ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالَّذِي فَدَى رَسُولَ اللهِ صَلَىّ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلّمْ بِنَفْسِهِ وَالَّذِي كَانَ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللهِ صَلَىّ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلّمْ وَلَا أحَدَ يَعْبُدُ اللهَ مَعَ رَسُولِهِ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ غَيْرُهُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ فَضِّلُوهُ فَقَدْ فَضَّلَهُ اللهُ وَاقْبَلُوهُ فَقَدْ نَصَبَهُ الله مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إِنَّهُ إمَامٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَلَنْ يَتُوبَ اللهُ عَلَى أَحَدٍ أنْكَرَ وَلايَتَهُ وَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُ حَتْماً علَى اللهِ أَنْ يَفْعَلَ ذلِكَ ممَّنْ خَالَفَ أمْرَهُ فِيهِ وَأنْ يُعَذِّبَهُ عَذَاباً نُكْراً أبَدَ الآبَادِ وَدَهْرَ الدُّهُورِ فَاحْذَرُوا أنْ تُخَالِفُوهُ فَتُصْلَوْا ناراً وَقُودُهَا النَّاسُ وَالْحِجارَةُ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكافِرِينَ أَيُّهَا النَّاسِ بِي وَاللهِ بُشِّرَ الأَوَّلُونَ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ وَالْمُرْسَلِينَ وَأنَا خَاتِمُ الأنْبياء وَالْمُرْسِلِينَ وَالْحُجّةُ عَلَى جَمِيعِ الْمَخْلُوقِينَ مِنْ أَهْلِ السَّمَواتِ وَاْلأَرضِينَ فَمَنْ شَكَّ فيِ ذلِكَ فَهُوَ كَافِر كُفْرَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةَ اْلأُولى وَمَنْ شَكَّ فيِ شَيْءٍ مِنْ قَوْليِ هذَا فَقَدْ شَكَّ فيِ الْكُلِّ مِنْهُ وَالشَّاكُّ فيِ الْكُلِّ فَلَهُ النَّارُ مَعَاشرَ النَّاس حَبانِيَ (٢) اللهُ بِهذِهِ الْفَضِيلَةِ مَنّاً مِنْهُ عَلَيَّ وَإحْسَاناً مِنْهُ إلَيَّ وَلَا إلَهَ إلَّا هُوَ لَهُ الْحَمْدُ مِنّي أبَدَ اْلآبِدِينَ وَدَهْرَ الدَّاهِرِينَ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ ، مَعَاشرَ النَّاس فَضِّلُوا عَلِيّاً فَإنَّهُ أَفْضَلُ النَّاسِ بَعْدِي مِنْ ذَكَر وَأُنْثَى بِنَا أَنْزَلَ اللهُ الرِّزْقَ وَبَقِي الْخَلْقُ مَلْعُونٌ مَلْعُونٌ مَغْضُوبٌ مَغْضُوبٌ مَنْ رَدَّ قَوْليِ هذَا وَإنْ لَمْ يُوَافِقْهُ إلَّا إنَّ جَبْرِئِيلَ خَبَّرَنِي عَنِ اللهِ تَعَالَى بِذلِكَ وَيَقُولُ مَنْ عَادَى عَلِيّاً وَلَمْ يَتَوَلَّهُ فَعَلَيْهِ لَعْنَتي وَغَضَبِي وَلْتَنْظُرْ نَفْسٌ ما قَدَّمَتْ لِغَدٍ ** (O assembly of people!) So know, O assembly of people, that Allah has appointed him for you as a Wali (وَلِيّاً) and an Imam (إمَاماً) whose obedience is obligatory (مُفْتَرَضاً طَاعَتُهُ) upon the Emigrants (الْمُهَاجِرِينَ) and the Helpers (اْلأنْصَارِ), upon those who follow them in goodness, upon the nomad (البَادِي) and the resident (الحَاضِرِ), upon the non-Arab (الأَعْجَمِيّ) and the Arab (الْعَرَبِيّ), the free man and the slave, the young and the old, the white and the black, and upon every monotheist (مُوَحِّدٍ). His judgment is binding (مَاضٍ حُكْمُهُ), his word is permissible (جَايِزٌ قَوْلُهُ), his command is effective (نَافِذٌ أَمْرُهُ). Cursed is he who opposes him, and blessed is he who follows him. And whoever confirms him, Allah has forgiven him and whoever hears from him and obeys him. O assembly of people! This is the last standing place (مَقامٍ) where I shall stand in this gathering (الْمَشْهَدِ). So listen, obey, and submit to the command of your Lord. For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is your Lord, your Wali (وَلِيُّكُمْ), and your God. Then, besides Him, His Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is your Wali (وَلِيُّكُمْ), the one standing and addressing you. Then, after me, Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him) is your Wali (وَلِيُّكُمْ) and your Imam (إمَامُكُمْ) by the command of Allah, your Lord. Then the Imamate (اْلإِمَامَةُ) is in my progeny (ذُرِّيَتِي) from his descendants until the Day of Resurrection, the Day they meet Allah and His Messenger. There is no lawful (حَلَالَ) except what Allah has made lawful, and no unlawful (حَرَامَ) except what Allah has made unlawful. He taught me the lawful and the unlawful, and I have entrusted (أفْضَيْتُ) (1) to him what my Lord taught me from His Book, and His lawful and His unlawful. O assembly of people! There is no knowledge except that Allah has enumerated it, and every knowledge I have known, I have enumerated it in Ali, the Imam of the pious. There is no knowledge except that I have taught it to Ali, and he is the Manifest Imam (الإمَامُ الْمُبِينَ). O assembly of people! Do not go astray from him, nor flee from him, nor disdain his Wilayah (وِلَايَتِهِ). For he is the one who guides to the truth, acts upon it, destroys falsehood, and forbids it. The blame of any blamer shall not deter him in the path of Allah. Furthermore, he is the first to believe in Allah and His Messenger, the one who ransomed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with his soul, and the one who was with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and no man worshipped Allah with His Messenger other than him. O assembly of people! Prefer him, for Allah has preferred him. Accept him, for Allah has appointed him. O assembly of people! He is an Imam from Allah. Allah will never accept the repentance of anyone who denies his Wilayah (وَلايَتَهُ), and Allah will never forgive him. It is incumbent upon Allah that He shall do this to whoever opposes His command concerning him, and that He shall punish him with a terrible punishment (عَذَاباً نُكْراً) forever and throughout eternity. So beware of opposing him, lest you be cast into a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, prepared for the faithless. O people! By Allah, the first of the Prophets and Messengers gave glad tidings of me. I am the Seal of the Prophets and Messengers, and the Proof (الْحُجّةُ) over all creation, from the inhabitants of the heavens and the earths. Whoever doubts this is a disbeliever (كَافِر) with the disbelief of the first Age of Ignorance (الْجَاهِلِيَّةَ اْلأُولى). And whoever doubts anything of this saying of mine has doubted the whole of it, and the doubter of the whole shall have the Fire. O assembly of people! Allah has favored me (2) with this virtue, as a grace from Him upon me and a kindness from Him toward me. There is no god except Him. To Him belongs the praise from me forever and throughout eternity, in every state. O assembly of people! Prefer Ali, for he is the most preferred of people after me, male or female. Through us, Allah sent down the provision, and the creation remains. Cursed, cursed, wrathful, wrathful is he who rejects this saying of mine, even if he does not agree with it. Indeed, Gabriel informed me of this from Allah the Almighty, and He says: "Whoever shows enmity toward Ali and does not take him as a Wali (يَتَوَلَّهُ), upon him is My curse and My wrath." And let every soul consider what it has sent ahead for tomorrow. وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ أَنْ تُخَالِفُوهُ فَتَزِلَّ قَدَمٌ بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِهَا إِنَّ اللهَ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ إِنَّهُ جَنْبُ اللهِ نَزَلَ فيِ كِتَابِهِ يا حَسْرَتى عَلى ما فَرَّطْتُ فِي جَنْبِ اللهِ ** And fear Allah lest you oppose Him, and a foot should slip after being firm. Indeed, Allah is well aware of what you do. O assembly of people! Indeed, he is the Side of Allah (جَنْبُ اللهِ) revealed in His Book: 'Alas for me, for what I fell short of in respect of Allah's side!' مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ تَدَبَّرُوا الْقُرْانَ وَافْهَمُوا آيَاتِهِ وَانْظرُوا إلَى مُحْكَمَاتِهِ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا مُتَشابِهَهُ فَوَ اللهِ لَنْ يُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ زَوَاجِرَهُ وَلَا يُوضِحُ لَكُمْ تَفْسِيرَهُ إلَّا الَّذِي أَنَا آخِذٌ بِيَدِهِ وَمُصْعَدُهُ إلَيَّ وَشَائِلٌ (١) بِعَضُدِهِ وَمُعَلِّمُكُمْ أَنَّ مَنْ كُنْتَ مَوْلَاهُ فَهذَا عَلَيٌّ مَوْلَاهُ وَهُوَ عَليُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامْ أَخِي وَوَصيي وَمُوَالاتُهُ مِنَ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَنْزَلَهَا عَلَيَّ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنَّ عَلِيّاً وَالطَّيِّبِينَ مِنْ وُلْدِي صَلَوَاتُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ هُوَ الثِّقْلُ اْلأَصْغَرُ وَالْقُرْآنُ هُوَ الثِّقْلُ اْلأَكْبَرُ فَكُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مُنْبئٌ عَنْ صَاحِبِهِ وَمُوَافِقٌ لَهُ لَنْ يَفْتَرِقَا حَتّى يَرِدَا عَلَيَّ الْحَوْضَ أُمَنآءُ اللهِ فيِ خَلْقِهِ وَحُكَّامُهُ فيِ أَرْضِهِ أَلَّا وَقَدْ بَلَّغْتُ ألَا وَقَدْ أَدَّيْتُ ألَا وَقَدْ أَسْمَعْتُ ألَّا وَقَدْ أوْضَحْتُ ألَا وَإنَّ اللهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ وَأنَا قُلْتُ عَنِ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ألَا إنَّهُ لَيْسَ أمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ صَلَوَاتُ اللهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ غَيْرَ أخي هذَا وَلَا تَحِلُّ إمْرَةُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لأَحَدٍ غَيْرِهِ ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ بِيَدِهِ إلَى عَضُدِهِ فَرَفَعَهُ وَكَانَ مُنْذُ أوَّلِ مَا صَعَدَ رَسُولُ اللهِ صَلَىْ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلّمْ شَالَ عَلِيّاً حَتّى صَارَتْ رِجْلُهُ مَعَ رُكْبَةِ رَسُولِ اللهِ صَلّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلّمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ هذَا عَليٌّ أخي وَوَصِيّي وَوَاعي عِلْمي وَخَلِيفَتي عَلَى أُمَّتِي وَعَلى تَفْسِيِر كتَابِ اللهِ وَالدَّاعِي إِلَيْهِ وَالْعَامِلُ بِمَا يَرْضَاهُ وَالْمُحَارِبُ لأَعْدَائِهِ وَالْمُوَاليِ عَلى طَاعَتِهِ وَالنَّاهِي عَنْ مَعْصِيَتِهِ خَلِيفَةُ رَسُولِ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلْيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلّمْ وَأَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالإِمَامُ الهَادِي وَقَاتِلُ النَّاكِثِينَ وَالْقَاسِطِينَ وَالْمارِقِينَ بِأَمْرِ اللهِ أَقُولُ مَا يُبَدَّلُ القَوْلُ لدَيَّ بِأَمْرِ اللهِ رَبِّي أَقُولُ اللهُمَّ وَالِ مَنْ وَالاهُ وَعَادِ مَنْ عَادَاهُ وَالْعَنْ مَنْ أنْكَرَهُ وَاغْضَب عَلَى مَنْ جَحَدَ حَقَّهُ اللهُمَّ إنَّكَ أَنْزَلْتَ عَلَيَّ أَنَّ اْلإِمَامَةَ لِعَليِّ وَلِيِّكَ عِنْدَ تِبْياني ذلِكَ وَنَصبْي إيَّاهُ عَلَماً بِمَا أَكْمَلْتَ لِعِبَادِكَ مِنْ دِينِهِمْ وَأَتْمَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ نِعْمَتَكَ وَرَضِيتَ لَهُمُ اْلإِسْلَامَ دِيناً فَقُلْتَ وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الْإِسْلامِ دِيناً فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ وَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ ** (O assembly of people!) Reflect upon the Quran, understand its verses, look at its decisive verses (مُحْكَمَاتِهِ), and do not follow its ambiguous verses (مُتَشابِهَهُ). For by Allah, no one will explain its prohibitions (زَوَاجِرَهُ) to you, nor clarify its interpretation (تَفْسِيِرَهُ) for you, except the one whose hand I am taking, and whom I am raising up to me, and whose upper arm (عَضُدِهِ) I am lifting (1), and whom I am informing you that whoever I am his Mawla (مَوْلَاهُ), this Ali is his Mawla (مَوْلَاهُ). He is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), my brother and my successor (وَصيي). His Wilayah (مُوَالاتُهُ) is from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, Who revealed it to me. O assembly of people! Indeed, Ali and the pure ones (الطَّيِّبِينَ) from my descendants (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all) are the Lesser Weight (الثِّقْلُ اْلأَصْغَرُ), and the Quran is the Greater Weight (الثِّقْلُ اْلأَكْبَرُ). Each informs about the other and is in agreement with it. They shall never separate until they meet me at the Pool (الْحَوْضَ). They are the Trustees (أُمَنآءُ) of Allah among His creation and His Rulers (حُكَّامُهُ) on His earth. Behold! I have communicated. Behold! I have fulfilled my duty. Behold! I have made you hear. Behold! I have clarified. Behold! Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has spoken, and I have spoken on behalf of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Behold! The Commander of the Faithful (أمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ), may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him, is none other than this brother of mine, and the commandership of the believers (إمْرَةُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) is not lawful for anyone other than him. Then He struck His hand upon his upper arm and raised him. Since the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم first ascended, He had lifted Ali until his foot was level with the knee of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then He said: O assembly of people! This is Ali, my brother, my successor (وَصِيّي), the container of my knowledge (وَاعي عِلْمي), my Caliph (خَلِيفَتي) over my community and over the interpretation (تَفْسِيِر) of the Book of Allah, the caller to Him, the one who acts according to what pleases Him, the fighter against His enemies, the Wali (الْمُوَاليِ) upon His obedience, and the prohibitor of His disobedience. The Caliph of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Commander of the Faithful (أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ), and the Guiding Imam (الإِمَامُ الهَادِي), and the killer of the oath-breakers (النَّاكِثِينَ), the unjust (الْقَاسِطِينَ), and the renegades (الْمارِقِينَ) by the command of Allah. I speak what the word shall not be changed with Me, by the command of Allah, my Lord. I say: O Allah, be a Wali (وَالِ) to him who takes him as a Wali (وَالاهُ), and be an enemy (عَادِ) to him who shows enmity toward him (عَادَاهُ). Curse him who denies him, and be wrathful toward him who rejects his right. O Allah, You revealed to me that the Imamate (اْلإِمَامَةَ) belongs to Ali, Your Wali (وَلِيِّكَ), when I clarify this and appoint him as a sign (عَلَماً), by which You have perfected the religion for Your servants, completed Your blessing upon them, and approved Islam as a religion for them. So You said: And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it shall never be accepted from him, and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers. اللهُمَّ إنِّي أُشْهِدُكَ أنّي قَدْ بَلّغْتُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنَّمَا اللهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أكْمَلَ دِينَكُمْ بِإمَامَتِهِ فَمَنْ لَمْ يَأتَمَّ بِهِ وَبِمَنْ يَقُومُ مَقَامَهُ مِنْ وُلْدِي مِنْ صُلْبِهِ إلى يَوْمِ القِيَامَةِ وَالْعَرْضِ عَلَى اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَ أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ وَفِي النَّارِ هُمْ خالِدُونَ ** لَا يُخَفِّفُ اللهُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ وَلَا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ. O Allah, I call You to witness that I have communicated. O assembly of people! Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, perfected your religion through his Imamate (إمَامَتِهِ). So whoever does not take him as an Imam, and those who stand in his place from my descendants, from his loins, until the Day of Resurrection and the presentation before Allah, the Mighty and Majestic— They are the ones whose works have failed, and in the Fire they will be immortal. Allah will not lighten the punishment for them, nor will they be granted any respite. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ هذَا عَليٌّ أَنْصَرَكُمْ لِي وَأَحَقُّكُمْ بِي وَأَقْرَبُكُمْ إليَّ وَأَعَزُّكُمْ عَلَيَّ واللهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ وَأنَا عَنْهُ رَاضِيانِ وَمَا نَزَلَتْ آيةُ رِضَىً إلَّا فِيهِ وَمَا خَاطَبَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إلَّا بَدَأَ بِهِ وَلَا نَزَلَتْ آيَةُ مَدْحٍ فيِ الْقُرْآنِ إلَّا فِيهِ وَلَا شَهِدَ اللهُ بِالْجَنَّةِ فيِ هَلْ أَتى عَلَى اْلإِنْسَانِ إلَّا لَهُ وَلَا أَنْزَلَهَا فيِ سِوَاهُ وَلا مَدَحَ بِهَا غَيْرَهُ. O assembly of people! This is Ali, the most helpful of you to me, the most entitled of you to me, the closest of you to me, and the dearest of you to me. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and I are both pleased with him. No verse of pleasure (رِضَىً) was revealed except concerning him, and Allah did not address those who believed except by beginning with him. No verse of praise (مَدْحٍ) was revealed in the Quran except concerning him. Allah did not bear witness to Paradise in Hal Ata ‘ala al-Insan (هَلْ أَتى عَلَى اْلإِنْسَانِ) except for him, nor did He reveal it concerning anyone other than him, nor did He praise anyone else with it. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ هُوَ نَاصرُ دِينِ اللهِ وَالْمُجَادِلُ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللهِ صَلَىّ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمْ وَهُوَ التَّقِيُّ النَّقِيُّ الْهادي المُهْدي نَبِيُّكُمْ خَيْرُ نَبِيِّ وَوَصِيُّكُمْ خَيْرُ وَصِيِّ وَبَنُوهُ خَيْرُ الأَوْصِياءِ. O assembly of people! He is the helper of the religion of Allah, the defender of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He is the pious (التَّقِيُّ), the pure (النَّقِيُّ), the guide (الْهادي), the guided (الْمُهْدي). Your Prophet is the best Prophet, and your successor (وَصِيُّكُمْ) is the best successor (وَصِيِّ), and his sons are the best successors (الأَوْصِياءِ). مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ ذُرِّيّةُ كُلِّ نَبِيِّ مِنْ صُلْبِهِ وَذُرِّيَتِي مِنْ صُلْبِ عَليِّ صَلَوَاتُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنَّ إبْلِيسَ أَخْرَجَ آدمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامْ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ بِالْحَسَدِ فَلَا تَحْسُدُوهُ فَتَحْبِطَ أَعْمَالَكُمْ وَتَزِلَّ أَقْدَامُكُمْ فَإنَّ آدَمَ أُهْبِطَ إلَى اْلأَرْضِ بِخَطيئةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ وَهُوَ صَفْوَةُ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَكَيْفَ بِكُمْ وَأَنْتُمْ أَنْتُمْ وَمِنْكُمْ أَعْدَاءُ اللهِ ألَّا إنَّهُ لَا يُبْعِضُ عَلِيّاً إلَّا شَقِيٌّ وَلَا يَتَوَلَّى عَلِيّاً إلَّا تَقِيٌّ وَلا يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ إلَّا مُؤْمِنٌ مُخْلِصٌ وَفِي عَليِ وَاللهِ أُنْزِلَ سُوَرَةُ الْعَصْرِ بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ وَالْعَصْرِ ** (O assembly of people!) The progeny (ذُرِّيّةُ) of every Prophet is from his own loins, but my progeny (ذُرِّيَتِي) is from the loins of Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all). O assembly of people! Indeed, Iblis expelled Adam (peace be upon him) from Paradise due to envy (الْحَسَدِ). So do not envy him, lest your deeds fail and your feet slip. For Adam was sent down to earth for a single mistake, and he was the chosen one (صَفْوَةُ) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. So how will it be for you, when you are who you are, and among you are the enemies of Allah? Behold! No one hates Ali except the wretched (شَقِيٌّ), and no one takes Ali as a Wali (يَتَوَلَّى) except the pious (تَقِيٌّ), and no one believes in him except a sincere believer (مُؤْمِنٌ مُخْلِصٌ). And concerning Ali, by Allah, Surah al-Asr was revealed: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. By the afternoon, to the end of it. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ قَدِ اسْتَشْهَدْتُ اللهَ وَبَلَّغْتُكُمْ رِسَالَتِي وَما عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ اتَّقُوا اللهَ حَقَّ تُقاتِهِ وَلا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ آمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالنُّورَ الَّذِي أُنْزِلَ مَعَهُ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ نَطْمِسَ وُجُوهاً فَنَرُدَّها عَلى أَدْبارِها ** (O assembly of people!) I have called Allah to witness and I have communicated my message to you, and the Messenger is responsible only for a manifest communication. O assembly of people! Fear Allah as is His due, and do not die except as Muslims. O assembly of people! Believe in Allah and His Messenger and the Light which We have sent down with Him, before We blot out faces and turn them on their backs. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ النُّوُرُ مِنَ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فيَّ ثُمَّ مَسْلُوكٌ فيِ عَليِّ ثُمَّ فِي النَّسْلِ مِنْهُ إلى القَائِمِ الْمَهْدِيّ صَلَوَاتُ اللهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ الَّذِي يَأْخُذُ بِحَقِّ اللهِ وَبِكُلِّ حَقّ هُوَ لَنَا لأَنَّ اللهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَدْ جَعَلَنَا حُجَّةً عَلَى الْمُقُصِرِّينَ وَالْمُعَانِدِينَ وَالْمُخَالِفِينَ وَالْخَائِنِينَ وَاْلآثِمينَ وَالظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ جَمِيعِ الْعَالَمِينَ. (O assembly of people!) The Light (النُّوُرُ) is from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in me, then it is channeled (مَسْلُوكٌ) into Ali, then into the lineage from him, until the Riser (القَائِمِ), the Mahdi (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him), who will take the right of Allah and every right that belongs to us. This is because Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has made us the Proof (حُجَّةً) over the negligent (الْمُقُصِرِّينَ), the obstinate (الْمُعَانِدِينَ), the dissenters (الْمُخَالِفِينَ), the treacherous (الْخَائِنِينَ), the sinners (اْلآثِمينَ), and the wrongdoers (الظَّالِمِينَ) from all the worlds. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنّي أُنْذِرُكُمْ أَنّي رَسُولُ اللهِ إِلَيْكُمْ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْليِ الرُّسُلُ أَفَإنْ مِتُّ أَوْ قُتِلْتُ انْقَلَبْتُمْ عَلى أَعْقابِكُمْ وَمَنْ يَنْقَلِبْ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَنْ يَضُرَّ اللهَ شَيْئاً وَسَيَجْزِي اللهُ الشَّاكِرِينَ ** (O assembly of people!) I warn you that I am the Messenger of Allah to you. Messengers have passed away before me. If I die or am slain, will you turn back on your heels? And whoever turns back on his heels will not harm Allah in the least, and Allah will reward the grateful. ألا وإنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ السَّلامْ الْمَوْصُوفُ بِالصَّبْرِ وَالشُّكْرِ ثُمَّ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وُلْدي مِنْ صُلْبِهِ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ لَا تَمُنُّوا عَلىَ اللهِ تَعَالَى إسْلَامَكُمْ فَيُسْخِطَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَيُصيبَكُمْ بِعَذَابٍ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ إِنَّهُ لَبِالْمِرْصَادِ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ سَيَكُونَ مِنْ بَعْدِي أَئِمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إلَى النَّارِ وَيَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا يُنْصِرُونَ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنَّ اللهَ وَأَنَا بَرِيئانِ مِنْهُمْ. Behold! Indeed, Ali (peace be upon him) is described with patience (الصَّبْرِ) and gratitude (الشُّكْرِ), and then after him, my descendants from his loins. O assembly of people! Do not confer a favor upon Allah the Almighty with your submission (إسْلَامَكُمْ), lest He become wrathful toward you and afflict you with a punishment from Him. Indeed, He is in ambush. O assembly of people! There will be Imams after me who call to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection, they will not be helped. O assembly of people! Indeed, Allah and I are free from them. مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ إنَّهُمْ وَأَشْيَاعَهُمُ وَاتَبْاعَهُمْ وَأنْصَارَهُمْ فيِ الدَّرْكِ اْلأَسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ وَلَبِئْسَ مَثْوى الْمُتَكَبِّرينَ ألا إنَّهُمْ (١) أَصْحَابُ الصَّحيفَةِ فَلْيَنْظُرْ أَحَدُكُمْ فيِ صَحِيفَتِهِ قَالَ فَذَهَبَ عَلَى النَّاسِ الا شرذمة منهم أمر الصحيفة مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ انِّي أدَعُهَا أَمَانَةً وَوِرَاثَةً فيِ عَقِبِي إلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ وَقَدْ بَلَّغْتُ مَا أُمِرْتُ بِتَبْلِيغِهِ حُجَّةً عَلَى كُلِّ حَاضِرٍ وَغَائِبٍ وَعَلى كُلِّ أَحَدٍ مِمَّنْ شَهِدَ أَو لَمْ يَشْهَدْ وُلِد أَو لَمْ يُولَدْ فَلْيُبلِغُ الْحَاضرُ الْغَائِبَ وَالْوَالِدُ الْوَلَدَ إلى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ وَسَيَجْعَلُونَهَا مِلْكاً اغْتِصَاباً أَلا لَعنَ اللهُ الْغَاصِبِينَ وَالْمُغْتَصِبِينَ وَعِنْدَهَا سَنَفْرُغُ لَكُمْ أَيُّهَ الثَّقَلانِ يُرْسَلُ عَلَيْكُما شُواظٌ مِنْ نارٍ وَنُحاسٌ فَلا تَنْتَصِرانِ ** (O assembly of people!) Indeed, they, their followers (أَشْيَاعَهُمُ), their adherents (اتَبْاعَهُمْ), and their helpers (أنْصَارَهُمْ) will be in the lowest pit (الدَّرْكِ اْلأَسْفَلِ) of the Fire, and how evil is the abode of the arrogant. Behold! Indeed, they (1) are the Companions of the Scroll (أَصْحَابُ الصَّحيفَةِ). Let each of you look into his scroll (صَحِيفَتِهِ)." He said: The matter of the Scroll (الصحيفة) was concealed from the people, except for a small group (شرذمة) among them. O assembly of people! I leave it as a trust (أَمَانَةً) and an inheritance (وِرَاثَةً) in my progeny until the Day of Resurrection. I have communicated what I was commanded to communicate, as a Proof (حُجَّةً) against every present and absent person, and against everyone who witnessed or did not witness, who was born or was not born. Let the present inform the absent, and let the father inform the child until the Day of Resurrection. They will soon turn it into a usurped dominion (مِلْكاً اغْتِصَاباً). Behold! May Allah curse the usurpers (الْغَاصِبِينَ) and the aggressors (الْمُغْتَصِبِينَ). And at that time: We shall attend to you, O you two great weights! There shall be sent against you two, a flame of fire and smoke, and you will not be able to defend yourselves. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنَّ اللهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَمْ يَكُنْ يَذَرُكُمْ عَلَى مَا أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ حَتّى يَميزَ الْخَبِيثَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبِ وَمَا كَانَ اللهُ لِيُطْلِعَكُمْ عَلَى الْغَيْبِ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ إنَّهُ مَا مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ إلَّا وَاللهُ مُهْلِكُهَا بِتَكْذِيبِهَا وَكَذلِكَ يُهْلِكُ الْقُرَى وَهِيَ ظَالِمَةٌ كَمَا ذَكَرَ اللهُ تَعَالَى وَهُوَ إمَامِكُمْ وَوَلِيُّكُمْ وَهُوَ مَواعِيدُ اللهِ وَاللهُ يَصْدُقُ مَا وَعَدَهُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ قدْ ضَلَّ قَبْلَكُمْ أكْثَرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ وَاللهُ أَهْلَكَ اْلأَوَّلِينَ وَهُوَ مُهْلِكُ اْلآخَرِينَ. (O assembly of people!) Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, would not leave you in the state you are in until He distinguishes the wicked from the good, nor would Allah inform you of the Unseen. O assembly of people! There is no town except that Allah will destroy it due to its denial, and thus He destroys the towns while they are wrongdoers (ظَالِمَةٌ), as Allah the Almighty mentioned. And he is your Imam (إمَامِكُمْ) and your Wali (وَلِيُّكُمْ), and he is the promise of Allah, and Allah fulfills what He promises. O assembly of people! Most of the ancients before you have gone astray, and Allah destroyed the ancients, and He is the destroyer of the later ones. مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ إنَّ اللهَ قَدْ أَمَرَنِي وَنَهَاني وَقَدْ أَمَرْتُ عَلِيّاً وَنَهَيْتُهُ فَعَلِمَ اْلأَمْرَ وَالنَّهْيَ مِنْ رَبِّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَاسْمَعُوا لأَمِرِهِ تَسْلَمُوا وَأطِيعُوهُ تَهْتَدُوا وَانْتَهُوا لِنَهْيِهِ تَرْشَدْوا وَصِيرُوا إلَى مُرَادِهِ وَلَا تَتَفَرَّقَ بِكُمُ السُّبُلُ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ أَنَا صِرَاطُ اللهِ الْمُسْتَقِيمُ الَّذِي أمَرَكُمْ بِاتِّبَاعِهِ ثُمَّ عَلِيٌّ مِنْ بَعْدِي ثُمَّ وُلْدِي مِنْ صُلْبِهِ أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ ثُمَّ قَرَأَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (O assembly of people!) Indeed, Allah has commanded me and forbidden me, and I have commanded Ali and forbidden him, so he knows the command and the prohibition from his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic. So listen to his command, and you will be safe; obey him, and you will be guided; cease from what he forbids, and you will be rightly guided; and proceed toward what he desires. Let not the paths scatter you away from his path. I am the Straight Path (صِرَاطُ اللهِ الْمُسْتَقِيمُ) of Allah which He commanded you to follow. Then Ali is after me, and then my descendants from his loins, a community who guide by the truth and thereby maintain justice. Then He recited: All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds, to the end of it. And He said: "It was revealed concerning me, and it was revealed concerning them, and it is general for them, and it is specific to them. They are Allah’s friends, there shall be no fear come upon them, nor shall they grieve. Behold! Indeed, the party of Allah (حِزْبُ اللهِ) are the victorious. Behold! Indeed, the enemies of Ali are the people of dissension (الشِّقَاقِ), and they are the transgressors (الْعَادُونَ), and the brothers of the devils who inspire one another with specious talk by way of delusion. Behold! Indeed, his Awliya’ (أَوْلِيَآءَهُمْ) are the true believers, whom Allah mentioned in His Book, and He the Exalted said: You will not find a people who believe in Allah and the Last Day befriending those who oppose Allah and His Messenger, to the end of the verse. Behold! Indeed, his Awliya’ (أَوْلِيَآءَهُمُ) are those whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, described, saying: Those who have faith and do not taint their faith with wrongdoing—for them is safety, and they are the guided. Behold! Indeed, his Awliya’ (أَوْلِيَآءَهُمُ) are those who enter Paradise safely, and the angels receive them with greetings: "If you have been good, enter it to abide therein." Behold! Indeed, his Awliya’ (أَوْلِيَآءَهُمُ) are those concerning whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: They will enter Paradise, provided therein without reckoning. Behold! Indeed, his enemies are those who shall enter the Blaze (سَعِيراً). Behold! Indeed, his enemies are those who will hear the roaring of Hell while it boils, and it has a sighing sound (زَفِيرٌ). Whenever a group enters, it curses its sister... the verse. Behold! Indeed, his enemies are those concerning whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: Whenever a group is cast into it, its keepers will ask them, 'Did there not come to you a warner?' the verse. Behold! Indeed, his Awliya’ (أَوْلِيَآءَهُمُ) are those who fear their Lord in secret; there is forgiveness for them and a great reward. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ شَتَّانَ مَا بَيْنَ السَّعِيرِ وَالْجَنَّةِ عَدُوُّنَا مَنْ ذَمَّهُ اللهُ وَلَعَنَهُ ووَلِيُّنا مَن أَحَبَّهُ اللهِ وَمَدَحَهُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ ألَا وَإنِّي مُنْذِرٌ عَلِيٌّ هَادٍ مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ إنِّي نَبِيٌّ وَعَلِيٌّ وَصِيّي ألَا وَإنَّ خَاتِمَ اْلأَئِمَّةِ مِنّا الْقَآئِمُ الْمَهْدِيُّ صَلَوَاتُ اللهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ ألَا إنَّهُ الظَّاهِرُ عَلَى الدِّينِ ألَا إِنَّهُ الْمُنْتَقِمُ مِنَ الظَّالمِينَ ألَا إنَّهُ فَاتِحُ الْحُصُونِ وَهَادِمُهَا ألَا إنَّهُ قَاتِلُ كُلِّ قَبِيلَةٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ الشِّرْكِ ألَا إنَّهُ مُدْرِكُ كُلِّ ثَارٍ لأَوْلِيَاءِ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ألَا إنَّهُ نَاصِرُ دِينِ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ألَا إنَّهُ الْغَرَّافُ (١) مِنْ بَحْرٍ عَمِيقٍ ألَا إنَّهُ يَسِمُ (٢) كُلَّ ذِي فَضْلٍ فَضْلَهُ وَكُلَّ ذِي جَهْلٍ بِجَهْلِهِ ألَا إنَّهُ خِيرَةُ اللهِ ومَخُتَارُهُ ألَا إنَّهُ وَارِثُ كُلِّ عِلْمٍ وَالْمُحِيطُ بِهِ ألَا إنَّهُ الْمُخْبِرُ عَنْ رَبِّهِ عزّ وَجَلَّ الْمُنَبِّهُ بِأَمْرِ إيمَانِهِ ألَا إنَّهُ الرَّشِيدُ السَّدِيدُ ألَا إنَّهُ الْمُفَوَّضُ إلَيْهِ ألَا إنَّهَ قَدْ بُشِّرَ بِهِ مَنْ سَلَفَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ألَا إنَّهُ الْبَاقِيَ حُجَّةً وَلَا حُجَّةَ بَعْدَهُ وَلَا حَقَّ إلَّا مَعَهُ وَلَا نُورَ إلَّا عِنْدَهُ ألَا إنَّهُ لَا غَالِبَ لَهُ وَلَا مَنْصُورَ عَلَيْهِ ألَا إنَّهُ وَليُّ اللهِ فيِ أرْضِهِ وَحَكَمُهُ فِي خَلْقِهِ وَأَمِينُهُ فيِ سِرِّهِ وَعَلَانِيَتِهِ مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ قَدْ بَيَّنْتُ لَكُمْ وَأفْهَمْتُكُمْ وَهذَا عَلِىّ يُفْهِمُكُمْ بَعْدِي ألَا وَإنَّ عِنْدَ انْقِضَاءِ خُطْبَتي أَدْعُوكُمْ إلَى مُصافَقَتِي عَلَى بَيْعَتِهِ وَاْلإِقْرَار بِهِ ثُمَّ مُصَافَقَتِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِي ألَا وَإنِّي قَدْ بَايَعْتُ اللهَ وَعَلِيٌّ قَدْ بَايَعَنِي وَأنَا آخِذُكُم بِالْبِيْعَةِ لَهُ عَنِ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَ (O assembly of people!)
