How great is the difference between the Blaze (السَّعِيرِ) and Paradise! Our enemy is he whom Allah has condemned and cursed, and our Wali (وَلِيُّنا) is he whom Allah has loved and praised. O assembly of people! Behold! Indeed, I am the Warner (مُنْذِرٌ), and Ali is the Guide (هَادٍ). O assembly of people! Indeed, I am the Prophet, and Ali is my successor (وَصِيّي). Behold! Indeed, the Seal of the Imams (خَاتَمَ اْلأَئِمَّةِ) from us is the Riser (الْقَآئِمُ), the Mahdi (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him). Behold! Indeed, he is the one who will prevail over the religion. Behold! Indeed, he is the avenger against the wrongdoers (الظَّالمِينَ). Behold! Indeed, he is the opener of fortresses and their demolisher. Behold! Indeed, he is the killer of every tribe of the people of polytheism (الشِّرْكِ). Behold! Indeed, he is the seeker of every vengeance for the Awliya’ (أَوْلِيَاءِ) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Behold! Indeed, he is the helper of the religion of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Behold! Indeed, he is the one who draws water (الْغَرَّافُ) (1) from a deep sea. Behold! Indeed, he marks (يَسِمُ) (2) every possessor of virtue with his virtue, and every possessor of ignorance with his ignorance. Behold! Indeed, he is the choice of Allah and His selected one. Behold! Indeed, he is the inheritor of all knowledge and the encompasser of it. Behold! Indeed, he is the one who informs on behalf of his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, the one who alerts concerning the matter of His faith. Behold! Indeed, he is the rightly guided (الرَّشِيدُ), the upright (السَّدِيدُ). Behold! Indeed, he is the one entrusted with authority (الْمُفَوَّضُ إلَيْهِ). Behold! Indeed, those who preceded him gave glad tidings of him. Behold! Indeed, he is the remaining Proof (حُجَّةً), and there is no Proof after him, and no truth except with him, and no light except with him. Behold! Indeed, there is no vanquisher for him, nor anyone victorious over him. Behold! Indeed, he is the Wali (وَليُّ) of Allah on His earth, His ruler (حَكَمُهُ) among His creation, and His trustee (أَمِينُهُ) in His secret and His manifest affairs. O assembly of people! I have clarified for you and made you understand, and this Ali will make you understand after me. Behold! Indeed, when my sermon (خُطْبَتي) concludes, I call you to shake hands with me in allegiance (بَيْعَتِهِ) to him and affirmation of him, and then to shake hands with him after me. Behold! Indeed, I have pledged allegiance (بَايَعْتُ) to Allah, and Ali has pledged allegiance to me, and I am taking the oath of allegiance (الْبِيْعَةِ) from you for him on behalf of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. فَمَنْ نَكَثَ فَإِنَّما يَنْكُثُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ (So whoever breaks his oath, breaks it only to his own detriment.) the verse. O assembly of people! Indeed, the Hajj and al-Safa and al-Marwah وَالصَّفا وَالْمَرْوَةَ and the Umrah are among Allah’s sacraments. So whoever makes Hajj to the House or performs the Umrah, مِنْ شَعائِرِ اللهِ فَمَنْ حَجَّ الْبَيْتَ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَ ** the verse. O assembly of people! Perform Hajj to the House, for no people of a household (أَهْلُ بَيْتٍ) have approached it except that they became rich, and none have stayed away from it except that they became poor. O assembly of people! No believer stands at the standing place (بِالْمُوْقِفِ) except that Allah forgives him what has passed of his sin up to that time. When his Hajj is completed, his deeds are renewed. O assembly of people! The pilgrims (الحُجّاج) are assisted, and their expenses are replaced, and Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous. مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ حِجُّوا الْبَيْتَ بِكَمَالِ الدِّينِ وَالتَّفَقُّهِ وَلَا تَنْصَرِفُوا عَنِ الْمَشَاهِدِ إلَّا بِتَوْبَةٍ وَاقْلَاعٍ مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ أَقِيمُوا الصَّلَوةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكْوَةَ كَمَا أَمَرَكُمْ اللهُ تَعَالَى لَئِن طَالَ عَلَيْكُمُ اْلأَمَدُ فَقَصُرْتُمْ أَوْ نَسِيتُمْ فَعَلِيٌّ وَلِيٌّكُمْ وَمُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ الَّذِي نَصَبَهُ اللهُ عَزّ وَجَلَّ بَعْدِي وَمَنْ خَلَفَهُ اللهُ مِنِّي وَمِنْهُ يُخْبِرُكُمْ بِمَا تَسْألُونَ عَنْهُ وَيُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ مَا لا تَعْلَمُونَ ألَا إنَّ الْحَلَالَ وَالْحَرَامَ أَكْثَرُ مِنْ أَنْ أُحْصِيهُمَا وَأُعَرِّفَهُمَا فَآمُر بالْحَلَال وَأنْهَى عَنْ الْحَرَام في مَقَامٍ (٣) وَاحِدٍ فَأمَرْتُ أَنْ آخُذَ الْبَيْعَةَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَالصَّفْقَةَ لَكُمْ بِقُبُولِ مَا جِئْتُ بِهِ عَنِ اللهِ تَعَالَى في عَليّ أمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ وَاْلأَئِمَّةَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ صَلَواتُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ مِنِّي وَمِنْهُ أُمَّةٌ قَائِمَةٌ وَمِنْهُمُ الْمَهْدِيّ صَلَوَاتُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ إلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ الَّذِي يَقْضِي بالْحَقِّ مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ وَكُلُّ حَلَالٍ دَلَلْتُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَكُلُّ حَرَامٍ نَهَيْتُكُمْ عَنْهُ فَإِنِّي لَمْ أَرْجعْ عَنْ ذلِكَ وَلَمْ أُبدّل ألا فاذْكُرُوا ذلِكَ (١) وَاحْفَظُوهُ وَتَواصَوْا به وَلَا تُبَدِّلُوهُ وَلَا تُغَيِّرُوهُ ألَا وإِنّي أُجَدِّدُ الْقَوْلَ ألَا فَأقِيمُوا الصَلوةَ وَآتوا الزَّكوة وأمُرُوا بالْمَعْرُوفِ وَانْهَوْا عَن الْمُنْكَر ألَا وإِنَّ رأسَ الأَمْر بالْمَعْرُوفِ أن تَنْتَهُوا إلَى قَوْليِ وَتْبَلِّغُوهُ مَنْ لَمْ يَحْضُرْهُ وَتَأمُرُوهُ بِقَبُولِهِ وَتَنْهَوْهُ عَنْ مُخَالَفَتِهِ فَإنَّهُ أَمْرٌ مِنَ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ وَمِنِّي وَلَا أَمْرَ بِمَعْرُوفٍ وَلَا نَهْيٌ عَنْ مُنْكَرٍ إلَّا مَعَ إمَامٍ مَعْصُومٍ مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ الْقُرْآنُ يُعَرِّفُكُمْ أَنَّ اْلأَئِمَّةَ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وُلْدُهُ وَعَرَّفْتُكُمْ أَنَّهُمْ مِنِّي وَمِنْهُ حَيْثُ يَقُولُ اللهُ وَجَعَلَها كَلِمَةً باقِيَةً فِي عَقِبِهِ وَقُلْتُ لَنْ تَضِلُّوا مَا إنْ تَمَسَّكْتُمْ بِهِمَا مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ التَّقْوَى التَّقْوَى احْذَرُوا السَّاعَةَ كَمَا قَالَ اللهُ تَعَالَى إِنَّ زَلْزَلَةَ السَّاعَةِ شَيْءٌ عَظِيمٌ أُذْكُرُوا الْمَمَاتَ وَالْحِسَابَ وَالْمَوازِينَ وَالْمُحَاسَبَةَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ وَالثّوابَ وَالْعِقَابَ فَمَنْ جَاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ أُثِيبَ وَمَنْ جَاءَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ فيِ الْجِنَانِ نَصِيبٌ. (O assembly of people!) Perform Hajj to the House with the perfection of religion and understanding (التَّفَقُّهِ), and do not depart from these sacred sites (الْمَشَاهِدِ) except with repentance and cessation (of sin). O assembly of people! Establish the Prayer (الصَّلَوةَ) and give the Zakat (الزَّكْوَةَ) as Allah the Almighty commanded you. If the time (اْلأَمَدُ) becomes long for you, and you fall short or forget, then Ali is your Wali (وَلِيٌّكُمْ) and the explainer (مُبَيِّنُ) for you, whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, appointed after me, and whom Allah made my successor and his successor. He will inform you of what you ask about and clarify for you what you do not know. Behold! Indeed, the lawful (الْحَلَالَ) and the unlawful (الْحَرَامَ) are too numerous for me to enumerate and define them in a single standing place (3). So I was commanded to take the oath of allegiance (الْبَيْعَةَ) from you and the pledge (الصَّفْقَةَ) for you, by accepting what I brought from Allah the Almighty concerning Ali, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and the Imams after him (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all), who are from me and from him—a standing community (أُمَّةٌ قَائِمَةٌ), and among them is the Mahdi (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) until the Day of Resurrection, who judges with the truth. O assembly of people! Every lawful thing I guided you to, and every unlawful thing I forbade you from, I have not retracted from that, nor have I changed it. Behold! So remember that (1), preserve it, enjoin it upon one another, and do not change it or alter it. Behold! Indeed, I renew the saying: Behold! So establish the Prayer (الصَلوةَ), give the Zakat (الزَّكوة), enjoin the right (الْمَعْرُوفِ), and forbid the wrong (الْمُنْكَر). Behold! Indeed, the head of enjoining the right is that you adhere to my saying, communicate it to those who are absent, command them to accept it, and forbid them from opposing it. For it is a command from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and from me. There is no enjoining of the right nor forbidding of the wrong except with an infallible Imam (إمَامٍ مَعْصُومٍ). O assembly of people! The Quran informs you that the Imams (peace be upon them) after him are his descendants, and I have informed you that they are from me and from him, where Allah says: And He made it an enduring word in his posterity. And I said: You will never go astray as long as you hold fast to both of them. O assembly of people! Piety (التَّقْوَى), piety! Beware of the Hour, as Allah the Almighty said: Indeed, the earthquake of the Hour is a tremendous thing. Remember death, the reckoning (الْحِسَابَ), the scales (الْمَوازِينَ), and the accounting before the Lord of the Worlds, and the reward (الثّوابَ) and the punishment (الْعِقَابَ). Whoever comes with a good deed shall be rewarded, and whoever comes with an evil deed shall have no share in the Gardens (الْجِنَانِ). مَعَاشرَ النَّاسِ إنَّكُمْ أكْثَرُ مِنْ أن تُصافِقُوني بِكَفٍ وَاحِدَةٍ وَأَمَرَني اللهُ أَنْ آخُذَ مِنْ الْسِنَتِكُمْ اْلإقْرَارَ بِمَا عَقَدْتُ لِعَليّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ مِنْ إمْرَةِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَمَنْ جَاءَ بَعْدَهُ مِنَ اْلأَئِمَّةِ مِنِّي وَمِنْهُ عَلَى مَا أَعْلَمْتُكُمْ أَنَّ ذُرِّيَتي مِنْ صُلْبِهِ فَقُولُوا بِأَجْمَعِكُمْ إنَّا سَامِعُونَ مُطِيعُونَ رَاضُونَ مُنْقَادُونَ لِمَا بَلَّغْتَ عَن رَبِّنَا وَرَبِّكَ فيِ أَمْرِ عَلِيّ وَأَمْرِ وُلْدِهِ مِنْ صُلْبِهِ مِنَ اْلأَئِمَّةِ نُبَايِعُكَ عَلَى ذلِكَ بِقُلُوبِنَا وَأَنْفُسِنَا وَأَلْسِنَتِنَا وَأَيْدِيَنَا عَلَى ذلِكَ نَحْيى وَنَمُوتُ وَنُبْعَثُ وَلَا نُغَيِّرُ وَلَا نُبَدِّلُ وَلَا نَشُكُّ وَلَا نَرْتَابُ وَلَا نَرْجِعُ عَنْ عَهْدٍ وَلَا نَنْقُضُ الْمِيثَاقَ وَنُطِيعُ اللهَ وَنُطِيعُكَ وَعَلِيّاً أمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَوُلْدَهُ اْلأَئِمَّةَ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ الَّذِينَ ذَكَرْتَهُمْ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِكَ مِنْ صُلْبِهِ بَعْدَ الْحَسَنِ وَالْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا السَّلَامُ الَّذِينَ قَدْ عَرَّفْتُكُمْ مَكَانَهُمَا مِنْ مِي وَمَحَلَّهُمَا عِنْدِي وَمَنْزِلَتَهُمَا مِنْ رَبِّي فَقَدْ أَدَّيْتُ ذلِكَ إلَيْكُمْ (O assembly of people!) You are too numerous for me to shake hands with you one by one. Allah has commanded me to take affirmation (اْلإقْرَارَ) from your tongues concerning what I have established for Ali (peace be upon him) of the Commandership of the Faithful (إمْرَةِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ), and those Imams who come after him, who are from me and from him, as I informed you that my progeny is from his loins. So say unanimously: "Indeed, we are hearing, obeying, pleased, and submitting to what you have communicated from our Lord and your Lord concerning the matter of Ali and the matter of his descendants from his loins, the Imams. We pledge allegiance (نُبَايِعُكَ) to you upon that with our hearts, our souls, our tongues, and our hands. Upon that we shall live, and upon that we shall die, and upon that we shall be resurrected. We shall not change, nor alter, nor doubt, nor waver, nor turn back from the covenant, nor break the solemn pledge (الْمِيثَاقَ). We obey Allah, and we obey you, and Ali, the Commander of the Faithful, and his descendants, the Imams (peace be upon them), whom you mentioned from your progeny, from his loins, after al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them both), whose position to me, their status with me, and their rank with my Lord I have already made known to you. I have thus fulfilled that to you."

And indeed, they are the Masters of the youth of Paradise, and indeed, they are the two Imams after their father, Ali, and I am their father before him. And say: 'We have obeyed Allah in this, and we have obeyed you, and Ali, and Al-Hasan, and Al-Husayn, and the Imams (may the prayers of Allah be upon them) whom you have mentioned.' This is a covenant (عَهْداً) and a solemn pledge (مِيثَاقاً) taken for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) from our hearts, our souls, our tongues, and the clasping of our hands (مُصافَقَةِ أيْدِينَا)—whoever reaches them with his hand and affirms them with his tongue. We seek no substitute for this, and we shall never see ourselves turning away from it. We call Allah to witness, and Allah suffices as a Witness, and you are a witness over us concerning it, as is everyone who obeys, whether they are manifest or concealed, and the angels of Allah, His hosts, and His servants. And Allah is greater than every witness.

O assembly of people! What do you say? For Allah knows every sound and the hidden thought (خَافِيَةَ) of every soul. So, whoever is guided, it is for his own soul, and whoever goes astray, he only goes astray against it. And whoever pledges allegiance (بَايَعَ), he only pledges allegiance to Allah; the Hand of Allah is over their hands. O assembly of people! Therefore, be wary of Allah (فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ) and pledge allegiance to Ali (may the prayers of Allah be upon him), the Commander of the Faithful, and Al-Hasan, and Al-Husayn, and the Imams (peace be upon them)—an enduring word (كَلِمَةً بَاقِيَةً). Allah destroys whoever betrays, and Allah shows mercy to whoever fulfills [the pledge]. فَمَنْ نَكَثَ فَإِنَّما يَنْكُثُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ (48:10) So whoever breaks his pledge, breaks it only to his own detriment. the Ayah.

O assembly of people! Say what I have told you, and greet Ali (upon him be the prayer and peace) with the title of Commander of the Faithful, and say: سَمِعْنا وَأَطَعْنا غُفْرانَكَ رَبَّنا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ (We hear and we obey. Your forgiveness, our Lord, and toward You is the ultimate return). And say: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي هَدانا لِهذا وَما كُنَّا لِنَهْتَدِيَ لَوْ لا أَنْ هَدانَا اللهُ (7:43) All praise belongs to Allah, who guided us to this. Had not Allah guided us, we would have never been guided. O assembly of people! Indeed, the merits (فَضَائِلَ) of Ali ibn Abi Talib in the sight of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, which He has revealed concerning Ali in the Quran, are too numerous for me to enumerate in a single place. So, whoever informs you of them and makes them known, believe him. O assembly of people! Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger and Ali and the Imams whom you have mentioned, he has indeed achieved a great triumph (فَوْزاً عَظِيماً). O assembly of people! Those who hasten to pledge allegiance to him (مُبَايَعَتِهِ), and to his guardianship (مُوَالاتِهِ), and to greet him with the title of Commander of the Faithful—those are the triumphant ones in the Gardens of Bliss (جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ). O assembly of people! Say the word with which Allah is pleased with you. For if you and everyone on earth disbelieve, it will not harm Allah in the least. O Allah, forgive the believing men and the believing women, and be wrathful toward the disbelievers. And all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

Then the people called out to Him: "Yes, we hear and we obey the command of Allah and the command of His Messenger with our hearts, our tongues, and our hands."

And they crowded (1) upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and upon Ali, and they clasped hands (2) with them. The first to clasp hands with the Messenger of Allah were the First, the Second, the Third, the Fourth, and the Fifth, and the rest of the Muhajirin and the Ansar, and the rest of the people, down to the last of them, according to their ranks and the measure of their stations, until the Isha and Atamah prayers were performed at one time. They continued the pledge (البيعة) and the clasping of hands (المصافقة) three times, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would say whenever a group pledged allegiance: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي فَضَّلَنَا عَلَى جَمِيعِ الْعالَمينَ (All praise belongs to Allah Who has favored us over all the worlds). And the clasping of hands (المصافقة) became a tradition (سنّة) and a custom (رسماً) used by those who had no right to it.

And Al-Qummi said: This Ayah was revealed upon the return of the Messenger of Allah from the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجّة الوداع). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم performed the Farewell Pilgrimage at the completion of ten years since his arrival in Medina. Among what He said in His sermon at Mina was that He praised Allah and lauded Him, then He said:

"O people! Listen to my words and understand them from me, for I do not know, perhaps I will not meet you after this year of mine." Then He said: "Do you know which day has the greatest sanctity (حرمة)?" The people said: "This day." He said: "Then which month?" The people said: "This month." He said: "And which land has the greatest sanctity?" They said: "This land of ours." He said: "Indeed, your blood, your wealth, and your honor are sacred to you, like the sanctity of this day of yours, in this month of yours, in this land of yours, until the Day you meet your Lord, and He questions you about your deeds. Behold, have I conveyed [the message], O people?" They said: "Yes." He said: "O Allah, bear witness." Then He said: "Behold, every custom (مأثرة) (3) or innovation (بدع) that existed in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية), or blood, or wealth, is placed beneath (1) these two feet of mine. None of you is more noble than another except by piety (التّقوى). Behold, have I conveyed [the message]?" They said: "Yes." He said: "O Allah, bear witness." Then He said: "Behold, every usury (رباً) (2) that existed in the Age of Ignorance is abolished, and the first usury to be abolished is the usury of Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib. Behold, every blood-feud (دمٍ) that existed in the Age of Ignorance is abolished, and the first blood-feud to be abolished is the blood of Rabi’ah. Behold, have I conveyed [the message]?" They said: "Yes." He said: "O Allah, bear witness." Then He said: "Behold, Satan has despaired of being worshipped in this land of yours, but he is content with what you deem insignificant of your deeds. Behold, when he is obeyed, he has been worshipped. Behold, O people! Indeed, a Muslim is truly the brother of a Muslim, and it is not lawful for a Muslim man to [shed] the blood or [take] the wealth of another Muslim man, except what he gives willingly from his own soul. And I have been commanded to fight the people until they say: 'There is no god but Allah.' When they say it, they have protected their blood and their wealth from me, except by its right, and their reckoning is with Allah. Behold, have I conveyed [the message], O people?" They said: "Yes." He said: "O Allah, bear witness." Then He said: "O people! Preserve my saying, and you will benefit from it after me; understand it, and you will be revived (3). Behold, do not return after me as disbelievers, striking the necks of one another with the sword over worldly matters. If you do that—and you surely will—you will find me in a squadron (كتيبة) (4) between Gabriel and Michael, striking your faces with the sword."

Then He turned to His right and remained silent for an hour, then He said: "If Allah wills, or Ali ibn Abi Talib." Then He said: "Behold, I have left among you two matters (أمرين): if you hold fast to them, you will never go astray: the Book of Allah and my progeny (عترتي), the People of my House (may the prayers of Allah be upon them). For the All-Subtle, the All-Aware (اللَّطِيفُ الْخَبِيرُ) has informed me that they will never separate until they meet me at the Pool (الحوض). Behold, whoever holds fast to them has been saved, and whoever opposes them has perished. Behold, have I conveyed [the message]?" They said: "Yes." He said: "O Allah, bear witness." Then He said: "Behold, men from among you will come to the Pool, and they will be recognized, but they will be driven away from me. So I will say: 'My Lord, my companions!' And it will be said: 'O Muhammad, they introduced innovations (أحدثوا) after you and changed your tradition (سنّتك).' So I will say: 'Away with them (سحقاً) (1), away with them!'"

When it was the last day of the Days of Tashriq, Allah the Almighty revealed: إِذا جاءَ نَصْرُ اللهِ وَالْفَتْحُ (110:1) When the help of Allah and the victory come, The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "My death (نُعيتُ) (2) has been announced to me." Then He called for the congregational prayer (الصّلوة جامعة) in the Mosque of Al-Khayf. The people gathered, and He praised Allah and lauded Him, then He said: "May Allah illuminate (3) the man who hears my speech, comprehends it, and conveys it to one who has not heard it. For perhaps a carrier of jurisprudence (فقه) is not a jurist (فقيه), and perhaps a carrier of jurisprudence conveys it to one who is more knowledgeable than him. Three things concerning which the heart of a Muslim man shall not harbor malice (لا يغلّ عليهنّ): Sincerity of action for Allah, sincere advice (النّصيحة) (4) to the Imams of the Muslims, and adherence to their community (جماعتهم), for their call (5) encompasses them from behind. The believers are brothers; their blood is equal (تكافي دماءهم); the lowest among them strives for their protection (يسعى بذمتهم); and they are a single hand against those besides them. O people! I am leaving among you the Two Weighty Things (الثقلين)."

They said: "O Messenger of Allah, what are the Two Weighty Things?" He said: "The Book of Allah and my progeny (عترتي), the People of my House. For the All-Subtle, the All-Aware (اللَّطِيفُ الْخَبِيرُ) has informed me that they will never separate until they meet me at the Pool, like these two fingers of mine"—and He joined His two index fingers—"and I do not say like these two"—and He joined His index finger and middle finger, so that one is superior to the other. A group of His companions gathered and said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم intends to place the Imamate in his Household." So, four men from among them went out to Mecca, entered the Kaaba, made a covenant (تعاهدوا), and entered into an agreement (تعاقدوا), and wrote a document among themselves that if Allah caused Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to die or killed him, they would never return this matter [of authority] to his Household. So, Allah revealed to His Prophet concerning that: أَمْ أَبْرَمُوا أَمْراً فَإِنَّا مُبْرِمُونَ أَمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّا لا نَسْمَعُ سِرَّهُمْ وَنَجْواهُمْ بَلى وَرُسُلُنا لَدَيْهِمْ يَكْتُبُونَ (43:79) Have they settled upon some matter? We too are settling [Our matter]. (43:80) Do they suppose that We do not hear their secret [talk] and their secret counsels? Yes indeed, and Our messengers are with them, writing down.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then departed from Mecca heading toward Medina until He stopped at a place called Ghadir Khumm. He had already taught the people their rituals (مناسكهم) and entrusted (1) His will to them, when Allah revealed this Ayah to Him: يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ (5:67) O Messenger! Communicate that which has been sent down to you from your Lord. the Ayah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said that it was a threat (تهديد) and a warning (وعيد). He praised Allah and lauded Him, then He said: "O people! Do you know who your Guardian (وليّكم) is?" They said: "Yes, Allah and His Messenger." He said: "Do you not know that I am more entitled to you than you are to yourselves?" They said: "Yes, indeed (بلى)." He said: "O Allah, bear witness." He repeated this three times, saying the same thing each time, and the people responded likewise, and He said: "O Allah, bear witness." Then He took the hand of the Commander of the Faithful (may the prayers and peace of Allah be upon him) and raised it until the whiteness of His armpits became visible to the people. Then He said: "Behold, whoever I am his Mawla (مولاه), this Ali is his Mawla. O Allah, befriend whoever befriends Him, and be hostile to whoever is hostile to Him, and help whoever helps Him, and forsake whoever forsakes Him, and love whoever loves Him." Then He said: "O Allah, bear witness over them, and I am among the witnesses." Umar then sought clarification among his companions and said: "O Messenger of Allah, is this from Allah or from His Messenger?"

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Yes, this is from Allah and from His Messenger. Indeed, He is the Commander of the Faithful, the Imam of the Pious (المتّقين), and the Leader of the Bright-faced (الغُرِّ المحجّلين). Allah will seat Him on the Bridge (الصّراط) on the Day of Resurrection, so He will admit His allies to Paradise and His enemies to the Fire." His companions who apostatized after Him said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said what He said in the Mosque of Al-Khayf, and He said what He said here, and if He returns to Medina, He will compel us to pledge allegiance to Him." So, fourteen individuals gathered and conspired (توامروا) to kill the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. They lay in wait for Him at the mountain pass (العقبة), which is the pass of Harsha (2), between Juhfah and Al-Iwa'. Seven of them sat on the right side of the pass and seven on the left side, intending to startle the she-camel of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. When night fell upon Him, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم advanced ahead of the army that night, approaching while dozing on His she-camel. When He drew near the pass, Gabriel called out to Him: "O Muhammad, so-and-so have lain in wait for you." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked and said: "Who is this behind me?" Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman said: "I am Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Did you hear what I heard?" He said: "Yes, indeed." He said: "Then keep it secret." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم approached them and called them by their names. When they heard the call of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, they fled and entered among the masses of people. They had tethered their riding beasts (رواحلهم) but left them. The people caught up with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and sought them out. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم reached their riding beasts and recognized them. When He dismounted, He said: "What is the matter with a people who swore an alliance in the Kaaba that if Allah caused Muhammad to die or killed him, they would never return this matter [of authority] to the Household of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم?" They swore by Allah that they had not said anything of that, nor intended it, nor plotted anything against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So, Allah revealed: يَحْلِفُونَ بِاللهِ ما قالُوا (They swear by Allah that they did not say [it]) that they would not return this matter [of authority] to the Household of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. وَلَقَدْ قالُوا كَلِمَةَ الْكُفْرِ وَكَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِسْلامِهِمْ وَهَمُّوا بِما لَمْ يَنالُوا (9:74) Certainly they said the word of unfaith, and became faithless after their submission, and they contemplated what they could not attain, —meaning the killing of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم— وَما نَقَمُوا إِلَّا أَنْ أَغْناهُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ فَإِنْ يَتُوبُوا يَكُ خَيْراً لَهُمْ وَإِنْ يَتَوَلَّوْا يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللهُ عَذاباً أَلِيماً فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَما لَهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا نَصِيرٍ (9:74) and they were resentful only because Allah and His Apostle had enriched them out of His grace. So should they repent, it will be better for them, but should they turn away, Allah will punish them with a painful punishment in the world and the Hereafter, and they will have no guardian or helper on the earth. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned to Medina and remained there throughout Muharram and the middle of Safar, complaining of nothing, then the illness began with which He passed away.

In Al-Majma', it is narrated: When this Ayah was revealed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to the guards among His companions who were protecting Him: "Go back to your posts, for Allah has protected Me from the people."

footnote (١) يعني ان لم تنصّ بولاية عليّ فيضيع امر التّوحيد ولا يخلص ايمان الله وفي بعض القراءات الشّاذة فما بلغت رسالاته بصيغة الجمع. (1) Meaning that if you do not explicitly state (تَنُصّ) the wilāyah (وِلايَة) of Ali, the matter of tawḥīd (التَّوْحِيد) will be lost, and the faith (إِيمَان) in Allah will not be pure. In some irregular (الشَّاذَّة) readings (القِرَاءَات): فما بلغت رسالاته (in the plural form).

footnote (١) يقال بتلت الشّيء أبتله بالكسر إذا قطعته وأبنته من غيره ومنه قوله طلّقها بتة بتلة ومنه حديث رسول الله صلىّ الله عليه وآله في خبر النّص : فاتتني عزيمة من الله تعالى بتلة أوعدني ان لم أبلّغ ان يعذّبني. (1) It is said: بتلت الشّيء أبتله (with a kasrah on the tā’) when you cut something and separate it from something else. From this is the saying: "He divorced her بتة بتلة (irrevocably)." And from this is the ḥadīth (حَدِيث) of the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله in the report of the explicit designation (النَّصّ): "A definitive (بَتْلَة) decree (عَزِيمَة) from Allah the Almighty reached me, wherein He promised to punish me if I did not convey [it]."

footnote (٢) قال الفيروزآباديّ **فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ **أي شقّ جماعاتهم بالتّوحيد أو أجبر بالقرآن أو أظهر أو احكم بالحقّ وافصِل بالأمر أو اقصد بما تؤمر أو أفرق به بين الحق والباطل وصدعه كمنعه شقّه أو شقّه نصفين أو شقّه ولم يفترق وفلاناً قصده لكرمه وبالحقّ تكلّم به جهاراً وبالأمر اصاب به موضعه وجاهر به انتهى. (2) Al-Fayrūzābādī (الفيروزآباديّ) said regarding فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ (So proclaim what you have been commanded): Meaning, split their groups (جَمَاعَاتِهِمْ) with tawḥīd (التَّوْحِيد), or compel [them] with the Quran, or manifest [it], or judge with the truth (الحَقّ), and decide the matter (الأَمْر), or intend what you are commanded, or distinguish with it between truth and falsehood (البَاطِل). And صدعه (ṣadaʿahu), like منعه (manaʿahu), means he split it, or split it in half, or split it without separating it. And [he meant] a person (فُلَاناً) when he intended him due to his generosity (كَرَمِهِ). And بالحقّ (bil-ḥaqq) means he spoke it openly (جِهَاراً). And بالأمر (bil-amr) means he hit its mark (مَوْضِعَهُ) with it and declared it openly (جَاهَرَ بِهِ). [End of quote].

footnote (١) قال الفيروزآباديّ **فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ **أي شقّ جماعاتهم بالتّوحيد أو أجبر بالقرآن أو أظهر أو احكم بالحقّ وافصِل بالأمر أو اقصد بما تؤمر أو أفرق به بين الحق والباطل وصدعه كمنعه شقّه أو شقّه نصفين أو شقّه ولم يفترق وفلاناً قصده لكرمه وبالحقّ تكلّم به جهاراً وبالأمر اصاب به موضعه وجاهر به انتهى. (1) Al-Fayrūzābādī (الفيروزآباديّ) said regarding فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ (So proclaim what you have been commanded): Meaning, split their groups (جَمَاعَاتِهِمْ) with tawḥīd (التَّوْحِيد), or compel [them] with the Quran, or manifest [it], or judge with the truth (الحَقّ), and decide the matter (الأَمْر), or intend what you are commanded, or distinguish with it between truth and falsehood (البَاطِل). And صدعه (ṣadaʿahu), like منعه (manaʿahu), means he split it, or split it in half, or split it without separating it. And [he meant] a person (فُلَاناً) when he intended him due to his generosity (كَرَمِهِ). And بالحقّ (bil-ḥaqq) means he spoke it openly (جِهَاراً). And بالأمر (bil-amr) means he hit its mark (مَوْضِعَهُ) with it and declared it openly (جَاهَرَ بِهِ). [End of quote].

footnote (١) فاعهد عهدك أي أوص وصيَّتك واستعمال العهد في الوصيّة والعكس فوق حدّ الإحصاء في الآيات والأخبار وغيرهما كقوله تعالى **وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنا إِلى آدَمَ وَعَهِدْنا إِلى إِبْراهِيمَ **وغير ذلك. (1) فاعهد عهدك (fa’had ‘ahdaka) means: make your bequest (وَصِيَّتَكَ). The usage of al-ʿahd (العَهْد) for bequest (الوَصِيَّة) and vice versa is beyond counting in the verses (الآيَات), narrations (الأَخْبَار), and other sources, such as His Almighty saying: وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنا إِلى آدَمَ (Certainly We had covenanted with Adam) وَعَهِدْنا إِلى إِبْراهِيمَ (and We covenanted with Abraham) and other instances.

footnote (١) البغض بالضمّ ضد الحبّ والبغضة بالكسر والبغضاء شدّته. (1) Al-bughḍ (البُغْض) with a ḍammah (الضَّمّ) is the opposite of love (الحُبّ). Al-bughḍah (البُغْضَة) with a kasrah (الكَسْر) and al-baghḍāʾ (البَغْضَاء) denote its intensity.

footnote (٢) الخيف ما انحدر من غلظ الجبل وارتفع عن مسيل الماء ومنه سمّى مسجد الخيف بمنى لأنّه بني في خيف الجبل والأصل مسجد خيف منى فخفّف بالحذف. (2) Al-Khayf (الخَيْف) is what slopes down from the ruggedness (غِلَظ) of the mountain and rises above the watercourse (مَسِيل المَاء). From this, the Mosque of al-Khayf (مَسْجِد الخَيْف) in Mina (مِنَى) was named, because it was built in the khayf (خَيْف) of the mountain. The original [name] was مسجد خيف منى (Mosque of the Khayf of Mina), but it was abbreviated by omission (بِالحَذْف).

footnote (٣) نهره وانتهره أي زبره وزجره. (3) Naharahū (نَهَرَهُ) and intaharahu (انْتَهَرَهُ) mean he rebuked him (زَبَرَهُ) and deterred him (زَجَرَهُ).

footnote (٤) الجحفة بضم الجيم هي مكان بين مكّة والمدينة محاذية لذي الحليفة من الجانب الشّامي قريب من رابغ بين بدر وخليص. (4) Al-Juḥfah (الجُحْفَة) with a ḍammah (الضَّمّ) on the jīm (الجِيم) is a place between Mecca (مَكَّة) and Medina (المَدِينَة), parallel to Dhū al-Ḥulayfah (ذِي الحُلَيْفَة) on the northern (الشَّامِيّ) side, near Rābigh (رَابِغ), between Badr (بَدْر) and Khulays (خُلَيْص).

footnote (٥) السّلمة كفرحة الحجارة ج ككتاب. (5) As-Salamah (السَّلَمَة), like fariḥah (فَرِحَة), means stones (الحِجَارَة). Its plural (ج) is like kitāb (كِتَاب).

footnote (٦) قمّ البيت قمّاً من باب قتل كنسه قوله تعالى **وَحَشَرْناهُمْ فَلَمْ نُغادِرْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَداً **أى لم نبق منهم أحداً ومنه سمّى الغدير لأنّه ماء يغادره السّيول أي تخلّفه فعيل بمعنى مفعول أو فعيل بمعنى فاعل لأنّه يغدر بأهله أي ينقطع عند شدّة الحاجة اليه ومنع الدّعاء اللهم من نعمك وهي أجلّ من أن تغادر أي تنقطع وغدير خم موضع بالجُحفة شديد الوباء قال الأصمعي لم يولد بغدير خم أحد فعاش الى أن يحتلم الّا أن ينجو منه ويوم الغدير هو يوم الثّامن عشر من ذي الحجّة وهو اليوم الذي نصب رسول الله (ص) عليّاً (ع) خليفة بحضرة الجمع الكثير من النّاس حيث قال : من كنت مولاه فعليّ مولاه قال الغزالي (6) Qamma al-bayt (قَمَّ البَيْت) qamman (قَمّاً) from the category of qatala (قَتَلَ) means he swept it (كَنَسَهُ). His Almighty saying: وَحَشَرْناهُمْ فَلَمْ نُغادِرْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَداً (and We shall gather them, leaving out none of them) means We did not leave anyone (أَحَداً) of them remaining. From this, al-Ghadīr (الغَدِير) was named, because it is water that the floods (السُّيُول) leave behind (يُغَادِرُهُ), meaning they leave it behind (تُخَلِّفُهُ). It is a faʿīl (فَعِيل) form meaning mafʿūl (مَفْعُول), or a faʿīl form meaning fāʿil (فَاعِل), because it betrays (يَغْدِرُ) its people, meaning it dries up (يَنْقَطِع) when it is severely needed. And [from this is] the prohibition in the supplication (الدُّعَاء): "O Allah, [protect me] from Your blessings (نِعَمِكَ) which are too magnificent to be left behind (تُغَادَر)," meaning to be cut off (تَنْقَطِع). Ghadīr Khumm (غَدِير خُمّ) is a place in al-Juḥfah (الجُحْفَة) known for severe epidemic (الوَبَاء). Al-Aṣmaʿī (الأَصْمَعِيّ) said: No one was born in Ghadīr Khumm and lived until puberty (يَحْتَلِم) unless he escaped from it. The Day of al-Ghadīr (يَوْم الغَدِير) is the eighteenth day of Dhū al-Ḥijjah (ذِي الحِجَّة). It is the day when the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) appointed Ali (عليه السلام) as Caliph (خَلِيفَة) in the presence of a large gathering of people, where He said: "Of whomsoever I am the Mawlā (مَوْلَاه), Ali is his Mawlā." Al-Ghazālī (الغَزَالِيّ) said:

footnote وهو من أكابر علماء القوم في كتابه المسمّى بسرّ العالمين ما هذا لفظه : قال رسول الله (ص) لعلي يوم الغدير من كنت مولاه فعليّ مولاه فقال عمر بن الخطّاب بخ بخ لك يا أبا الحسن لقد أصبحت مولاي ومولى كلّ مؤمن ومؤمنة ثمّ قال وهذا رضىً وتسليم وولاية وتحكيم ثمّ بعد ذلك غلب الهوى وحبّ الرياسة وعقود البنود وخفقان الرايات وازدحام الخيول وفتح الأمصار والأمر والنهي فحملتهم على الخلاف فَنَبَذُوهُ وَراءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَاشْتَرَوْا بِهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً فَبِئْسَ ما يَشْتَرُونَ إلى أن قال ثمّ أنّ أبا بكر قال على منبر رسول الله (ص) اقيلوني فلست بخيركم وعليّ فيكم أفقال ذلك هزواً وجداً أو امتحاناً فان كان هزواً فالخلفاء لا يليق بهم الهزل ثمّ قال والعجب من منازعة معاوية بن أبي سفيان عليّاً في الخلافة وأين ومن أين أليس رسول الله (ص) قطع طمع من طمع فيها بقوله : إذا ولّى الخليفتان فاقتلوا الأخير منهما والعجب من حقّ واحد كيف ينقسم بين اثنين والخلافة ليست بجسم ولا عرض فتتجزّى انتهى كلامه وفيه دلالة على انحرافه عمّا كان عليه والله أعلم. —who is one of the greatest scholars (أَكَابِر عُلَمَاء) of the people— in his book named Sirr al-ʿĀlamīn (سِرّ العَالَمِين), the wording of which is as follows: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) said to Ali on the Day of al-Ghadīr: "Of whomsoever I am the Mawlā (مَوْلَاه), Ali is his Mawlā." Then ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb (عُمَر بن الخَطَّاب) said: "Bravo! Bravo! (بَخْ بَخْ) to you, O Abū al-Ḥasan! You have become my Mawlā and the Mawlā of every believing man and woman." Then he said: "And this is acceptance (رِضَىً), submission (تَسْلِيم), wilāyah (وِلايَة), and arbitration (تَحْكِيم)." Then, after that, desire (الهَوَى) and the love of leadership (حُبّ الرِّيَاسَة), the tying of banners (عُقُود البُنُود), the fluttering of standards (خَفَقَان الرَّايَات), the crowding of horses (ازْدِحَام الخُيُول), the conquest of cities (فَتْح الأَمْصَار), and commanding and forbidding (الأَمْر وَالنَّهْي) prevailed, which led them to opposition (الخِلَاف). فَنَبَذُوهُ وَراءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَاشْتَرَوْا بِهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً فَبِئْسَ ما يَشْتَرُونَ (but they cast it behind their backs and sold it for a paltry price. How evil is what they buy!) until he said: Then Abū Bakr said upon the pulpit (مِنْبَر) of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله): "Relieve me (أَقِيلُونِي), for I am not the best among you while Ali is among you." Did he say that in jest (هَزْواً), seriously (جِدّاً), or as a test (امْتِحَاناً)? If it was in jest, then jesting is not appropriate for Caliphs (الخُلَفَاء). Then he said: And it is astonishing that Muʿāwiyah ibn Abī Sufyān (مُعَاوِيَة بن أَبِي سُفْيَان) disputed Ali regarding the Caliphate (الخِلَافَة). Where [is he] and from where [did he get this right]? Did not the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) cut off the ambition of anyone who aspired to it by His saying: "If two Caliphs are appointed, kill the latter of them"? And it is astonishing how a single right (حَقّ وَاحِد) can be divided between two people, for the Caliphate is neither a body (جِسْم) nor an accident (عَرَض) that can be partitioned (تَتَجَزَّى). [End of his speech]. And in this, there is an indication of his deviation (انْحِرَافِهِ) from what he was upon. And Allah knows best.

footnote (١) الدّغل محرّكة دخل في الأمر مفسد والشجرُ الكبير الملتفّ واشتباك النّبت وكثرته والموضع يخاف فيه الاغتيال ج أدغال ودِغال ومكان دغِل ككتِف. (1) Ad-Daghāl (الدَّغَل) (with movement on the ghayn) means corruption (مُفْسِد) entering into a matter, and the large, dense trees (الشَّجَر المُلْتَفّ), and the intertwining and abundance of vegetation (النَّبْت), and a place where assassination (الاغْتِيَال) is feared. Its plural (ج) is adghāl (أَدْغَال) and dighāl (دِغَال). And a place is described as daghil (دَغِل) like katif (كَتِف).

footnote (٢) يقال ختله يختله إذا خدعه وراوغه والمخاتلة المخادعة. (2) It is said: Khatalahū yakhtiluhū (خَتَلَهُ يَخْتِلُهُ) when he deceived him (خَدَعَهُ) and evaded him (رَاوَغَهُ). Al-Mukhātalah (المُخَاتَلَة) is deception (المُخَادَعَة).

footnote (١) يقال أفضيت بكذا إلى فلان أي أوصلته إليه ومسته به. (1) It is said: Afḍaytu bi-kadhā ilā fulān (أَفْضَيْتُ بِكَذَا إِلَى فُلَان) meaning I conveyed it (أَوْصَلْتُهُ) to him and touched him (مَسَسْتُهُ) with it.

footnote (٢) يقال حبوت الرّجل حباءً بالكسر والمدّ أعطيته الشيء بغير عوض والاسم منه الحبوة بالضمّ ومنه بيع المحاباة. (2) It is said: Ḥabawtu ar-rajul (حَبَوْتُ الرَّجُل) ḥibāʾan (حِبَاءً) (with a kasrah and madd) meaning I gave him the thing without compensation (عِوَض). The noun (الاسْم) derived from it is al-ḥubwah (الحُبْوَة) with a ḍammah (الضَّمّ). From this comes the sale of favor (بَيْع المُحَابَاة).

footnote (١) شلت بالجرّة أشول بها شولاً رفعتها ولا تقل شِلتُ ويقال أيضاً اشلت الجرّة فانشالت هي. (1) Shiltu bi-al-jarrah (شِلْتُ بِالجَرَّة) ashūlu bihā shawlan (أَشُولُ بِهَا شَوْلاً) means I lifted it (رَفَعْتُهَا). Do not say shiltu (شِلْتُ). It is also said: ashaltu al-jarrah (أَشَلْتُ الجَرَّة) and it lifted itself (فَانْشَالَتْ هِيَ).

footnote (١) قوله صلّى الله عليه وآله : الا انّهم أصحاب الصّحيفة أي أئمّة النار الغاصبين لحقّ عليّ (ع) فلينظر أحدكم ... أي فلينظر بعض منكم في صحيفته التي صنعها وحفظها عنده فيعرف نفسه انه من أئمة النار وأصحابها ويعرف شركاءه في هذا الأمر انهم بأسرهم من رؤساء أهل النار وقضية الصحيفة معروفة مشهورة لا يناسب المقام التعرض لتفصيلها لطولها واجمالها انّ سبعين رجلاً من رؤوس المعاندين وأصول الكفر والنفاق منهم الأول والثاني لما عرفوا هذا الأمر من رسول الله (ص) ورغبته في عليّ (ع) قالوا في أنفسهم وبعضهم انّا انّما آمنّا بمحمّد (ص) ظاهراً لجلب الرّياسة ونظم أمر دنيانا والآن قد تردّ الأمر على ابن عمّه وقطع رجاءنا فما الحيلة ولا يسعنا طاعة عليّ (ع) فتوطئوا أو تحالفوا على دفع هذا الأمر وعلاجه ولو تقبل رسول الله (ص) حتى إذا دفعوا السّم اليهما فدسّتا في اللبن واستقاه واجتمعوا في السقيفة وأوحى إليهم الشّيطان وافسدُوا ما أفسدوا قال يعني الإمام محمّد بن علي الباقر فذهب على الناس إلّا شرذمة منهم آه يعني ضاع واختفى عليهم أمر الصحيفة فلم يدروا ما في الصحيفة ولم يعرفوا أربابها فاغترّوا بهم بعد وفاة النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله واما الشرذمة فهؤلاء المتحالفون وبعض خواصّ النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله الذين أعلمهم النبيّ بفعل هؤلاء وما عقدوا عليه وما يريدون وسيركبونه في تخريب الدّين وافساد أمور المسلمين. (1) His saying صلى الله عليه وآله: "Verily, they are the Companions of the Scroll (أَصْحَاب الصَّحِيفَة)," meaning the Imams (أَئِمَّة) of the Fire, those who usurped the right (حَقّ) of Ali (عليه السلام). "So let one of you look..." meaning, let some of you look into his scroll (صَحِيفَتِهِ) which he made and kept with him, so that he may know himself to be one of the Imams of the Fire and its companions, and know his partners (شُرَكَاءَهُ) in this matter, that they are all leaders (رُؤَسَاء) of the people of the Fire. The matter of the Scroll (قَضِيَّة الصَّحِيفَة) is well-known and famous, but it is not appropriate for this context to delve into its details due to its length. In brief, seventy men from the heads of the obstinate (المُعَانِدِين) and the origins of disbelief (الكُفْر) and hypocrisy (النِّفَاق), among them the First and the Second, when they learned of this matter from the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) and His desire for Ali (عليه السلام), they said among themselves and to some others: "We only believed in Muḥammad (صلى الله عليه وآله) outwardly to gain leadership (الرِّيَاسَة) and organize our worldly affairs (أَمْر دُنْيَانَا). Now the matter has reverted to his cousin, and our hope has been cut off. What is the solution? We cannot tolerate obedience to Ali (عليه السلام)." So they conspired (تَوَطَّئُوا) or allied (تَحَالَفُوا) to repel this matter and deal with it, even if it meant killing the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله). They even administered poison (السَّمّ) to the two of them, and they mixed it into the milk, and he drank it. They gathered in the Saqīfah (السَّقِيفَة), and Satan (الشَّيْطَان) inspired them, and they caused the corruption they caused. He said—meaning Imam Muḥammad ibn Ali al-Bāqir (الإِمَام مُحَمَّد بن عَلِي البَاقِر)—"So it was lost upon the people except for a small group (شِرْذِمَة) of them." Ah, meaning the matter of the Scroll was lost and hidden from them, so they did not know what was in the Scroll and did not know its masters (أَرْبَابَهَا), and thus they were deceived by them after the death of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. As for the small group (الشِّرْذِمَة), they are these allies (المُتَحَالِفُون) and some of the close companions (خَوَاصّ) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله whom the Prophet informed about the actions of these people, what they had agreed upon, and what they intended and would undertake in destroying the religion (الدِّين) and corrupting the affairs of the Muslims (المُسْلِمِين).

footnote (١) الغراف كشداد نهر بين واسط والبصرة عليه كورة كبيرة وفرس البرّاء بن قيس ومن الأنهر الكثير الماء قاله الفيروزآبادي والمراد به هنا المعنى الأخير أي هو النهر العظيم المنشق من عميق بحر الولاية. (1) Al-Ghirāf (الغِرَاف), like Shaddād (شَدَّاد), is a river between Wāsiṭ (وَاسِط) and Basra (البَصْرَة), upon which is a large district (كُورَة). It is also the name of the horse of Al-Barāʾ ibn Qays (البَرَّاء بن قَيْس). And it is one of the rivers with abundant water, as stated by Al-Fayrūzābādī (الفيروزآباديّ). What is intended here is the last meaning: that is, it is the great river (النَّهْر العَظِيم) branching off from the deep sea of al-wilāyah (الوِلايَة).

footnote (٢) قوله تعالى **سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الْخُرْطُومِ **أي سنجعل له سمة أهل النَّار وهي أن سودَّ وجهه وقوله أنّه يسم ... يمكن ان يكون من هذا القبيل بأن يجعل سمة الفضل والجهل على أربابهما ووسِمه وسماً وسِمَة إذا اثر فيه بسمة وكي ووسمت في الشيء وسماً من باب وعد علميّة ومن هذين أيضاً يناسب اخذه. (2) His Almighty saying: سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الْخُرْطُومِ (We will brand him on the snout) means We will place upon him the mark (سِمَة) of the people of the Fire, which is that his face will be blackened. And His saying: "He marks (يَسِمُ) ..." may be of this category, by placing the mark of virtue (الفَضْل) and ignorance (الجَهْل) upon their respective masters (أَرْبَابِهِمَا). And wasamahū wasman wa simah (وَسِمَهُ وَسْماً وَسِمَة) means he left a trace (أَثَّرَ) on him with a mark (سِمَة) or cauterization (كَيّ). And wasamtu fī ash-shayʾi wasman (وَسَمْتُ فِي الشَّيْءِ وَسْماً) from the category of waʿada (وَعَدَ) means a sign (عِلْمِيَّة). Taking [the meaning] from these two is also appropriate.

footnote (٣) قوله في مقام واحد أي في مرتبةٍ واحدة. (3) His saying: fī maqām wāḥid (فِي مَقَام وَاحِد) means in a single rank (مَرْتَبَةٍ وَاحِدَة).

footnote (١) أي يوصي بعضكم إلى بعض. (1) Meaning, some of you bequeath (يُوصِي) to others.

footnote (١) تداك عليه الناي أي اجتمعوا. (1) Tadāka ʿalayhi an-nāy (تَدَاكَ عَلَيْهِ النَّاي) means they gathered (اجْتَمَعُوا).

footnote (٢) يقال صفقت له بالبيعة صفقاً أي ضربت بيدي على يده وكانت العرب إذا وجب البيع ضربت أحدهما يده على يد صاحبه ثمّ انتقلت الصفقة في العقد فقيل بارك الله لك في صفقة يدك. (2) It is said: Ṣafaqa lahu bi-al-bayʿah ṣafqan (صَفَقَ لَهُ بِالبَيْعَة صَفْقاً) meaning I struck my hand upon his hand [in allegiance (بَيْعَة)]. The Arabs, when a sale (البَيْع) was finalized, one of them would strike his hand upon the hand of his companion. Then aṣ-ṣafqah (الصَّفْقَة) transferred to the contract (العَقْد), so it was said: "May Allah bless you in the striking (صَفْقَة) of your hand."

footnote (٣) المأثرة بضم الثاء المكرمة لأنّها تؤثر وتتحدّث بها. (3) Al-Maʾthurah (المَأْثُرَة) with a ḍammah (ضَمّ) on the thāʾ (الثَّاء) means a noble deed (المَكْرُمَة), because it is narrated (تُؤْثَر) and spoken about (تَتَحَدَّث بِهَا).

footnote (١) قوله تحت قدمي أي مضمحلّ ومشفٍ وموهون كالشّيء الّذي يقع تحت القدمين فانّه ليس شيء أهون منه ونسبه الى نفسه لأنّه الّذي أزال حرمته. (1) His saying: "under my feet" means annihilated (مُضْمَحِلّ), cured (مُشْفٍ), and weakened (مَوْهُون), like a thing that falls under the two feet, for there is nothing easier (أَهْوَن) than it. He attributed it to Himself because He is the One Who removed its sanctity (حُرْمَتَهُ).

footnote (٢) لما تعارف بينهم في الجاهلية أكل الربا وممّن كان يكثر هذه المعاملة العبّاس عمّه أو كان ذمّة كثير منهم مشغولة بالمنافع الربويّة للعبّاس بمقتضى المعاملات الصّادرة منه معهم في الجاهلية وقد حرّمها الله فحينئذٍ إذا سروا العبّاس ومنْ عليه النّبي بالفداء شرط عليه بخصوصه وإن كان من لوازم الإسلام أيضاً ان لا يطالب بها ويقنع بالأصل ويترك الفرع فأشار (ص) في خطبته الى هذا الأمر وإلى أنّه لا خصوصية في هذا للعباس بل هو حكم عام للمسلمين. (2) Because the consumption of usury (الرِّبَا) was customary among them during the Age of Ignorance (الجَاهِلِيَّة). Among those who frequently engaged in this transaction was Al-ʿAbbās (العَبَّاس), his uncle. Or, the liabilities (ذِمَّة) of many of them were occupied with usurious benefits (المَنَافِع الرِّبَوِيَّة) owed to Al-ʿAbbās, according to the transactions issued by him with them during the Age of Ignorance, which Allah had forbidden. Therefore, when they released Al-ʿAbbās—and the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) bestowed a favor (مَنَّ) upon him by ransom (الفِدَاء)—He stipulated this specifically for him, even though it is also one of the requirements (لَوَازِم) of Islam not to demand it, but to be content with the principal (الأَصْل) and abandon the increment (الفَرْع). So, He (صلى الله عليه وآله) alluded to this matter in His sermon (خُطْبَتِهِ), and to the fact that there is no specificity in this for Al-ʿAbbās, but rather it is a general ruling (حُكْم عَامّ) for the Muslims (المُسْلِمِين).

footnote (٣) وفي الدعاء أسألك نعمةً تنعشني بها وعيالي أي ترفعني بها عن مواطن الذلّ من قولهم نعشه الله ينعشه نعشاً رفعه. (3) And in the supplication (الدُّعَاء): "I ask You for a blessing (نِعْمَةً) by which You raise me (تُنْعِشُنِي) and my family (عِيَالِي)," meaning You elevate me by it above places of humiliation (مَوَاطِن الذُّلّ). This comes from their saying: Naʿashahu Allāh yanʿishuhu naʿshan (نَعَشَهُ الله يَنْعِشُهُ نَعْشاً) meaning He raised him (رَفَعَهُ).

footnote (٤) الكتيبة على فعيلة الطّائفة من الجيش والجمع الكتائب. (4) Al-Katībah (الكَتِيبَة) on the pattern of faʿīlah (فَعِيلَة) means a detachment (الطَّائِفَة) of the army (الجَيْش). The plural (الجَمْع) is al-katāʾib (الكَتَائِب).

footnote (١) قوله تعالى **فَسُحْقاً لِأَصْحابِ السَّعِيرِ **أي بعداً يقال سحق المكان فهو سحيق مثل بعد فهو بعيد لفظاً ومعنىً. (1) His Almighty saying: فَسُحْقاً لِأَصْحابِ السَّعِيرِ (So away with the inmates of the Blaze!) means distance (بُعْداً). It is said: Saḥuqa al-makān (سَحُقَ المَكَان) so it is saḥīq (سَحِيق), just like baʿuda (بَعُدَ) so it is baʿīd (بَعِيد), in both wording (لَفْظاً) and meaning (مَعْنىً).

footnote (٢) يقال نعيت الميت من باب نفع إذا اخبر بموته وهو منعيّ ونعى إليه نفسه أخبر بموته. (2) It is said: Naʿaytu al-mayyit (نَعَيْتُ المَيِّت) from the category of nafaʿa (نَفَعَ) when one announces his death (أَخْبَرَ بِمَوْتِهِ), and he is manʿiyy (مَنْعِيّ). And naʿā ilayhi nafsahū (نَعَى إِلَيْهِ نَفْسَهُ) means he announced his own death.

footnote (٣) نضر وجهه من باب قتل أي حسن ونضّر الله وجهه يتعدى ولا يتعدى ويقال نضّر الله وجهه بالتشديد وأنضر الله وجهه بمعناه وفي الخبر نضّر الله امرأ سمع مقالتي ... أي حسنه بالسّرور والبهجة لما رزق بعلمه ومعرفته من القدر والمنزلة بين النّاس ونعمة في الأخرى حتّى يرى عليه رونق الرخاء ورفيق النعمة. (3) Naḍura wajhuhū (نَضُرَ وَجْهُهُ) from the category of qatala (قَتَلَ) means his face became beautiful (حَسُنَ). And naḍḍara Allāh wajhahū (نَضَّرَ الله وَجْهَهُ) can be transitive or intransitive. It is also said: naḍḍara Allāh wajhahū (نَضَّرَ الله وَجْهَهُ) with tashdīd (التَّشْدِيد), and anḍara Allāh wajhahū (أَنْضَرَ الله وَجْهَهُ) with the same meaning. And in the narration (الخَبَر): "May Allah beautify (نَضَّرَ) the man who heard my saying..." meaning He beautified him with joy (السُّرُور) and delight (البَهْجَة) due to the status (القَدْر) and rank (المَنْزِلَة) he was granted among the people through his knowledge (عِلْمِهِ) and recognition (مَعْرِفَتِهِ), and a blessing (نِعْمَة) in the Hereafter, until the luster (رَوْنَق) of prosperity (الرَّخَاء) and the companion (رَفِيق) of blessing are seen upon him.

footnote (٤) أي ترك الغش وركوب الصّفا والصدق وإخلاص لطاعة. (4) Meaning, abandoning deceit (الغِشّ), adhering to purity (الصَّفَا) and truthfulness (الصِّدْق), and sincerity (إِخْلَاص) in obedience (الطَّاعَة).

footnote (٥) أي دعوة الله وهي الموت محيطة من ورائهم أي محيطة بالناس بعد انقضاء اجالهم أي من كان عاقبة أمره الموت ينبغي أن لا يترك هذه الخصال الثلاث. (5) Meaning, the call (دَعْوَة) of Allah, which is death (المَوْت), encompasses them from behind (مُحِيطَة مِنْ وَرَائِهِمْ), meaning it encompasses people after the expiration of their appointed times (آجَالِهِمْ). That is, whoever has death as the outcome (عَاقِبَة) of his affair should not abandon these three qualities (الخِصَال الثَّلَاث).

footnote (١) او عزت إليه بكذا تقدّمت وكذلك وعزّت إليه توعيزاً قال في ص وقد يخفّف. (1) Awʿaztu ilayhi bi-kadhā (أَوْعَزْتُ إِلَيْهِ بِكَذَا) means I preceded [him] (تَقَدَّمْتُ). Likewise, waʿaztu ilayhi tawʿīzan (وَعَّزْتُ إِلَيْهِ تَوْعِيزاً). He said in [the book] Ṣād (ص): And sometimes it is lightened (يُخَفَّف).

footnote (٢) هرشى كسكرى ثنية قرب الجحفة. (2) Harshā (هَرْشَى), like sakrā (سَكْرَى), is a mountain pass (ثَنِيَّة) near al-Juḥfah (الجُحْفَة).

(٦٨) قُلْ يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لَسْتُمْ عَلى شَيْءٍ ** (68) Say, 'O People of the Book! You do not stand on anything (You do not stand) upon a religion that is considered worthy (يُعْتَدّ بِهِ) so that it might be called a 'thing' (شَيْءٍ), due to its corruption and invalidity. حَتَّى تُقِيمُوا التَّوْراةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ ** until you maintain the Torah and the Evangel, by affirming what is in them concerning the glad tidings of Muhammad صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and submitting to his ruling. وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ. and what has been sent down to you from your Lord.' Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: It is the wilayah (وِلايَة) of the Commander of the Faithful صلوات الله وسلامه عليه. وَلَيَزِيدَنَّ كَثِيراً مِنْهُمْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ طُغْياناً وَكُفْراً فَلا تَأْسَ عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكافِرِينَ ** What has been sent down to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in rebellion and disbelief. So do not grieve for the faithless lot. So do not feel sorrow for them due to the increase in their rebellion (طُغْيان) and disbelief (كُفْر), for the harm of that returns to them and does not pass beyond them. And in the believers, there is sufficiency (مَنْدُوحَة) for you, dispensing with them.

(٦٩) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَالَّذِينَ هادُوا وَالصَّابِئُونَ وَالنَّصارى مَنْ آمَنَ ** (Indeed, those who have faith, and the Jews, the Sabaeans, and the Christians—those among them who have faith) meaning: from among them بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً فَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ ** (in Allah and the Last Day and act righteously—they shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.) Its exegesis (تفسير) has already preceded in Surat al-Baqarah.

(٧٠) لَقَدْ أَخَذْنا مِيثاقَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** with al-tawhid (التوحِيد), prophethood (النبوّة), and al-wilayah (الولاية), وَأَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْهِمْ رُسُلاً ** so that they might remind them, clarify for them the matter of their religion, and make them follow the commands and prohibitions. كُلَّما جاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ بِما لا تَهْوى أَنْفُسُهُمْ ** namely, the religious obligations (التكاليف), فَرِيقاً كَذَّبُوا وَفَرِيقاً يَقْتُلُونَ** (70) We certainly took a covenant from the Children of Israel, and We sent apostles to them. Whenever an apostle brought them what their souls did not desire, they would call a group liars, and slay a group.

It was said that He recounted the past situation (الحال الماضية) to bring it vividly to mind, to express horror at the killing (القتل), to draw attention to the fact that this is their custom (ديدنهم) both in the past and the future, and to preserve the rhyme/endings of the verses (رؤس الأي).

(٧١) وَحَسِبُوا أَلَّا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ ** (71) and they supposed there would be no trial. that no affliction (بلاء) or punishment (عذاب) from Allah would befall them due to their killing of the prophets and their denial of them. And it was read (وقرء) لا تكون with the rafʿ (الرّفع) (nominative case), meaning that it would not be (أنّه لا يكون). فَعَمُوا ** concerning the religion (عن الدين) وَصَمُّوا ** concerning the listening to the truth (عن استماع الحق). ثُمَّ تابَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ عَمُوا وَصَمُّوا ** a second time (كرة أخرى). كَثِيرٌ مِنْهُمْ (many of them) is a substitute (بدل) for the pronoun (الضمير). وَاللهُ بَصِيرٌ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ ** (71) Then Allah relented toward them; then again many of them became blind and deaf, and Allah is watchful of what they do.**

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding وَحَسِبُوا أَلَّا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ ** (and they supposed there would be no trial), He said: [This was] when the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was among them. فَعَمُوا وَصَمُّوا ** (so they became blind and deaf) [This was] when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed away. ثُمَّ تابَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (Then Allah relented toward them) [This was] when the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) arose. ثُمَّ عَمُوا وَصَمُّوا ** (then again many of them became blind and deaf) [This continues] until this hour (2).

(٧٢) **لَقَدْ كَفَرَ الَّذِينَ قالُوا إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ وَقالَ الْمَسِيحُ يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ رَبِّي وَرَبَّكُمْ ** (72) Certainly they are faithless who say, 'Allah is the Messiah, son of Mary,' while the Messiah has said, 'O Children of Israel, worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.'

Meaning: I am a servant (عَبْد) who is nurtured (مَرْبُوب); there is no difference between me and you. The Almighty Allah argued against them by His saying: إِنَّهُ مَنْ يُشْرِكْ بِاللهِ ** (Indeed, whoever ascribes partners to Allah) in His worship or in what is specific to Him of His attributes (صِفَاتِهِ) and actions (أَفْعَالِهِ), فَقَدْ حَرَّمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ الْجَنَّةَ ** (Allah has forbidden him paradise) because it is the abode (دَار) of the monotheists (المُوَحِّدِين), وَمَأْواهُ النَّارُ ** (and his refuge will be the Fire) because it is prepared for the polytheists (المُشْرِكِين). وَما لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنْصارٍ ** (and the wrongdoers will have no helpers.) The explicit noun (الظَّاهِر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (المُضْمَر) to confirm (تَسْجِيلًا) that polytheism (الشِّرْك) is wrongdoing (ظُلْم). And this [statement] is either from the speech of Jesus (عِيسَى) (upon our Prophet and him be peace) or from the speech of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.

footnote (١) فظع الأمر ككرم فظاعة فهو فظيع اي شديد شنيع جاوز المقدار في ذلك كافظع وأفظعه واستفظعه وجده فظيعاً م. (1) The matter was terrible (فَظُعَ) like (كَكَرُمَ) terribleness (فَظَاعَة), so it is terrible (فَظِيع), meaning severe (شَدِيد), atrocious (شَنِيع), exceeding the measure in that, like (كَأَفْظَعَ) and (أَفْظَعَهُ). And (اِسْتَفْظَعَهُ) means he found it terrible (فَظِيعًا). (م)

footnote (٢) لعلّ المراد بالسّاعة في هذه الرواية ساعة غلبة الحق بظهور القائم عليه السلام جمعاً بينها وبين سائر الرّوايات فانّ غلبة الحق على الكفر في ثلاث دورات الأولى في زمان الرسول صلى الله عليه وآله بحيث انقطع العذر عن كلّ أحد والثانية في زمان أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام والثالثة في زمان القائم عليه السلام فحسب أهل الكتاب في الأولين انّهم لم يفتتنوا بما وعدوا في كتبهم فلم يؤمنوا حتّى غلب الحق وانقطع عذرهم واختفى ظهور الحق فعموا وصمّوا لكن في الثالثة يؤمنون به كما فسّر سابقاً قوله تعالى **وَإِنْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ إِلَّا لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ بِهِ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ.** (2) Perhaps the intent of the Hour (السَّاعَة) in this narration is the hour of the triumph of the Truth (غَلَبَةُ الْحَقّ) through the appearance of the Riser (الْقَائِم) (peace be upon him), reconciling it with the rest of the narrations. For the triumph of the Truth over disbelief (الْكُفْر) occurs in three cycles (دَوْرَات): the first is during the time of the Messenger (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), such that the excuse (الْعُذْر) was cut off from everyone; the second is during the time of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him); and the third is during the time of the Riser (الْقَائِم) (peace be upon him). Thus, the People of the Book (أَهْلُ الْكِتَاب) assumed in the first two [cycles] that they would not be tested (يُفْتَتَنُوا) by what they were promised in their books, so they did not believe until the Truth triumphed and their excuse was cut off, and the manifestation of the Truth became hidden, so they became blind (عَمُوا) and deaf (صَمُّوا). However, in the third [cycle], they will believe in Him, as was previously interpreted regarding the Almighty's saying: وَإِنْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ إِلَّا لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ بِهِ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ. (There is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death.)

(٧٣) **لَقَدْ كَفَرَ الَّذِينَ قالُوا إِنَّ اللهَ ثالِثُ ثَلاثَةٍ ** (73) (They are certainly faithless who say, 'Allah is the third of three.')

meaning, one of three. It is said that those who say this are the majority of the Christians. They say: three hypostases (أقانيم) (1), one essence (جوهر واحد): Father (أب), Son (ابن), and Holy Spirit (روح القدس), [which is] one God (إله واحد). They do not say three gods (ثلاثة آلهة), and they prohibit this expression, even though it is necessitated for them, because they say the Son is a god (إله), and the Father is His, and the Holy Spirit is a god (إله), and the Son is not the Father.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "As for the Messiah (المسيح), they disobeyed him and magnified him in their souls until they claimed that He is a god (إله) and that He is the Son of Allah. And a group among them said: 'The third of three,' and a group among them said: 'He is Allah.'"

(٧٤) وَما مِنْ إِلهٍ إِلَّا إِلهٌ واحِدٌ ** (74) There is no god except the One God. And He is Allah alone, having no partner. And مِنْ ** is an extra particle (مزيدة) for emphasizing the negation (النّفي). وَإِنْ لَمْ يَنْتَهُوا عَمَّا يَقُولُونَ ** And if they do not desist from what they say, He swore: لَيَمَسَّنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ ** there shall surely befall those of them who are faithless (meaning: those who persist in their disbelief (كفره) and do not desist from it) عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. a painful punishment.

(٧٥) أَفَلا يَتُوبُونَ إِلَى اللهِ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَهُ ** (75) Will they not turn to Allah in repentance and plead with Him for forgiveness? This contains astonishment at their persistence [in error]. وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (75) and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. He conceals the sins of the servants and shows mercy to them if they repent. مَا الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ ** (75) The Messiah, son of Mary, is but an apostle. [Other] apostles have passed before him. He is nothing but an Apostle from the same kind of Apostles who passed before him. He came with dazzling miracles from Allah, the Exalted, just as they came. For if He brought the dead to life through his hand, then He brought the staff to life through the hand of Moses and made it a living thing that moved, and that is more astonishing. And if He created him without a father, then He created Adam without a father or a mother, and that is stranger. وَأُمُّهُ صِدِّيقَةٌ ** (75) And his mother was a truthful woman. She affirmed the truth of the words of her Lord and His Books, like the rest of the women who adhere to truthfulness (الصدق). كانا يَأْكُلانِ الطَّعامَ ** (75) They both used to eat food. In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Its meaning is that they both used to defecate (يتغوّطان). And al-Qummi said: كانا ** (They both used to) pass waste (يحدثان), so He alluded to the passing of waste (الحدث), and everyone who eats food passes waste (يحدث).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): In response to the Zindiq (heretic) who said to him: "Were it not for the disagreement (الإختلاف) and contradiction (التناقض) in the Quran, I would have entered your religion." Then he mentioned among those points that Allah exposed the slips (هفوات) of His Prophets and alluded to the names of His enemies. He (peace be upon him) said: "As for the slips (هفوات) of the Prophets and what Allah made clear in His Book, that is among the clearest proofs of Allah's dazzling wisdom, His overwhelming power, and His manifest might. This is because He knew that the proofs (براهين) of the Prophets would become magnified in the hearts of their nations, and that some of them would take some of them as a god, such as what occurred among the Christians regarding the son of Mary. So He mentioned a proof indicating their falling short of the perfection (الكمال) with which He, the Mighty and Majestic, was uniquely characterized. Have you not heard His saying regarding the description of Jesus (upon our Prophet and upon him be peace), where He said concerning him and his mother: كانا يَأْكُلانِ الطَّعامَ ** (They both used to eat food)? This means that whoever eats food has waste (ثفل), and whoever has waste (ثفل) is far removed from what the Christians claimed for the son of Mary."

footnote (١) *Al-Afanim* (*الأفانيم*) means the origins (*الأصول*), the singular of which is *Uqnum* (*اقنوم*). I believe it is a Roman word (*روميّة*).

(٧٦) **انْظُرْ كَيْفَ نُبَيِّنُ لَهُمُ الْآياتِ ثُمَّ انْظُرْ أَنَّى يُؤْفَكُونَ ** (76) Observe how We elaborate the signs for them, then observe how they are turned away!

(This explains) how they are turned away from listening to the truth and reflecting upon it. And the word ثُمَّ (then) is used due to the great difference between the two wonders (العجبين). That is, Our clarification of the signs is wondrous (عجيب), but their turning away from them is even more wondrous (أعجب).

**قُلْ أَتَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ ضَرًّا وَلا نَفْعاً ** Say, 'Do you worship, instead of Allah, that which has no power to benefit or harm you?'

This refers to Jesus (peace be upon him), for he did not possess any of that (power) inherently (من ذاته). And if he possessed any part of it, it was only by the permission of Allah and by His granting him ownership (تمليكه إيّاه).

وَاللهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** لما يقولون الْعَلِيمُ ** And Allah is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

And Allah is He, the All-hearing of what they say, the All-knowing of what they believe (يعتقدون).

(77) قُلْ يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لا تَغْلُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ غَيْرَ الْحَقِ ** (77) Say, 'O People of the Book! Do not exaggerate in your religion unjustly, (meaning) an undue exaggeration (غُلُوّاً بَاطِلاً). That is, do not exceed the limit (الحدّ) which Allah has set for you, and do not raise Jesus (peace be upon him) from the limit (حدّ) of prophethood (النبوّة) to the limit (حدّ) of divinity (الألوهية). وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا أَهْواءَ قَوْمٍ قَدْ ضَلُّوا مِنْ قَبْلُ ** They are their leaders (أئمتهم) in Christianity (النصرانية), who were in error (الضلال) before the advent (مبعث) of the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. وَأَضَلُّوا كَثِيراً ** from those who pledged allegiance (بايعهم) to them regarding the Trinity (التثليث). وَضَلُّوا ** when the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم was sent, عَنْ سَواءِ السَّبِيلِ ** when they denied him and acted wrongfully (بغوا) against him.

** (٧٨) **لُعِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ عَلى لِسانِ داوُدَ وَعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ ** (78) Those of the Children of Israel who were faithless were cursed upon the tongue of David and Jesus son of Mary.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The swine (الخنازير) were [cursed] upon the tongue of David (peace be upon him), and the apes (القردة) upon the tongue of Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon them both).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for David, He cursed the people of Aylah (أيلة) (1) when they transgressed concerning their Sabbath, and their transgression occurred during his time. So he said: "O Allah, clothe them in the curse like a cloak upon the shoulders and like a belt upon the loins (الحَقْوين)." So Allah transformed them into apes (قردة). And as for Jesus (peace be upon him), he cursed those upon whom the Table (المائِدة) was sent down, and who then disbelieved after that.

And He narrated it in al-Jawami' as a broken chain (مقطوعاً) and added: So Jesus (peace be upon him) said: "O Allah, punish those who disbelieved after they had eaten from the Table (المائِدة) with a punishment that You do not punish anyone else in the worlds, and curse them just as You cursed the People of the Sabbath (أصحاب السبت)." So they became swine (خنازير), and they were five thousand men.

ذلِكَ بِما عَصَوْا وَكانُوا يَعْتَدُونَ. That was because they disobeyed and used to commit transgression.

(1) Aylah (أيلة) is a mountain between Mecca (مكّة) and Medina (المدينة) near Yanbu' (ينبع), and a place between Yanbu' (ينبع) and Egypt (مصر) and its pass (عقبتها).

(٧٩) كانُوا لا يَتَناهَوْنَ عَنْ مُنكَرٍ فَعَلُوهُ ** (79 They did not forbid one another from the evil that they committed.) This is a clarification of their disobedience and transgression, meaning they would not desist, or some of them would not forbid others from the evil (المنكر).

Al-Qummi said: They used to eat pork, drink intoxicants (الخمور), and approach women during their days of menstruation.

And in Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): When deficiency (التقصير) occurred among the Children of Israel, a man among them would see his brother committing a sin and would forbid him, but he would not desist. Yet, that would not prevent him from being his table-companion (أكيله), his close associate (جليسه), and his drinking-companion (شريبه), until Allah struck the hearts of some of them against others, and the Quran was revealed concerning them, where He, majestic and mighty is He, says: لُعِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (The ones who were faithless were cursed...) [referring to the continuation of the Ayah].

And Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, they would not enter their entrances (مداخلهم) nor sit in their gatherings (مجالسهم), but when they met them, they would feel comfortable with them (أنسوا بهم).

لَبِئْسَ ما كانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ ** (—evil indeed was what they used to do!) An expression of astonishment at the evil of their deed, confirmed by an oath (بالقسم).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a group of the Shi'a who engage in the affairs of the ruler (السلطان), work for them, collect taxes (يجبون لهم) for them, and show loyalty (يوالونهم) to them. He said: "They are not from the Shi'a, but rather they are from those [cursed ones]." Then He recited: لُعِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (The ones who were faithless were cursed...) [referring to the continuation of the Ayah].

(٨٠) تَرى كَثِيراً مِنْهُمْ يَتَوَلَّوْنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (80) You see many of them befriending the faithless. They befriend them (يوالوهم) and they take them as sincere friends (ويصادقونهم). لَبِئْسَ ما قَدَّمَتْ لَهُمْ أَنْفُسُهُمْ ** Evil is what their souls have sent ahead for them. Evil is their provision (زادهم) for the Hereafter: that Allah is wrathful toward them, and in the torment they shall abide eternally. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding يَتَوَلَّوْنَ (they befriend): They befriend the tyrannical kings (الملوك الجبارين) and beautify their desires (أهوائهم) for them, so that they may attain something from their worldly possessions (من دنياهم).

(٨١) وَلَوْ كانُوا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالنَّبِيِّ وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَا اتَّخَذُوهُمْ أَوْلِياءَ ** (81) Had they believed in Allah and the Prophet and what has been sent down to him, they would not have taken them as allies. For indeed, faith prevents that. وَلكِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنْهُمْ فاسِقُونَ ** but many of them are transgressors. They are deviating (خارجون) from their religion. (٨٢) لَتَجِدَنَّ أَشَدَّ النَّاسِ عَداوَةً لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الْيَهُودَ وَالَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا ** (82) You will surely find the most hostile of all people to the believers to be the Jews and the polytheists. Due to the severity of their obstinacy (شكيمتهم) (١), the doubling of their disbelief (كفرهم), their immersion (انهماكهم) (٢) in following desires (الهوى), their reliance (ركونهم) upon imitation (التقليد), their distance (بعدهم) from verification (التحقيق), and their habituation (تمرنهم) to denying the Prophets and showing enmity towards them. وَلَتَجِدَنَّ أَقْرَبَهُمْ مَوَدَّةً لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الَّذِينَ قالُوا إِنَّا نَصارى ** and you will surely find the nearest of them in affection to the believers to be those who say, 'We are Christians.' Due to the softness of their disposition (لين جانبهم), the tenderness of their hearts (رقة قلوبهم), their lack of eagerness for the world (الدنيا), and the abundance of their concern (اهتمامهم) with knowledge (العلم) and action (العمل). ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ مِنْهُمْ قِسِّيسِينَ ** That is because among them are priests (قِسِّيسِينَ), who are leaders (رؤساء) in religion (الدين) and knowledge (العلم), وَرُهْباناً and monks, who are worshippers (عباداً). وَأَنَّهُمْ لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ** and that they are not arrogant. They do not show arrogance (يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ) regarding the acceptance of the truth (الحق) when they understand it, and they are humble (يتواضعون).

(٨٣) **وَإِذا سَمِعُوا ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ تَرى أَعْيُنَهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ الدَّمْعِ مِمَّا عَرَفُوا مِنَ الْحَقِّ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنا آمَنَّا فَاكْتُبْنا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ ** (When they hear what has been sent down to the Apostle, you see their eyes overflowing with tears because of the truth that they have recognized. They say, 'Our Lord! We believe, so write us down with the witnesses.) (They are) among those who bore witness that it is the truth.

(٨٤) **وَما لَنا لا نُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَما جاءَنا مِنَ الْحَقِّ وَنَطْمَعُ أَنْ يُدْخِلَنا رَبُّنا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الصَّالِحِينَ ** (84) What is wrong with us that we should not believe in Allah and what has come to us of the truth, while we are eager that our Lord should admit us with the righteous people?

This is an interrogative (استفهام) expressing denial (إنكار) and deeming it unlikely (استبعاد) regarding the absence of faith (الإِيمان), despite the presence of the motive (الداعي), which is the eagerness (الطمع) to join (الانخراط) (3) with the righteous people (الصَّالِحِينَ) and to enter their places of entry (مداخلهم).

(85) So Allah rewarded them for what they said due to belief (اعتقاد) and sincerity (إخلاص), as indicated by His saying: from the truth they recognized. And when speech is coupled with recognition (المعرفة), faith (الإِيمان) is perfected. gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them [forever]. eternally. And that is the reward of the virtuous.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: That is because there are among them priests and monks. He said: "Those were the ones who lived between [the time of] Jesus and Muhammad (peace be upon them both), awaiting the coming of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

Al-Qummi [said]: The reason for its revelation was that when Quraysh intensified their harm against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his Companions who believed in Mecca before the Hijrah (الهجرة), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded them to leave for Abyssinia (الحبشة). He commanded Ja‘far ibn Abi Talib to go out with them. So Ja‘far departed, accompanied by seventy men from the Muslims, until they sailed across the sea. When their departure reached Quraysh, they sent ‘Amr ibn al-‘As and ‘Umarah ibn al-Walid to the Negus (النجاشيّ) to return them [the Muslims] to them. ‘Amr and ‘Umarah were enemies. So Quraysh said: "How can we send two men who are enemies?" So Banu Makhzum absolved themselves of ‘Umarah’s crime (جناية) and Banu Sahm absolved themselves of ‘Amr ibn al-‘As’s crime (جناية). ‘Umarah departed, and he was handsome, a pampered youth. ‘Amr ibn al-‘As took his family with him. When they boarded the ship, they drank wine (الخمر). ‘Umarah said to ‘Amr ibn al-‘As: "Tell your family to accept me." ‘Amr said: "Is this permissible? Glory be to Allah (سبحان الله)!" So ‘Umarah fell silent. When ‘Amr became intoxicated (1)—and he was at the bow of the ship—‘Umarah pushed him and threw him into the sea. ‘Amr clung to the bow of the ship, and they caught him and pulled him out.

Then they arrived before the Negus, having brought gifts for him, which He accepted from them. ‘Amr ibn al-‘As said: "O King, a people who have opposed us in our religion and insulted our deities (آلهتنا) have come to you, so return them to us." The Negus sent for Ja‘far, and he came to Him. He said: "O Ja‘far, what are these people saying?" Ja‘far said: "O King, what are they saying?" He said: "They are asking me to return you to them."

Ja‘far said: "O King, ask them: Are we slaves belonging to them?" ‘Amr said: "No, rather they are noble freemen (أحرار كرام)." Ja‘far said: "Then ask them: Do they have debts upon us that they demand from us?" ‘Amr said: "No, we have no debts upon you." Ja‘far said: "Do you have blood-claims (دماء) against us that you demand?" ‘Amr said: "No." Ja‘far said: "Then what do you want from us? You harmed us, so we left your land."

‘Amr ibn al-‘As said: "O King, they opposed us in our religion, insulted our deities (آلهتنا), corrupted our youth, and divided our community (جماعتنا). So return them to us so that we may unite our affair." Ja‘far said: "Yes, O King, we opposed them. Allah sent a Prophet among us who commanded [us] to cast off rivals (الأنداد) and abandon seeking omens by arrows (الاستقسام بالأزلام). He commanded us to perform the prayer (الصّلوة) and pay the poor-rate (الزّكوة). He forbade oppression (الظلم), injustice (الجور), the shedding of blood unjustly, fornication (الزّناءِ), usury (الرّباءِ), carrion (الميتة), blood, and the flesh of swine (لحم الخنزير). He commanded us to [uphold] justice (العدل), goodness (الإِحسان), and giving to relatives (إيتاء ذي القربى), and He forbade indecency (الفحشاء), evil (المنكر), and transgression (البغي)." The Negus said: "With this, Allah sent Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him)." Then the Negus said: "O Ja‘far, do you memorize anything of what Allah revealed to your Prophet?" He said: "Yes." So he recited to Him Surah Maryam (peace be upon her). When he reached His saying: and shake toward yourself the trunk of the palm tree, and fresh ripe dates will drop upon you. 26. Eat and drink and be comforted.

When the Negus heard this, He wept intensely and said: "By Allah, this is the truth (الحق)." ‘Amr ibn al-‘As said: "O King, this man opposes us, so return him to us." The Negus raised His hand and struck ‘Amr’s face with it, then said: "Be silent! By Allah, if you mention him with evil, I will surely take your life from you!" ‘Amr ibn al-‘As stood up and left Him, with blood flowing down his face, saying: "If this is as you say, O King, then we will not interfere with him." There was a maidservant (1) (وصيفةً) of the Negus standing at His head, driving away [flies] (2) (تذب) from Him. She looked at ‘Umarah ibn al-Walid, who was a handsome youth, and she loved him. When ‘Amr ibn al-‘As returned to his lodging, he said to ‘Umarah: "If only you would send a message to the King’s maidservant." So he sent her a message, and she responded. ‘Amr said: "Tell her to send you some of the King’s perfume (طيب)." So he told her, and she sent it to him. ‘Amr took some of that perfume. What ‘Umarah had done to him when he threw him into the sea was still in his heart. So he brought the perfume to the Negus and said: "O King, the sanctity of the King in our view, and our obedience to Him, and what is incumbent upon us when we enter His lands and feel safe therein, is that we do not deceive Him or cause Him suspicion. And this companion of mine who is with me has sent messages to your sanctity (حرمتك) and deceived her, and she sent him some of your perfume." Then he placed the perfume before Him. The Negus became angry and intended to kill ‘Umarah. Then He said: "It is not permissible to kill him, for they entered my country under protection (بأمان)." So the Negus summoned the sorcerers (السّحرة) and said to them: "Do something to him that is worse than killing." So they took him and blew mercury (الزيبق) into his urethra (إحليله). He then became [like] the wild beasts, going out in the morning and returning in the evening, and he did not feel comfortable with people.
