After that, Quraysh sent [men] who lay in wait for him in a place until he came to the water with the wild beasts. They seized him, and he continued to struggle in their hands and scream until he died. ‘Amr returned to Quraysh and informed them that Ja‘far was in the land of Abyssinia (الحبشة) enjoying the highest honor (أكرم كرامة). He remained there until the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم made a truce (هادن) with Quraysh and reconciled with them, and conquered Khaybar. Ja‘far arrived with all those who were with him. ‘Abdullah ibn Ja‘far was born to Ja‘far in Abyssinia from Asma bint ‘Umays. A son was also born to the Negus, and the Negus named him Muhammad. Umm Habib bint Abi Sufyan loved ‘Abdullah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم wrote to the Negus proposing marriage to Umm Habib. The Negus sent for her and proposed marriage to her on behalf of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. She accepted, so He proposed to her and married her to Him, giving her a dowry (أصدقها) of four hundred dinars. He delivered her on behalf of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, sent her many clothes and perfume, equipped her, and sent her to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He also sent Him Mariyah the Copt, the mother of Ibrahim, and sent Him clothes, perfume, and a horse. And He sent thirty men from the priests (القسيسين) and said to them: "Observe his speech, his sitting place, his drink, and his place of prayer (مصلاه)." When they arrived in Medina, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم invited them to Islam (الإِسلام) and recited the Quran to them, [starting with]: When Allah said, 'O Jesus son of Mary, remember My blessing upon you and upon your mother,' up to His saying: But those of them who were faithless said, 'This is nothing but manifest magic.' When they heard that from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, they wept and believed. They returned to the Negus and informed Him of the news of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and recited to Him what [the Prophet] had recited to them. The Negus wept, and the priests wept, and the Negus embraced Islam (أسلم). He did not reveal his Islam to the Abyssinians, fearing for himself. He departed from the land of Abyssinia, intending [to reach] the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. When He crossed the sea, He passed away. Then Allah revealed to His Messenger: You will surely find the most hostile of all people toward the faithful to be the Jews up to His saying: And that is the reward of the virtuous.

(٨٦) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَحِيمِ (86) But those who are faithless and deny Our signs—they shall be the inmates of the Hellfire. (٨٧) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُحَرِّمُوا ** لا تمنعوا أنفسكم طَيِّباتِ ما أَحَلَّ اللهُ لَكُمْ ** ما طاب منه ولذ وَلا تَعْتَدُوا ** عما حدّ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ ** (87) O you who have faith, do not forbid the good things that Allah has made lawful for you, and do not transgress; indeed Allah does not like the transgressors.

In al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This verse was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), Bilal, and Uthman ibn Maz'un. As for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he swore that he would never sleep at night. As for Bilal, he swore that he would never break his fast during the day. And as for Uthman (1) ibn Maz'un, he swore that he would never marry.

And al-Qummi added: The wife of Uthman entered upon Aisha, and she was a beautiful woman. Aisha said: "Why do I see you unadorned (متعطلة)?" She replied: "And for whom should I adorn myself? By Allah, my husband has not approached me for such and such a time, for he has become a monk (ترهّب), worn sackcloth (المسوح), and renounced the world (زهد في الدنيا)." When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered, Aisha informed Him of that. So He went out and called: "The prayer is comprehensive (الصّلوة جامعه)." The people gathered, and He ascended the pulpit (المنبر). He praised Allah and extolled Him, then He said: "What is wrong with people who forbid good things (الطّيّبات) upon themselves? I sleep at night, I marry, and I break my fast during the day. Whoever turns away from my tradition (سنّتي) is not from me." So these men stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah, we have sworn an oath to that effect." Then Allah revealed: **لا يُؤاخِذُكُمُ اللهُ بِاللَّغْوِ فِي أَيْمانِكُمْ ** — the verse.

I say: There is no deficiency (منقصة) in such an address (الخطاب) and admonishment (العتاب) directed at the addressed and admonished, if it is not a praise (محمدة). An example of this is His saying, the Almighty: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ ما أَحَلَّ اللهُ لَكَ تَبْتَغِي مَرْضاتِ أَزْواجِكَ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ قَدْ فَرَضَ اللهُ لَكُمْ تَحِلَّةَ أَيْمانِكُمْ وَاللهُ مَوْلاكُمْ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيمُ الْحَكِيمُ ** (1) O Prophet! Why do you forbid [yourself] what Allah has made lawful for you, seeking to please your wives? And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (2) Allah has certainly prescribed for you the absolution of your oaths, and Allah is your Guardian, and He is the All-knowing, the All-wise. And the entire Quran has been revealed as a reprimand (تقريع), yet its inner meaning is drawing near (تقريب).

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Hasan ibn Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them both), in a tradition: He said to Mu'awiyah and his companions: "I adjure you by Allah, do you know that Ali (peace be upon him) was the first among the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to forbid pleasures (الشّهوات) upon himself, so Allah revealed: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُحَرِّمُوا طَيِّباتِ ما أَحَلَّ اللهُ لَكُمْ. (O you who have faith, do not forbid the good things that Allah has made lawful for you.)"

footnote (١) Uthman ibn Maz'un was the first Companion (*صحابي*) to die in Medina.

(٨٨) وَكُلُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ حَلالاً طَيِّباً ** (88) And eat of what Allah has provided you as lawful and good, permissible (مباحاً) and delicious (لذيذاً). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ الَّذِي أَنْتُمْ بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ ** and be wary of Allah, in whom you are believers. This is an invitation to piety (التقوى) through the gentlest means (بألطف الوجوه).

(٨٩) لا يُؤاخِذُكُمُ اللهُ بِاللَّغْوِ فِي أَيْمانِكُمْ ** (89) Allah will not take you to task for the frivolous [oath] in your oaths, which is what appears without intention. In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the saying of a man, "No, by Allah," and "Yes, by Allah," without intending to bind himself to anything (ولا يعقد على شيء). وَلكِنْ يُؤاخِذُكُمْ بِما عَقَّدْتُمُ الْأَيْمانَ ** but He will take you to task for what you have sworn to in earnest. for what you have confirmed the oaths upon with intention (بالقصد) and purpose (والنية), meaning, if you break them (إذا حنثتم). This was omitted because it is known. And the reading (وقرء) is عقدتم (with takhfif—lightened) and عاقدتم. فَكَفَّارَتُهُ ** Its atonement So, the atonement for breaking it (نكثه), meaning the action (الفعلة) that removes its sin (إثمه) and covers it (وتستره), إِطْعامُ عَشَرَةِ مَساكِينَ مِنْ أَوْسَطِ ما تُطْعِمُونَ أَهْلِيكُمْ. is feeding ten needy persons with the average of what you feed your families, In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He read it as أهاليكم (your households). أَوْ كِسْوَتُهُمْ ** or clothing them, In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The average (الوسط) is vinegar (الخلّ) and olives (الزّيتون), and the highest (ارفعه) is bread (الخبز) and meat (اللحم). The charity (الصدقة) is a mudd (مدّ) of wheat (حنطة) for every needy person (مسكين), and the clothing (الكسوة) is two garments (ثوبان). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is as it happens: there may be someone in the house who eats more than a mudd (المدّ), and some who eat less than a mudd (المدّ), so it is the average between that. And if you wish, you may provide him with a condiment (أدماً). The lowest of the condiments (الأدم) is salt (ملح), the average is vinegar (الخلّ) and oil (الزّيت), and the highest is meat (اللحم). And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [It is] what you feed your dependents (عيالكم) from the average of that. He was asked: "And what is the average of that?" He said: "Vinegar (الخلّ), oil (الزّيت), dates (التّمر), and bread (الخبز), satisfying them with it once." He was asked about their clothing (كسوتهم). He said: "One garment (ثوب واحد)." And in a narration: "A garment (ثوب) with which he covers his private parts (عورته)." I say: The two garments (الثوبان) mentioned in the preceding narration are thus interpreted as applying when one garment does not cover them (لم يوارها الواحد). أَوْ تَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ ** or freeing a slave. The freeing (عتق) of a male or female slave (عبد أو أمة). And the newborn (المولود) is permissible, as narrated in al-Kafi from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Everything in the Quran [where the word] أو (meaning the word أو) appears, its owner (صاحبه) has a choice (بالخيار) regarding it, and he chooses what He wills. And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him). فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيامُ ثَلاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ. But whoever cannot afford it, let him fast for three days. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the atonement for an oath (كفّارة اليمين): "What is the limit (حَدُّ) for one who cannot afford it (لم يجد)? Indeed, a man begs with his hand (يسأل في كفِّهِ) while he can afford it (وهو يجد)." He replied: "If he does not have a surplus (فضل) beyond the sustenance (قوت) of his dependents (عياله), then he is among those who cannot afford it (ممن لا يجد)." And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Every fast (صوم) may be separated (يفرق فيه) except for the three days in the atonement for the oath (كفّارة اليمين). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The fasting of three days in the atonement for the oath (كفّارة اليمين) must be consecutive (متتابعات), without separation between them. ذلِكَ كَفَّارَةُ أَيْمانِكُمْ إِذا حَلَفْتُمْ ** That is the atonement for your oaths when you have sworn. meaning, you have sworn and broken the oath (حَنَثتم) (1). وَاحْفَظُوا أَيْمانَكُمْ ** And keep your oaths. Be truthful (بَرّوا) (2) in them as much as you are able, and do not break them (لا تحنثوا), and do not offer them for every matter (لكل أمرٍ), or make atonement (كفّروا) if you break them, or all of the above. كَذلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمْ آياتِهِ ** Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you the signs (أعلام) of His laws (شرايعه), so that you may be grateful for the blessing of teaching (التّعليم) and clarification (التّبيين).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Oaths (الأيمان) are of three types: an oath for which there is no atonement (كفّارة), an oath (3) for which there is atonement, and the plunging oath (يمين غموس) which necessitates the Fire. The oath for which there is no atonement is when a man swears concerning an act of righteousness (باب برّ) that he will not perform it, and its atonement is that he performs it. The oath for which atonement is obligatory is when a man swears concerning an act of disobedience (باب معصية) that he will not perform it, but then performs it, so atonement becomes obligatory upon him. The plunging oath (اليمين الغموس) which necessitates the Fire is when a man swears concerning the right (حقّ) of a Muslim man, intending to withhold his property (على حبس ماله). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever swears an oath, and then sees that something else is better than it, and performs that [better thing], then that is the atonement for his oath (كفّارة يمينه). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whatever you swear concerning that which involves righteousness (البرّ), atonement is upon you if you do not fulfill it (لم تف به). And whatever you swear concerning that which involves disobedience (المعصية), atonement is not upon you if you retract it (رجعت عنه). And whatever is besides that, which involves neither righteousness nor disobedience, is nothing (فليس بشيء). And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): There is no breaking of the oath (حنث) and no atonement (كفّارة) upon one who swears an oath out of dissimulation (تقيّة), thereby warding off oppression (ظلماً) from himself. And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): There is no valid oath (لا يمين) for a child in the presence of his parent, nor for a woman in the presence of her husband.

(1) Al-Masḥ (المسح) with kasrah followed by sukūn is the singular of al-Musūḥ (المسوح), and it is expressed by al-Bilās (البلاس), which is a known coarse garment (كساء معروف).

(1) Al-Ḥinth (الحِنث) with kasrah is sin (الإثم) and breaking the oath (الخلف في اليمين).

(2) Al-Birr (البرّ) is truthfulness (الصّدق) in the oath, and it can be pronounced with kasrah (ويكسر). One says barirtu (برِرَت) and barartu (بَرَرت), and the oath barrat (برّتِ) yatabirru (يَتَبر) and tabirru (تَبِرّ), like yamallu (يملُّ) and yaḥillu (يحل), meaning birran (بَرّاً) and birran (بِرّاً) and burūran (بُرُوراً). And abarrūhā (وَابَرِّها) means he executed it upon truthfulness (أمضاها على الصّدق).

(3) In the Hadith: The plunging oath (اليمين الغموس) is that which leaves the dwellings desolate (تذر الدّيار بلاقع). The plunging oath (اليمين الغَموس) with fatḥah on the ghayn (بفتح الغين) is the lying, wicked oath (اليمين الكاذبة الفاجرة) by which the swearer cuts off the property of another, while knowing that the matter is otherwise. There is no atonement (كفّارة) for it due to the severity of the sin (شدّة الذّنب) involved. It was named thus because it plunges (تغمس) its taker into sin (الإِثم) and then into the Fire. It is a fa'ūl (فعول) form for emphasis (للمبالغة). And in it [the Hadith]: The plunging oath (اليمين الغموس) is that whose punishment is entry into the Fire, and it is when a man swears concerning the property (مال) of a Muslim man or his right (حقّه) unjustly (ظلماً).

(90) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالْأَنْصابُ وَالْأَزْلامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (O you who have faith! Indeed, intoxicants and gambling, idols and the divining arrows are but a defilement of Satan’s doing, so shun it, so that you may be felicitous.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When this verse was revealed, it was said: "O Messenger of Allah, what is الْمَيْسِرُ (gambling)?" He replied: "Everything upon which you stand [to gamble], even the dice (الكعاب) and the walnuts (الجوز)." It was asked: "Then what are الْأَنْصابُ (idols)?" He said: "What they slaughtered for their deities." It was asked: "Then what are الْأَزْلامُ (divining arrows)?" He said: "Their arrows (قداحهم) with which they seek to divine the future (يستقسمون بها)."

I say: Another narration concerning the interpretation of الْأَنْصابُ وَالْأَزْلامُ has already passed at the beginning of the Surah. In this verse, there are various forms of emphasis regarding the prohibition of intoxicants (الخمر) and gambling (الميسر). Narrations concerning this have already passed at the Almighty's saying: وَيَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ (And they ask you concerning intoxicants and gambling) from Surah al-Baqarah.

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), regarding this verse: As for الْخَمْرُ (intoxicants), it is every intoxicating drink (مسكر من الشراب); if it is left to ferment (خمّر) (1), then it is khamr. That whose large amount intoxicates, its small amount is forbidden (haram). This is because Abu Bakr drank before khamr was forbidden, became intoxicated, and began reciting poetry and weeping over the slain polytheists from the people of Badr. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم heard this and said: "O Allah, restrain his tongue." So, it was restrained, and he did not speak until the intoxication left him. Thereafter, Allah revealed its prohibition. When khamr was forbidden in Medina, it was the fadhikh (a drink made from unripe dates (البُسر) and dates (التّمر)). When its prohibition was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم went out and sat in the Mosque, then called for their vessels in which they used to steep the dates (ينبذون فيها), and He overturned (فكفأها) (1) all of them, saying: "All of this is khamr, and Allah has forbidden it." The fadhikh was the most common drink overturned that day. I do not know of anything made from grape khamr that was overturned that day, except for one vessel which contained both raisins (زبيب) and dates (تمر) together. As for grape juice (عصير العنب), there was none of it in Medina that day. Allah has forbidden khamr, its small amount and its large amount, its sale, its purchase, and benefiting from it. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever drinks khamr, flog him. If he returns [to it], flog him. If he returns [to it], flog him. If he returns [to it] a fourth time, kill him." He also said: "It is incumbent upon Allah that whoever drinks khamr shall be given to drink from what issues forth from the private parts (فروج) of the marked prostitutes (الموسمات المومسات)." The mūmisāt (المومسات) are the adulteresses (الزّواني). Pus (صديد) issues forth from their private parts, and sadīd (الصديد) is thick, mixed pus and blood, whose heat and stench harm the people of the Fire. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever drinks khamr, his prayer is not accepted for forty nights. If he returns [to it], then [it is] forty nights from the day he drank it. If he dies during those forty [nights] without repentance, Allah will give him to drink on the Day of Resurrection from the mud of khabāl (طينة خبال)." (الخبال is corruption (الفساد)). The Mosque where the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sat on the day the drinks were overturned was named the Mosque of al-Fadhikh (مسجد الفضيخ) from that day forward, because fadhikh was the most common drink overturned.

As for الْمَيْسِرُ (gambling), it is backgammon (النّرد), chess (الشطرنج), and every form of gambling (قمار) is maysir. As for الْأَنْصابُ (idols), they are the idols (الأوثان) that the polytheists used to worship. As for الْأَزْلامُ (divining arrows), they are the arrows (القداح) that the polytheists of the Arabs used for divination (يستقسم بها) regarding affairs during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية). The sale, purchase, and benefiting from any of this is forbidden (haram) and prohibited by Allah, and it is رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطانِ (a defilement of Satan’s doing). And Allah linked khamr and maysir with the idols (الأوثان).

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم cursed ten concerning khamr: its planter (غارسها), its guard (حارسها), its presser (عاصرها), its drinker (شاربها), its server (ساقيها), its carrier (حاملها), the one to whom it is carried (المحمول إليه), its seller (بايعها), its buyer (مشتريها), and the consumer of its price (آكل ثمنها).

(1) Narrated from Ibn al-A'rabi: Al-Khamr (الخمر) was only named khamr because it was left and fermented (اختمرت), and its fermentation (اختمارها) is the change of its smell. It is also said that it was named thus because it mixes with (لمخامرتها) the intellect (العقل), and al-takhmīr (التخمير) means covering (التغطية).

(٩١) **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ الشَّيْطانُ أَنْ يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ الْعَداوَةَ وَالْبَغْضاءَ فِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلاةِ فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ ** (91) The Devil only seeks to cast enmity and hatred among you through wine and gambling, and to bar you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. Will you not then desist?

It was said that He only specified wine (الخمر) and gambling (الميسر) by repeating their mention and explaining the calamity (الوبال) within them, as an alert that they are the intended subject of the clarification. The mention of the idols (الأنصاب) and the divining arrows (الأزلام) was to indicate that they (wine and gambling) are like them in prohibition (الحرمة) and evil (الشرارة), similar to the saying of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "The drinker of wine is like the worshipper of an idol (الوثن)." And He specified the prayer (الصّلوة) from the remembrance (الذكر) by singling it out for magnification (للتعظيم) and to indicate that the one who bars others from it (الصاد عنها) is like the one who bars others from faith (الإِيمان), given that it is its pillar (عماده) and the differentiator between it and disbelief (الكفر). Then He repeated the urging to desist (الحثّ على الانتهاءِ) using the interrogative form (بصيغة الاستفهام), structured upon the preceding types of deterrents (الصوارف), signifying that the matter of prohibition and warning has reached its limit (بلغ الغاية) and that excuses (الأعذار) have been cut off.

footnote (١) كفأه كمنعه صَرَفه وكتبه وقلّبه كأكفأه واكتفاه.

(٩٢) وَأَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَاحْذَرُوا ** (92) Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and beware. concerning what They (Allah and the Messenger) have forbidden, or concerning opposing Them both. فَإِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّما عَلى رَسُولِنَا الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ But if you turn away, then know that Our Apostle is only responsible for a manifest delivery. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: "By Allah, those who perished before you did not perish, nor will those who perish until our Qā’im (قائمنا) rises, except due to abandoning our Wilāyah (ولايتنا) and denying our right (حقنا). And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not depart from the world until He made our right (حقنا) binding upon the necks of this Ummah (الأمّة)." وَاللهُ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ.** (93) And Allah guides whomever He wishes to a straight path.

(٩٣) لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ جُناحٌ فِيما طَعِمُوا ** (93) There is no sin upon those who have faith and do righteous deeds in regard to what they have eaten concerning the pleasant things (المستلذات), whether it was eating or drinking, for the word al-ta‘m (الطعم) encompasses both of them. In al-Majma‘, in the exegesis (تفسير) of the Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them): فِيما طَعِمُوا ** in regard to what they have eaten of the lawful (الحلال). إِذا مَا اتَّقَوْا وَآمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ ثُمَّ اتَّقَوْا وَآمَنُوا ثُمَّ اتَّقَوْا وَأَحْسَنُوا وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** when they are wary [of Allah] and have faith and do righteous deeds, then are wary [of Allah] and have faith, and then are wary [of Allah] and do good. And Allah loves the good-doers. Al-Qummi said: When the prohibition of wine (الخمر) and gambling (الميسر) was revealed, and the matter concerning them was made severe, the people among the Muhajirun (المهاجرين) and the Ansar (الأنصار) said: "O Messenger of Allah, our companions were killed while they were drinking wine, and Allah the Almighty has called it an abomination (رجساً) and made it part of the work of Satan (الشيطان), and You have said what You have said. Will that harm our companions in any way after they have died?" So Allah revealed this verse. This applies to whoever died or was killed before the prohibition of wine. The al-junah (الجناح) is the sin (الإثم), and it is upon whoever drank it after the prohibition. And it was said: in regard to what they have eaten, meaning: from what was not forbidden to them, when they are wary [of Allah], meaning: wary of the forbidden (المحرم), and have faith and do righteous deeds, meaning: they remained firm upon faith (الإِيمان) and righteous deeds (الأعمال الصالحات), then are wary [of Allah], meaning: wary of what was forbidden to them afterwards, like wine, and had faith (وَآمَنُوا) in its prohibition, and then are wary [of Allah], meaning: they continued and remained firm upon avoiding acts of disobedience (المعاصي), and do good, meaning: they sought out beautiful deeds (الأعمال الجميلة) and occupied themselves with them.

I say: Since both faith (الإِيمان) and wariness (التقوى) have degrees (درجات) and stations (منازل), as has been narrated from them (peace be upon them), it is not unlikely that their repetition in the verse is a reference to those degrees and stations. Thus, in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Faith (الإيمان) has states (حالات), degrees (درجات), levels (طبقات), and stations (منازل). Some of it is complete, reaching its perfection; some of it is deficient, its deficiency being manifest; and some of it is preponderant, its preponderance being excessive." And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The believers (المؤمنين) are in stations (منازل). Some of them are at one, some at two, some at three, some at four, some at five, some at six, and some at seven. If you were to try to impose two [degrees] upon the one who has one, he would not be able; and upon the one who has two, three, he would not be able." He then continued the narration and said: "And [they are] upon these degrees (الدرجات)." And in Misbah al-Shari‘ah, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Wariness (التقوى) is of three types: Wariness in Allah (تقوى في الله), which is abandoning the lawful (الحلال), let alone the doubtful (الشبهة); this is the wariness of the elite of the elite (تقوى خاصّ الخاصّ). Wariness of Allah (تقوى من الله), which is abandoning the doubtful things (الشبهات), let alone the forbidden (الحرام); this is the wariness of the elite (تقوى الخاصّ). And wariness out of fear of the Fire (النار) and punishment (العقاب), which is abandoning the forbidden; this is the wariness of the general populace (تقوى العام). The parable of wariness is like water flowing in a river. And the parable of these three levels (الطبقات) in the meaning of wariness is like trees planted on the bank of that river, of every color and species. Each tree draws water from that river according to its essence (جوهر), nature (طبع), subtlety (لطافته), and density (كثافته). Then the benefits for creation (الخلق) derived from those trees and fruits are according to their measure and value." Allah the Almighty said: صِنْوانٌ وَغَيْرُ صِنْوانٍ يُسْقى بِماءٍ واحِدٍ وَنُفَضِّلُ بَعْضَها عَلى بَعْضٍ فِي الْأُكُلِ ** (9) and clusters (of dates), some of them paired and some unpaired, irrigated with the same water, and We favour some of them over others in taste. So, wariness (التقوى) is to acts of obedience (الطاعات) like water is to trees. And the parable of the natures (طبائع) of the trees in their color and taste is like the measures (مقادير) of faith (الإِيمان). Whoever is higher in degree of faith and purer in essence (جوهر) through the spirit (الرّوح) is more wary (أتقى). And whoever is more wary, his worship (عبادته) is more sincere (أخلص) and purer (أطهر). And whoever is like that is closer to Allah. Every act of worship not founded upon wariness is scattered dust (هباء منثور). And Allah the Almighty said: أَفَمَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيانَهُ عَلى تَقْوى مِنَ اللهِ وَرِضْوانٍ خَيْرٌ أَمْ مَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيانَهُ عَلى شَفا جُرُفٍ هارٍ فَانْهارَ بِهِ فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ ** (109) Is he who founded his building on the fear of Allah and His pleasure better, or he who founded his building on the brink of a collapsing bank, which collapsed with him into the fire of hell? His (peace be upon him) speech concluded. So we say in explanation of that: Indeed, the initial degrees (أوائل درجات) of faith (الإِيمان) are affirmations (تصديقات) mixed with doubts (الشّبه) and uncertainties (الشكوك), varying in their ranks, and polytheism (الشرك) is possible alongside them, as He, glory be to Him, said: وَما يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِاللهِ إِلَّا وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ ** (106) Most of them do not have faith in Allah without associating [others] with Him. And this is expressed as Islam (الإِسلام), as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: قالَتِ الْأَعْرابُ آمَنَّا قُلْ لَمْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَلكِنْ قُولُوا أَسْلَمْنا وَلَمَّا يَدْخُلِ الْإِيمانُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ ** (14) The Bedouins say, 'We have faith.' Say, 'You do not have faith; rather, say, "We submit," for faith has not yet entered your hearts.' And the wariness (التّقوى) that precedes this is the wariness of the general populace (تقوى العامّ). And the middle degrees (أوسطها) are affirmations that are not mixed with doubt (شكّ) or uncertainty (شبهة), as He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَرْتابُوا ** (15) those who have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and then have not doubted. And the application of the term faith (الإِيمان) is mostly specific to this degree, as He said: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ إِذا ذُكِرَ اللهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَإِذا تُلِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُهُ زادَتْهُمْ إِيماناً وَعَلى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ ** (2) The faithful are only those whose hearts tremble when Allah is mentioned, and when His signs are recited to them, they increase their faith, and who put their trust in their Lord. And the wariness that precedes this is the wariness of the elite (تقوى الخاص). And the final degrees (أواخرها) are affirmations likewise, accompanied by witnessing (شهود), direct observation (عيان), and complete love (محبة كاملة) for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, as He said: He loves them and they love Him. And this is sometimes expressed as al-ihsan (الإِحسان), as occurred in the Prophetic tradition صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Al-Ihsan (الإِحسان) is that you worship Allah as if you see Him." And sometimes it is expressed as certainty (الإيقان), as He said: وَبِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ ** (4) and are certain of the Hereafter. And the wariness that precedes this is the wariness of the elite of the elite (تقوى خاصّ الخاصّ). Wariness (التقوى) was only placed before faith (الإِيمان) because faith is attained and strengthened only through wariness, for whenever wariness increases, faith increases according to its increase. This does not contradict the precedence of the root of faith over wariness; rather, the increase of wariness is also according to the increase of faith, because the preceding degree (الدرجة المتقدّمة) for each of them is different from the succeeding degree (الدرجة المتأخّرة). The parable of this is like someone walking with a lamp (سراج) in darkness (ظلمة): whenever a section of the path is illuminated for him, he walks upon it, and that walking then becomes a cause for the illumination of another section of it, and so on. And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Umar brought Qudamah ibn Maz‘un, who had drunk wine (الخمر), and proof (البيّنة) was established against him. He asked the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who ordered him to be lashed eighty times. Qudamah said: 'O Commander of the Faithful, there is no prescribed punishment (حَدٌّ) upon me. I am one of the people of this verse: لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ جُناحٌ فِيما طَعِمُوا ** There is no sin upon those who have faith and do righteous deeds in regard to what they have eaten.' He said: Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him) said: 'You are not one of its people. The food of its people is lawful (حلال) for them; they do not eat or drink except what Allah has made lawful for them.' Then Ali (peace be upon him) said: 'Indeed, when the drinker drinks, he does not know what he eats or what he drinks. So lash him eighty times.'"

I say: In his (peace be upon him) saying: "except what Allah has made lawful for them," there is an alert that they abstain from doubtful things (الشّبهات), indeed, from everything that prevents them from witnessing (الشهود) with Allah. And the al-junah (الجناح) in the verse is an indefinite noun (نكرة) in the context of negation (سياق النفي), encompassing its lowest degrees, such as deserving punishment (العقاب). The secret in this is that the gratitude (شكر) for the favors (نعم) of Allah the Almighty is that they be spent in obedience (طاعة) to Allah, glory be to Him, in their proper manner. So let this be contemplated. And based on what we have established, if it is correct that the reason for the revelation (سبب نزول) of this verse is what Al-Qummi mentioned, in agreement with a group of exegetes (المفسّرين), then the meaning of the verse is that those who used to drink wine before the revelation of its prohibition, if they were at this level (المثابة) of faith, wariness, and righteous deeds, then there is no sin (جناح) upon them for drinking it.

(٩٤) **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ اللهُ بِشَيْءٍ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ تَنالُهُ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَرِماحُكُمْ ** (94) O you who have faith! Allah will surely test you with some game that your hands and spears can reach.

It means: while you are in the state of Ihrām (إحرام). He drew attention, by His saying بِشَيْءٍ (with some thing), to its insignificance in comparison to the trial (الابتلاء) involving the sacrifice (بَذْل) of souls (الأنفس) and wealth (الأموال).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It was revealed concerning the Umrah (عمرة) of Al-Hudaybiyyah (الحديبية). Allah gathered the game upon them, and they entered among their baggage (رحالهم).

And in al-Kāfī, narrated from Al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): The game was gathered upon them in every place until it drew near to them, so that Allah might test them with it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The wild animals (الوحوش) were gathered for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم during the Umrah (عمرة) of Al-Hudaybiyyah (الحديبية), until their hands and spears could reach them.

And in a narration: That which the hands can reach (تناله الأيدي) is the eggs (البيض) and the fledglings (الفراخ), and that which the spears can reach (تناله الرّماح) is that which the hands cannot reach. And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): That which the hands can reach (تناله الأيدي) is the fledglings of the birds (فراخ الطير), the small wild animals (صغار الوحش), and the eggs (البيض); and that which the spears can reach (تناله الرّماح) is the large game (الكبار من الصيد).

**لِيَعْلَمَ اللهُ مَنْ يَخافُهُ بِالْغَيْبِ ** that Allah may know who fears Him in secret.

So that the one who fears the punishment of the Hereafter (عقاب الآخرة)—while it is unseen (غائب) and awaited (منتظر)—may be distinguished, and thus he avoids the game (الصيد), from the one who does not fear Him and proceeds to hunt it.

فَمَنِ اعْتَدى بَعْدَ ذلِكَ فَلَهُ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. Whoever transgresses after that, there is a painful punishment for him.

(95) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَقْتُلُوا الصَّيْدَ وَأَنْتُمْ حُرُمٌ ** (O you who have faith! Do not kill game while you are in pilgrim sanctity.) — meaning محرمون (in the state of ihram). In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "When you enter the state of ihram, beware of killing all beasts, except the viper (الأفعى), the scorpion (العقرب), and the mouse (الفأرة), for it (1) weakens the waterskin (السقاء) and sets fire to the house upon its inhabitants. As for the scorpion, the Prophet of Allah extended His hand toward a stone, and a scorpion stung Him, so He said: 'May Allah curse you! You spare neither the righteous (برّاً) nor the wicked (فاجراً).' If the snake (الحيّة) intends to harm you, kill it; but if it does not intend to harm you, do not repel it. If the rabid dog (الكلب العقور) and the beast of prey (السبع) intend to harm you, kill them; but if they do not intend to harm you, do not repel them. And the great black snake (الأسود العِذْر) (2), kill it in all circumstances. Stone the crow (الغراب) and the kite (الحدأة) (3) while you are on the back of your camel." And in al-Kafi: that which is similar in meaning. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The muhrim may kill the wasp (الزّنبور), the eagle (النسر), the great black snake (الأسود العِذْر), the wolf (الذّئب), and anything he fears might attack him. And He said: The rabid dog (الكلب العقور) is the wolf (الذّئب). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Everything the muhrim fears for himself from the beasts of prey (السباع) and snakes (الحيّات), he should kill it; but if it does not intend to harm you, do not repel it. وَمَنْ قَتَلَهُ مِنْكُمْ مُتَعَمِّداً فَجَزاءٌ مِثْلُ ما قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ ** (And whoever of you kills it intentionally, the atonement is a parity of what he has killed, in livestock,) And it was recited (فَجَزاءٌ) with idafa (الإِضافة) (as فَجَزاءُ مِثْلِ). In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation: For the gazelle (الظبي), a sheep (شاة); for the wild ass (حمار وحش), a cow (بقرة); and for the ostrich (النعامة), a camel (جزور). And He added in another narration: And for the cow (البقرة), a cow (بقرة). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that which is close to this. يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ ** (to be judged by two just men among you,) In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): (ذو عدل) (one just man). And in al-Kafi, narrated from them (peace be upon them), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The just man (العدل) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Imam after Him. Then He said: "This is among the things where the scribes (الكتاب) erred." And al-Ayyashi added: "Meaning one man (رجلاً واحداً), meaning the Imam." I say: This means that the drawing of the alif (الألف) in ذَوا عَدْلٍ ** is an error resulting from the action of the copyists (نساخ) of the Quran, and the correct thing is not to copy it, because it implies that the judge is two, whereas in reality, He is one: the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) in His time, and then every Imam (peace be upon him) in His time, successively. In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The just man (العدل) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Imam after Him. يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ** (to be judged by Him), and He is one just man (ذو عدل). So, when you know what the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Imam (peace be upon him) have judged, that is sufficient for you, and do not ask about it. هَدْياً بالِغَ الْكَعْبَةِ. (as an offering delivered to the Ka‘bah.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever is obligated to offer a sacrificial animal (هدي) during his ihram may slaughter it wherever he wishes, except for the ransom (فداء) for game, for Allah says: هَدْياً بالِغَ الْكَعْبَةِ ** (as an offering delivered to the Ka‘bah)." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever is obligated to offer a sacrificial animal (هدي) as ransom for game he killed while he was muhrim, if he is performing Hajj (حاجّاً), he slaughters the sacrificial animal that is incumbent upon him in Mina (منى); and if he is performing Umrah (معتمر), he slaughters it in Mecca, facing the Ka‘bah." And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) similarly, and He added: "And if he wishes, he may postpone it until he arrives, then he buys it, for that suffices for him." أَوْ كَفَّارَةٌ طَعامُ مَساكِينَ ** (or an expiation, feeding the needy,) And it was recited (كفارة طعام) with idafa (الإِضافة) (as كفارةُ طعامِ مساكين). أَوْ عَدْلُ ذلِكَ صِياماً. (or its equivalent in fasting.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a muhrim who killed an ostrich (نعامة) or a wild ass (حمار وحش). He said: "A camel (بدنة) is incumbent upon him." It was asked: "What if he cannot afford a camel?" He said: "Then let him feed sixty needy people (مسكيناً)." It was asked: "What if he cannot afford to give charity (يتصدّق)?" He said: "Then let him fast eighteen days. The charity (الصدقة) is one mudd (مدّ) for every needy person." He was asked about a muhrim who killed a cow (بقرة). He said: "A cow (بقرة) is incumbent upon him." It was asked: "What if he cannot afford a cow?" He said: "Then let him feed thirty needy people." It was asked: "What if he cannot afford to give charity?" He said: "Then let him fast (1) nine days." It was asked: "What if he killed a small bird (طيباً)?" He said: "A sheep (شاة) is incumbent upon him." It was asked: "What if he cannot afford it?" He said: "Then feeding ten needy people. If he does not find anything with which to give charity, then he must fast three days." In al-Faqih and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) in the hadith of al-Zuhri: "Do you know how the equivalent (عَدْلُ ذلِكَ) in fasting (صِياماً) is calculated, O Zuhri?" He said: "I do not know." He said: "The game is valued (يقوم) in currency, then that value is distributed (2) as wheat (البرّ). Then that wheat is measured in sa's (أصواعاً), and he fasts one day for every half a sa' (نصف صاع)." لِيَذُوقَ وَبالَ أَمْرِهِ ** (so that he may taste the consequence of his deed.) — meaning this penalty, لِيَذُوقَ ** (so that he may taste) the burden of his action and the evil consequence of his violation of the sanctity of ihram (الإحرام). عَفَا اللهُ عَمَّا سَلَفَ ** (Allah has excused what is past.) — meaning the first instance (of killing). وَمَنْ عادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللهُ مِنْهُ وَاللهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انْتِقامٍ. (But whoever reverts, Allah shall take vengeance on him, and Allah is all-mighty, Avenger.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding a muhrim who killed game. He said: "The expiation (الكفّارة) is incumbent upon him." It was asked: "What if he kills another?" He said: "If he kills another, there is no expiation incumbent upon him, and he is one of those about whom Allah the Almighty said: وَمَنْ عادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللهُ مِنْهُ (But whoever reverts, Allah shall take vengeance on him)." And there are other narrations with this meaning. In al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): If the muhrim kills game by mistake (خطأ), the expiation is incumbent upon him. If he kills a second time by mistake, the expiation is always incumbent upon him, provided it was a mistake. But if he kills it intentionally (متعمّداً), the expiation is incumbent upon him. If he kills it a second time intentionally, then he is one of those about whom فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللهُ مِنْهُ ** (Allah shall take vengeance on him) is said, and the expiation is not incumbent upon him. In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَمَنْ عادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللهُ مِنْهُ ** (But whoever reverts, Allah shall take vengeance on him). He said: "A man went out while he was muhrim and caught a fox (ثعلباً). He began to bring fire close to its face, and the fox began to cry out and defecate from its anus (اسْتِهِ) (3). His companions began to forbid him from what he was doing. Then he released it after that. While the man was sleeping, a snake came and entered his mouth and did not leave him until he began to defecate just as the fox had defecated. Then it left him."

(٩٦) أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعامُهُ مَتاعاً لَكُمْ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ ** (96) Lawful for you is the game of the sea and its food, as a provision for you and for the travellers. And for your travelers (السيارة), they take it as provision, dried (قديداً). (1) وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَرِّ ما دُمْتُمْ حُرُماً ** and forbidden to you is the game of the land as long as you are in pilgrim sanctity. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: There is no harm for the one in pilgrim sanctity (المحرم) to hunt fish, and to eat its salted (مالحه) (2) and its fresh (طريه) (3) [parts], and to take provision. And He said regarding: أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعامُهُ مَتاعاً لَكُمْ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ ** (Lawful for you is the game of the sea and its food, as a provision for you and for the travellers.) He said: It is its salted [part] (مالحه) which they eat. And He distinguished between the two [types of game]: Every bird that is in the thickets (الآجام) (4), lays eggs in the land (البر) and hatches in the land (البر), then it is from the game of the land (صيد البر). And whatever was from the game of the land (صيد البر) that is in the land (البر) but lays eggs in the sea (البحر), then it is from the game of the sea (صيد البحر). And narrated from him عليه السلام: Everything whose origin is in the sea, and is found in both the land (البر) and the sea (البحر), it is not proper for the one in pilgrim sanctity (المحرم) to kill it. If he kills it, then the compensation (الجزاء) is upon him, as Allah the Almighty said. And narrated from one of the two of them عليهما السلام: He said: The one in pilgrim sanctity (المحرم) does not eat water fowl (طير الماء). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ. and be wary of Allah, toward Whom you will be mustered.**

(٩٧) جَعَلَ اللهُ الْكَعْبَةَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرامَ قِياماً ** (97) Allah has made the Ka‘bah, the Sacred House, a sanctuary And it was read قيماً without the alif. لِلنَّاسِ ** for mankind, for their livelihoods (مَعَايِشِهِمْ) and earnings (مَكَاسِبِهِمْ). Through it, the affairs of their religion and their worldly life are set right. The fearful seek refuge in it, the weak feel secure within it, and the merchants profit near it due to their gathering there from all directions. The sinner is forgiven by intending it, and its pilgrim achieves rewards.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever comes to this House desiring something in this world or the Hereafter shall attain it." And al-Qummi said: "As long as the Ka‘bah remains standing and people perform the Hajj to it, they will not perish. But if it is demolished and they abandon the Hajj, they will perish." وَالشَّهْرَ الْحَرامَ وَالْهَدْيَ وَالْقَلائِدَ ** and the sacred month, and the offering, and the garlands. The explanation of these has already passed. ذلِكَ لِتَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** That is so that you may know that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, Meaning: when you become acquainted with the wisdom in making the Ka‘bah a sanctuary (قِيَاماً) and the wisdom contained in the Hajj and its rituals (مَنَاسِكِهِ), you will know that Allah knows all things entirely. وَأَنَّ اللهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ and that Allah has knowledge of all things. This is a generalization after a specification, and an emphasis after a general statement.

(٩٨) **اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ وَأَنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (98) Know that Allah is severe in retribution, and that Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

(This is) a threat and a promise for the one who violates His prohibitions (مَحَارِمَهُ) and for the one who safeguards them. In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, from his forefathers عليهم السلام, from the Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, from Gabriel عليه السلام: He said: Allah the Almighty said: "Whoever commits a sin, whether it is small or great, while knowing that it is within My power to punish him or to forgive him, I will forgive him."

footnote (١) القديد اللحم المقدّد أي المشرّح طولاً. (1) al-Qadīd (القديد) is the dried meat (اللحم المقدّد), meaning that which is sliced lengthwise.

footnote (٢) ملح السّمك والقدر طرح فيه الملح. (2) Milḥ al-Samak (ملح السّمك) (salt of the fish) and al-Qidr (القدر) (the pot) is that into which the salt is thrown.

footnote (٣) والطري الغضّ. (3) And al-Tarī (الطري) is the fresh (الغضّ).

footnote (٤) الأجمة محرّكة الشجر الكثير الملتف جمع أجم بالضم وبضمّتين وبالتحريك وآجام واجام وأجَمَات. (4) Al-Ajama (الأجمة) (with taḥrīk) is the abundant, dense trees (الشجر الكثير الملتف). Its plural forms are ujum (أجم) (with ḍamm), ujum (أجم) (with two ḍammas), ajam (أجم) (with taḥrīk), ājām (آجام), ujām (اجام), and ajamāt (أجَمَات).

(٩٩) ما عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلاغُ ** (99) The Apostle is responsible only for the delivery [of the message]. This is an emphasis on the obligation of carrying out what He commanded. وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ ما تُبْدُونَ وَما تَكْتُمُونَ and Allah knows what you disclose and what you conceal, whether it be belief (تصديق) or disbelief (تكذيب), action (فعل) or intention (عزيمة). (١٠٠) قُلْ لا يَسْتَوِي الْخَبِيثُ وَالطَّيِّبُ ** (100) Say, 'The bad and the good are not equal,' whether it be a person, or an action, or wealth, or anything else. وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ كَثْرَةُ الْخَبِيثِ even if the abundance of the bad should impress you. For the consideration (العبرة) lies in quality (الجودة) and poorness (الرّداءة), not in abundance (الكثرة) or scarcity (القلّة). فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ يا أُولِي الْأَلْبابِ ** So be wary of Allah, O people of understanding, regarding the seeking (١) (تحرّي) of the bad (الخبيث), even if it is abundant, and prefer (٢) (اثروا) the good (الطّيّب), even if it is scarce, لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ. so that you may be felicitous.**

(101) **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَسْئَلُوا عَنْ أَشْياءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ وَإِنْ تَسْئَلُوا عَنْها حِينَ يُنَزَّلُ الْقُرْآنُ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ ** (O you who have faith! Do not ask about things which, if disclosed to you, would trouble you. But if you ask about them while the Qur’ān is being sent down, they will be disclosed to you.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): لا تَسْئَلُوا عَنْ أَشْياءَ ** (Do not ask about things) that have not been disclosed to you, إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ.** (which, if disclosed to you, would trouble you.)

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم delivered a sermon and said: "Indeed, Allah has prescribed the Hajj upon you." So, Ukkashah ibn Mihsan—and it is also narrated as Suraqah ibn Malik—said: "Is it every year, O Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم?" He turned away from him until he repeated the question two or three times. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Woe to you! What assures you that I will not say yes? By Allah, if I said yes, it would become obligatory, and if it became obligatory, you would not be able to perform it, and if you abandoned it, you would disbelieve (كفرتم). So, leave me alone as long as I leave you alone. For those who were before you were destroyed only by their excessive questioning and their disagreement with their prophets. Therefore, when I command you to do something, perform of it what you are able, and when I forbid you from something, avoid it."

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That Safiyyah bint Abd al-Muttalib (3) had a son who died, and she approached. Umar said to her: "Cover your earring (قرطك) (4), for your kinship to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم will not benefit you at all." She said to him: "Did you see an earring on me, O son of the foul-smelling woman (يا ابن اللّخناء) (5)?" Then she entered upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, informed Him of that, and wept. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم went out and called: "Prayer is gathering (الصّلوة جامعة)." The people gathered, and He said: "What is wrong with people who claim that my kinship is of no benefit? If I stand at the Praised Station (المقام المحمود), I shall intercede for those outside your ranks. Let no one ask Me today who his father is, but I will inform him."

A man stood up to Him and said: "Who is my father, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Your father is not the one you are called after; your father is so-and-so son of so-and-so (فلان بن فلان)." Then another man stood up and said: "Who is my father, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Your father is the one you are called after." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "What is wrong with the one who claims that my kinship is of no benefit? Why does he not ask Me about his father?" So, Umar stood up to Him and said: "I seek refuge with Allah, O Messenger of Allah, from the wrath of Allah and the wrath of His Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Pardon me, may Allah pardon you." Then Allah revealed يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (O you who have faith!)—the Ayah.

عَفَا اللهُ عَنْها ** (Allah has excused them). It is said that this is a new sentence (استئناف), meaning: Allah has excused what has passed of your questioning, so do not return to the like of it. And it is said that it is another attribute (صفة أخرى), meaning: about things that Allah has excused (عَفَا الله عنها), and He did not obligate them, and He refrained from mentioning them. This is supported by the saying of the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him): "Indeed, Allah has made obligations incumbent upon you, so do not neglect them; and He has set limits for you, so do not transgress them; and He has forbidden you from things, so do not violate them; and He has remained silent concerning things for you, and He did not leave them out of forgetfulness, so do not impose them upon yourselves." وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ ** (And Allah is All-forgiving, All-clement), meaning He does not hasten to punish you for what lapses from you, وَيَعْفُوا عَنْ كَثِيرٍ. (and He excuses much.)

(١٠٢) **قَدْ سَأَلَها قَوْمٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ ثُمَّ أَصْبَحُوا بِها كافِرِينَ ** (102) A people before you have already asked them, and then they became disbelievers in them. since they did not comply and they denied them.

(١٠٣) ما جَعَلَ اللهُ ** Allah has not legislated (ما شرع الله) any مِنْ بَحِيرَةٍ وَلا سائِبَةٍ (٢) وَلا وَصِيلَةٍ وَلا حامٍ ** (103) Allah has not ordained any bahirah, or sa’ibah, or wasilah, or ham. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The people of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) were such that if a she-camel gave birth to two offspring in one womb, they would say, "It has joined (وصلت)," and they would not deem it lawful to slaughter or eat it. And if it gave birth to ten, they would make it a سائِبَةٍ (free-roaming) and would not deem its back (for riding) or its meat lawful. As for the حامٍ (protected male), it was the stud camel, which they did not deem lawful (to use or slaughter). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed that He had not forbidden anything of that. He said: It has also been narrated that the البحيرة (slit-eared) was the she-camel that had produced five offspring. If the fifth was male, they would slaughter it, and the men would eat it. If the fifth was female, they would slit (بحروا) its ear—meaning they would split it—and it would be forbidden (حراماً) to women, both its meat and its milk. If it died, it became lawful for women. The السّائبة (free-roaming) was the camel that was set free (يسيّب) due to a vow (نذر) made by a man that if Allah saved him from illness or brought him to his destination, he would do that. The الوصيلة (joined) was from the sheep: if the ewe gave birth to seven offspring, and the seventh was male, it was slaughtered, and both men and women ate from it. If it was female, it was left among the flock. If it was both male and female, they would say, "It has joined (وصلت) its brother," and it would not be slaughtered. The meat of both was forbidden to women, unless something from it died, in which case it became lawful for men and women to eat. The الحام (protected male) was the stud camel: when it mounted the offspring of its offspring, they would say, "Its back has been protected (قد حمى ظهره)."

It is also narrated that the الحام was from the camels: if it produced ten offspring, they would say, "Its back has been protected (قد حمى ظهره)," so it would not be ridden, nor would it be prevented from pasture (كلاءٍ) or water (ماءٍ). وَلكِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ ** (103) but those who disbelieve fabricate lies against Allah. by forbidding that and attributing it to Him. وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْقِلُونَ ** (103) And most of them do not apply reason. that this is fabrication and a lie—meaning the followers who imitate their leaders in forbidding them, whose love of leadership prevents them from acknowledging (the truth).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: That Luhayy ibn Qam'ah ibn Jundub had ruled Mecca (مكّة) and was the first person to change the religion of Isma'il (إسمعيل). He adopted idols (الأصنام), erected statues (الأوثان), slit the ear of the البحيرة, set free the السّائبة, joined the الوصيلة, and protected the الحامي. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I certainly saw him in the Fire, harming the people of the Fire with the stench of his intestines (قصبه) (1)." And it is narrated: "His intestines (قصبه) are dragged in the Fire."

(١٠٤) **وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعالَوْا إِلى ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ وَإِلَى الرَّسُولِ قالُوا حَسْبُنا ما وَجَدْنا عَلَيْهِ آباءَنا ** (104) When they are told, 'Come to what Allah has sent down and to the Apostle,' they say, 'Sufficient for us is what we found our fathers following.'

A clarification of the deficiency of their intellect and their immersion in traditions, and that they have no support other than it. **أَوَلَوْ كانَ آباؤُهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ شَيْئاً وَلا يَهْتَدُونَ ** (What, even if their fathers did not know anything and were not guided?)

Meaning, what they found their fathers upon obligated them, even if they were ignorant and misguided.

(١٠٥) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ ** (105) O you who have faith! Take care of your own souls. Guard them (your souls) and adhere to their rectification (صلاحها). لا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ ** the misguided will not harm you as long as you are guided. It was said that it was revealed when the believers were grieving over the disbelievers and wishing for their faith.

And al-Qummi said: Rectify your souls, do not follow the faults (عورات) of the people, and do not mention them, for their misguidance will not harm you if you are righteous (صالحين).

And in al-Majma': That Abu Tha‘labah asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about this Ayah, and He said: "Enjoin the good (بالمعروف) and forbid the evil (عن المنكر). But when you see a worldly life preferred (مؤثرة), avarice obeyed (شحّاً مطاعاً), desire followed (هوى متّبعاً), and every person of opinion (ذي رأي) admiring his own opinion (برأيه), then you must attend to the specific concern (خويصّة) (1) of your soul and leave their common folk (عوامهم)." إِلَى اللهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعاً فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** To Allah is the return of you all, and He will inform you about what you used to do. A promise and a threat (وعد ووعيد) for both groups, based on the principle that no one will be held accountable for the sin of another.

footnote (١) *Al-Qaṣab* (*القصب*) with a *ḥarakah* (vowel mark) refers to the bones of the fingers, the branches of the throat, and the exits of the breaths. And *al-Qaṣab* (*القصب*) with a *ḍammah* (vowel mark) refers to the back (*الظهر*) and the intestines (*المِعى*). What is intended here is the intestines (*الأمعاء*). It was narrated from Ibn ‘Abbas, and it was narrated in place of (*ريح حرّ*) [a hot wind], so it is also suitable for the back (*الظهر*).

(١٠٦) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ ** (106) O you who have faith! The testimony between you The testimonies (الأشهاد) which He has legislated among you concerning that which you have been commanded. إِذا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ ** when he is near it and its signs (أماراته) are present, at the time of making the will (الْوَصِيَّةِ). It is said that in this there is an alert that the will (الْوَصِيَّةِ) is something that should not be treated lightly. اثْنانِ ** the testimony of two ذَوا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ ** from the Muslims أَوْ آخَرانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ ** from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) and the Magians (المجوس), as will follow, إِنْ أَنْتُمْ ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** if you travel (سافرتم) فَأَصابَتْكُمْ مُصِيبَةُ الْمَوْتِ the appointed time (الأجل) approaches you. تَحْبِسُونَهُما ** you detain them مِنْ بَعْدِ الصَّلاةِ ** in order to emphasize the oath (اليمين) due to the honor of the time, and because it is a time when people gather. فَيُقْسِمانِ بِاللهِ ** meaning, the two others (الآخران) إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ ** (2) if the heir (الوارث) among you is suspicious (ارتاب), and this is an interjection (اعتراض). لا نَشْتَرِي بِهِ ** by the oath (القسم) or by Allah ثَمَناً ** a worldly recompense (عوضاً) وَلَوْ كانَ ذا قُرْبى meaning, even if the one for whom the oath is taken (المقسم له) is a relative (ذا قربى). وَلا نَكْتُمُ شَهادَةَ اللهِ ** which Allah commanded to be established. إِنَّا إِذاً لَمِنَ الْآثِمِينَ ** meaning, if we conceal it.

(106) when death approaches one of you, [is that of] two just men from among you, or two others from another people, if you are journeying in the land and the affliction of death befalls you. You shall detain them after the prayer, and should you be suspicious, they shall swear by Allah: 'We will not sell it for any price, even if he be a relative, nor will we conceal the testimony of Allah, for then we would indeed be among the sinners.'

فَإِنْ عُثِرَ ** If it is looked into and knowledge is obtained عَلى أَنَّهُمَا ** meaning the other two deserved sin (إِثْماً), meaning they necessitated its punishment due to distortion in the testimony or treachery فَآخَرانِ ** then two other witnesses يَقُومانِ مَقامَهُما مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ** meaning those against whom the right was established, meaning the heirs الْأَوْلَيانِ ** the two most deserving of the testimony due to their kinship and knowledge. And it was read (وقرء) عليهم اسْتَحَقَّ (against them deserved) built upon the active voice (للفاعل), and الأوّلين (the first ones) in the plural, as an adjective for الذين (those). فَيُقْسِمانِ بِاللهِ لَشَهادَتُنا أَحَقُّ مِنْ شَهادَتِهِما meaning our oath is more truthful. He named the oath (اليمين) a testimony (شهادة) because it takes its place, as in the case of al-Li'an (mutual cursing). وَمَا اعْتَدَيْنا ** and we have not exceeded the truth in it إِنَّا إِذاً لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ.

footnote (١) الخويصة is the diminutive of الخاصّة (*al-khāṣṣah*). Its *yā’* (*ياؤها*) is quiescent because the *yā’* of the diminutive is not vocalized.

footnote (٢) Meaning **إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ ** is an interjection (*اعتراض*). The pronoun in **بِهِ ** refers to the oath (*القسم*), and the pronoun in **كانَ ** refers to the object of the oath (*المقسم له*). Meaning: We do not exchange the validity of the oath by Allah for a worldly gain, even if the one for whom we swear is close to us. He intended that this is their custom in their perpetual truthfulness and trustworthiness, like His saying: **شُهَداءَ لِلَّهِ ** even if it is against yourselves. And He specified **ذا قُرْبى ** (a relative) in the mention because the inclination towards them is greater and the compromise (*المداهنة*) among them is more complete, as stated by al-Naysaburi.

(١٠٨) ذلِكَ ** meaning: the ruling that preceded, or the swearing of the two witnesses أَدْنى ** closer أَنْ يَأْتُوا بِالشَّهادَةِ عَلى وَجْهِها that they bring the testimony in its proper form, according to the manner in which they bear it, without alteration or treachery therein أَوْ يَخافُوا أَنْ تُرَدَّ أَيْمانٌ ** meaning: that the oath be returned to the claimants بَعْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ after their oaths, so they are disgraced by the manifestation of treachery and the false oath. The word al-yamin (اليمين) is pluralized (أَيْمانٌ) to encompass all the witnesses.

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the interpretation of this Ayah: The two who are from among you are Muslims, and the two مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ ** are from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب). If you do not find any from the People of the Book, then [they are] from the Magians (المجوس), because the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) established for the Magians the custom (سُنَّة) of the People of the Book regarding the jizyah (الجزية). That is when a man dies in a foreign land and does not find Muslims, he calls two men from the People of the Book to witness. They are detained after the afternoon prayer (al-ʿAsr) and they swear by Allah: إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ لا نَشْتَرِي بِهِ ثَمَناً وَلَوْ كانَ ذا قُرْبى وَلا نَكْتُمُ شَهادَةَ اللهِ إِنَّا إِذاً لَمِنَ الْآثِمِينَ (if you have doubts, we will not sell it for a price, though it were a relative, nor will we conceal the testimony of Allah, for then we would be among the sinners). He said: That is if the guardian (وليّ) of the deceased doubts their testimony. فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلى أَنَّهُمَا ** testified falsely, then he is not permitted to reject their testimony until he brings two witnesses who stand in the place of the first two witnesses, and they swear by Allah: لَشَهادَتُنا أَحَقُّ مِنْ شَهادَتِهِما وَمَا اعْتَدَيْنا إِنَّا إِذاً لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ (Our testimony is surely truer than their testimony, and we have not transgressed, for then we would indeed be among the wrongdoers). When he does that, the testimony of the first two is invalidated, and the testimony of the latter two is accepted. Allah the Almighty says: **ذلِكَ أَدْنى أَنْ يَأْتُوا ** [to the end of] the Ayah.

In al-Kafi, narrated marfūʿan (مرفوعاً): Tamim al-Dari (تميم الدّاري), Ibn Biday (ابن بيدي), and Ibn Abi Mariyah (ابن أبي مارية) set out on a journey. Tamim al-Dari was a Muslim, while Ibn Biday and Ibn Abi Mariyah were Christians (نصرانيّين). Tamim al-Dari had with him a khurj (خُرْج) (1) containing goods, gold-engraved vessels (آنية), and a necklace, which he had taken out to some of the markets of the Arabs for sale. Tamim al-Dari fell severely ill, and when death approached him, he handed over what He had to Ibn Biday and Ibn Abi Mariyah and commanded them to deliver it to his heirs. They arrived in Medina, having taken the vessels and the necklace from the goods, and delivered the rest to his heirs. The people missed the vessels and the necklace. The family of Tamim asked: "Did our companion suffer a long illness during which he spent a great deal of money?" They both replied: "No, he was only ill for a few days." They asked: "Was anything stolen from him during this journey?" They replied: "No." They asked: "Did he engage in a trade in which he lost money?" They replied: "No." They said: "We are missing the best thing he had with him: gold-engraved vessels studded with jewels and a necklace." They both replied: "Whatever was handed over to us, we have delivered to you." So, they brought the matter before the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace). He required them to take an oath, and they swore, so He released them. Then, those vessels and the necklace appeared in their possession.

The guardians (أولياء) of Tamim came to the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, what we claimed against Ibn Biday and Ibn Abi Mariyah has become manifest." The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) awaited the ruling from Allah concerning this, so Allah the Almighty revealed: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ ** [to the end of] the Ayah. Thus, Allah the Almighty permitted the testimony of the People of the Book only regarding a bequest (الوصية) if [the testator] was on a journey and did not find Muslims: فَأَصابَتْكُمْ مُصِيبَةُ الْمَوْتِ تَحْبِسُونَهُما مِنْ بَعْدِ الصَّلاةِ فَيُقْسِمانِ بِاللهِ إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ لا نَشْتَرِي بِهِ ثَمَناً وَلَوْ كانَ ذا قُرْبى وَلا نَكْتُمُ شَهادَةَ اللهِ إِنَّا إِذاً لَمِنَ الْآثِمِينَ (and the affliction of death overtakes you. You shall detain them after the prayer, and they shall swear by Allah, if you have doubts: 'We will not sell it for a price, though it were a relative, nor will we conceal the testimony of Allah, for then we would be among the sinners'). This is the first testimony that the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) established. فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلى أَنَّهُمَا اسْتَحَقَّا إِثْماً ** meaning: that they swore a false oath فَآخَرانِ يَقُومانِ مَقامَهُما ** meaning: from the guardians of the claimant مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَوْلَيانِ فَيُقْسِمانِ بِاللهِ ** they swear by Allah that they are more entitled to this claim than the two of them, and that the two of them lied in what they swore by Allah لَشَهادَتُنا أَحَقُّ مِنْ شَهادَتِهِما وَمَا اعْتَدَيْنا إِنَّا إِذاً لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ . So, the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) commanded the guardians of Tamim al-Dari to swear by Allah as He had commanded them, and they swore. Then the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) took the necklace and the vessels from Ibn Biday and Ibn Abi Mariyah and returned them to the guardians of Tamim al-Dari. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated something close to this.

In al-Kafi, narrated through several reports from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If a man is in a foreign land where no Muslim can be found, the testimony of one who is not a Muslim is permissible regarding the bequest (الوصيّة). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَاسْمَعُوا ** (and be wary of Allah and listen) meaning: listen with acceptance and compliance وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ ** (and Allah does not guide the transgressing lot) to the path of Paradise.

(١) The khurj (الخُرج), with a ḍammah (الضمّ), is the saddlebag (الجوالق) with two ears, and it is an Arabic word.

(109) يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ اللهُ الرُّسُلَ ** (mention) فَيَقُولُ ** (to them) **ما ذا أُجِبْتُمْ قالُوا لا عِلْمَ لَنا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ ** (The day Allah will gather the apostles and say, 'What was the response you received?' They will say, 'We have no knowledge; indeed, You are the Knower of all that is unseen.')

In al-Jawami, the question is a form of rebuke (توبيخ), and that is why They will say, 'We have no knowledge,' and they delegated the matter to His knowledge concerning the poor response they received, and they sought refuge in Him for vengeance against them.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They say 'We have no knowledge' besides You. And He said: The entire Quran is a form of censure (تقريع), but its inward meaning is drawing near (تقريب).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, this has an esoteric interpretation (تأويلاً). 'and say, "What was the response you received?"' refers to your successors (أوصيائكم) whom you left among your nations. So they will say, 'We have no knowledge' of what they did after us.

And al-Qummi narrated the like of it from him (peace be upon him), without naming it an esoteric interpretation (تأويلاً).

(110) إِذْ قالَ اللهُ ** (When Allah said) It is a substitute (بدل) for يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ ** (the Day He gathers). يا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ اذْكُرْ نِعْمَتِي عَلَيْكَ وَعَلى والِدَتِكَ إِذْ أَيَّدْتُكَ ** (O Jesus son of Mary, remember My blessing upon you and upon your mother, when I strengthened you) I strengthened you (قويتك) بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ تُكَلِّمُ النَّاسَ فِي الْمَهْدِ وَكَهْلاً ** (with the Holy Spirit, when you spoke to the people in the cradle and in adulthood) You spoke to them in all your states equally (على سواء). وَإِذْ عَلَّمْتُكَ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَالتَّوْراةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ وَإِذْ تَخْلُقُ مِنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ بِإِذْنِي فَتَنْفُخُ فِيها فَتَكُونُ طَيْراً بِإِذْنِي وَتُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ وَالْأَبْرَصَ بِإِذْنِي وَإِذْ تُخْرِجُ الْمَوْتى بِإِذْنِي ** (and when I taught you the Book and the wisdom, the Torah and the Evangel, and when you created from the clay the likeness of a bird by My leave, and you breathed into it, and it became a bird by My leave, and you healed the blind and the leper by My leave, and when you raised the dead by My leave) Its interpretation has already passed in Surat Āl ‘Imrān. And it was read (وقرءَ) as طائراً (a bird, accusative case). وَإِذْ كَفَفْتُ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ عَنْكَ ** (and when I kept the Children of Israel from you) meaning the Jews (اليهود) when they intended to kill him. إِذْ جِئْتَهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ إِنْ هذا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ (when you brought them manifest proofs, and the faithless among them said, 'This is nothing but manifest magic.') And it was read (وقرء) as ساحر (a sorcerer).

(١١١) وَإِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَى الْحَوارِيِّينَ (111) And when I inspired the disciples Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They were inspired (أُلْهِمُوا) أَنْ آمِنُوا بِي وَبِرَسُولِي قالُوا آمَنَّا وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنا مُسْلِمُونَ ** that, 'Believe in Me and My apostle.' They said, 'We believe, and bear witness that we are Muslims.' meaning sincere (مُخْلِصُونَ). The explanation regarding the naming of the disciples (الْحَوارِيِّينَ) and the mention of their number has already passed in Surat Āl 'Imrān.

(١١٢) إِذْ قالَ الْحَوارِيُّونَ يا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ رَبُّكَ ** (when the Disciples said, 'O Jesus son of Mary, can your Lord) And it was read with the address (بالخطاب), and Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated its reading separately (مقطوعاً قراءتها): "Can you ask (أن تدعو) your Lord (رَبُّكَ)?" And it was said that this ability (الاستطاعة) is based on what wisdom (الحكمة) and the Will (الإرادة) necessitate, not on what power (القدرة) necessitates: أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنا مائِدَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (to send down to us a table from the sky?) Al-Mā'idah (المائِدة) is the table (الخوان) when food (الطعام) is upon it. قالَ اتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (He said, 'Be wary of Allah) from the likes of this question, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** (if you are faithful.') in the perfection of His power (قدرته).

(١١٣) **قالُوا نُرِيدُ أَنْ نَأْكُلَ مِنْها ** (113) They said, 'We desire to eat from it [This is] preparing an excuse and clarifying what prompted them to ask.

وَتَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُنا ** and that our hearts may be at rest [This is] through witnessing [it] (بالمشاهدة).

وَنَعْلَمَ أَنْ قَدْ صَدَقْتَنا ** and that we may know that you have told us the truth [This is] regarding the claim of prophethood (في ادعاءِ النبوّة).

وَنَكُونَ عَلَيْها مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ and that we may be witnesses to it.' It was said: We will bear witness to it before those who were not present for it.

(١١٤) قالَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ اللهُمَّ رَبَّنا أَنْزِلْ عَلَيْنا مائِدَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ تَكُونُ لَنا عِيداً ** (‘Isa son of Maryam said, ‘O Allah, our Lord! Send down to us a table from the sky, to be a festival for us,) It was said that the day of its descent would be a festival (عيداً) which we honor. And it was Sunday (يوم الأحَد), and for this reason, the Christians took it as a festival (عيداً). And it was said: rather, the ʿīd (العيد) is the returning joy (السرور العائد), and from this is the Day of ʿĪd (يوم العيد). for the first of us and the last of us, we eat from it all together. And it was said: for those in our time and for those after us. وَآيَةً مِنْكَ وَارْزُقْنا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ.** (and a sign from You, and provide for us, for You are the best of providers.)

(115) The Almighty said: قالَ اللهُ إِنِّي مُنَزِّلُها عَلَيْكُمْ ** (Allah said: 'I will send it down to you) in response to your request. And it was read (مُنْزِلُها) without the tashdid (doubling of the zāy). فَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بَعْدُ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنِّي أُعَذِّبُهُ عَذاباً لا أُعَذِّبُهُ أَحَداً مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ ** (But whoever of you disbelieves after that, I will punish him with a punishment that I will not inflict upon anyone in all the nations.')

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon them both) said to the Children of Israel: "Fast for thirty days, then ask Allah whatever you wish, and He will grant it to you." So they fasted for thirty days. When they finished, they said: "If we worked for any person and completed his work, he would feed us food. We have fasted and gone hungry, so supplicate to Allah to send down upon us a table (مائدة) from the heaven." Then the angels approached with a table (مائدة) carrying upon it seven loaves of bread and seven trays (أخوان) (1), until they placed it between their hands. The last person among them ate from it just as the first person among them ate.

And narrated from Ammar ibn Yasir, from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace): The Table (المائدة) descended as bread and meat. That was because they asked Jesus (peace be upon him) for food that would not run out, from which they could eat. He said: "It was said to them: 'Indeed, it will remain for you as long as you do not betray (2), hide, or lift it. If you do that, I will punish you.'" He said: Their day had not passed until they hid, lifted, and betrayed.

And narrated from Salman al-Farsi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: By Allah, Jesus (peace be upon him) never pursued any evil thing, nor did He seize anything, nor did He laugh loudly (قهقه ضحكاً), nor did He swat a fly from His face, nor did He ever hold His nose due to the stench of anything, nor did He ever trifle. When الْحَوارِيُّونَ ** (the Disciples) asked Him to send down the Table (المائدة) upon them, He wore wool and wept. He said: اللهُمَّ رَبَّنا أَنْزِلْ عَلَيْنا مائِدَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (O Allah, our Lord! Send down to us a table from the heaven) [the rest of] the Ayah. Then a red leather cloth (سفرة) descended between two clouds while they were looking at it, and it was descending rapidly until it fell between their hands. Jesus (peace be upon our Prophet and his progeny and upon him) wept and said: "O Allah, make me among the grateful. O Allah, make it a mercy and do not make it a cause of turning away (فتلة) (1), degradation, or punishment." The Jews were looking at it, looking at something they had never seen the like of before, and they did not find a scent purer than its scent.

Then Jesus (peace be upon him) stood up, performed ablution (توضّأ), and prayed a long prayer. Then He uncovered the cloth (المنديل) from it and said: "In the Name of Allah, the Best of Providers (خير الرّازقين)." Behold, it was a roasted fish (سمكّة مشويّة) without scales (فلوس), dripping profusely with fat (الدسم). At its head was salt, and at its tail was vinegar (خلّ). Around it were various kinds of herbs (البقول) except for leeks (الكرّاث). And behold, there were five loaves of bread: on one of them was olives (زيتون), on the second honey (عسل), on the third clarified butter (سمن), on the fourth cheese (جبن), and on the fifth dried meat (قديد).

Then Sham'un said: "O Spirit of Allah (يا روح الله), is this food from this world (الدّنيا) or from the Hereafter (الآخرة)?" Jesus (peace be upon him) said: "None of what you see is food from this world nor food from the Hereafter, but it is something Allah created by His Overpowering Might (القدرة الغالبة). Eat what you requested; He will sustain you and provide for you from His bounty." The Disciples (الحواريّون) said: "O Spirit of Allah, if only You would show us another sign (آية) today from this sign." Jesus (peace be upon him) said: "O fish (سمكّة), come alive by the permission of Allah, the Exalted!" So the fish thrashed about, and its scales (فلوسها) and bones (شوكها) returned to it. They were frightened of it. He said: "What is wrong with you? You ask for things, and when they are given to you, you dislike them! How afraid I am for you that you will be punished! O fish, return to how you were by the permission of Allah!" So the fish returned to being roasted as it was. They said: "O Spirit of Allah, be the first to eat from it, and then we will eat." Jesus (peace be upon him) said: "I seek refuge in Allah from eating from it. Rather, let those who requested it eat from it." So they feared eating from it.
