Jesus (peace be upon him) then called for the needy (أهل الفاقة), the chronically ill (الزّمني) (2), the sick, and the afflicted. He said: "Eat from it, and for you is well-being (الهناء), and for others is tribulation (البلاء)." So one thousand three hundred men and women—poor, sick, and afflicted—ate from it, and all of them were full, belching (3).

Then Jesus (peace be upon him) looked at the fish, and behold, it was in the same state as when it descended from the heaven. Then the Table (المائدة) flew upwards while they watched it until it disappeared from their sight. No chronically ill person (زمن) ate from it that day except that he was cured, nor any sick person except that he recovered, nor any poor person except that he became rich and remained rich until he died. The Disciples (الحواريّون) and those who did not eat from it regretted it. When it descended, the rich, the poor, the young, and the old would gather, jostling one another for it. When Jesus (peace be upon him) saw that, He made it a rotation (نوبةً) among them. It remained for forty mornings, descending in the forenoon (ضحىً) and remaining set up, being eaten from, until the shadow (الفيء) returned, whereupon it flew upwards while they watched it in its shadow until it disappeared from them. It used to descend intermittently (غبّاً), one day yes and one day no. Then Allah, the Exalted, revealed to Jesus (peace be upon him): "Make My Table (مائدتي) for the poor, excluding the rich." This was difficult for the rich, so they doubted it and caused the people to doubt it. Then Allah, the Exalted, revealed to Jesus: "I have stipulated a condition upon the deniers: that whoever disbelieves after its descent, أُعَذِّبُهُ عَذاباً لا أُعَذِّبُهُ أَحَداً مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ ** (I will punish him with a punishment that I will not inflict upon anyone in all the nations')." So Jesus (peace be upon him) said: إِنْ تُعَذِّبْهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ عِبادُكَ وَإِنْ تَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ فَإِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** (If You punish them, they are indeed Your servants; but if You forgive them, You are indeed the All-mighty, the All-wise.) Then three hundred and thirty-three men among them were transformed (مسخ). They had spent the night on their beds with their wives in their homes, but they awoke as pigs, running in the streets and garbage heaps, eating filth (العذرة) in the latrines (الحشوش) (1). When the people saw that, they rushed to Jesus (peace be upon him) and wept, and the families of the transformed wept over them. They lived for three days, then perished. He said: And in Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them): The Table (المائدة) used to descend upon them, and they would gather around it and eat from it, then it would be lifted. Their elders and affluent ones said: "We will not let our common folk (سفلتنا) eat from it." So Allah lifted the Table (المائدة) due to their transgression, and they were transformed into apes and pigs.

And al-Qummi confined himself to what he attributed to Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them) as a truncated narration (مقطوعاً). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Table (المائدة) that descended upon the Children of Israel was suspended (مدلاةً) (2) by chains of gold, upon it were nine trays (أَخْوِنَة) and nine loaves of bread. In another narration: nine kinds of loaves of bread.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): They were transformed into pigs. And al-Ayyashi: similar to it.

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The jarrith (الجرّيث) (eel/scaly fish) and the dabb (الضّب) (mastigure lizard) are a group from the Children of Israel who did not believe when the Table (المائدة) descended upon Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon them both), so they wandered (تاهوا) (1). One group fell into the sea and another group fell onto the land. And in al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) in a hadith about the transformed creatures (المسوخات): As for the pigs (الخنازير), they were a group of Christians (نصارى) who asked their Lord to send down the Table (المائدة) upon them, but when it was sent down, they were more intense in disbelief (كفراً) and more intense in denial (تكذيباً) than they had ever been.

(1) Al-khuwān (الخُوان), like (غُراب) and (كتاب), is that upon which food is eaten, like (الإخوان).

(2) His saying: "as long as you do not betray (تخونوا) and hide (تخبأوا)." It is possible to derive (تخبأوا) from (جبأ) with the single jīm (ج) from the pattern of (منع) and (فرح), meaning: you did not leave, dislike, or hate. Or from (خبأ) with the dotted khā (خ) and the single bā (ب) from the pattern of (منع), meaning: as long as you do not conceal or hide its matter, or hide in it the deceit of a disappointed one (خائب). Or the doubled tā (ت) from (ختأه) like (منعه), meaning: He restrained him from the matter; (اختتأ له) means: He served him.

(1) His saying: (فتلة). It is said: He turned him (فتله) away from his face, so he turned away (فانفتل), meaning: He diverted him, so he departed. The intended meaning is perhaps: "Do not make it a cause for the turning away of the blessing (النّعمة)."

(2) Al-zamānah (الزّمانة) is a defect (العاهة) and affliction (آفة) in an animal. It is said: The person became chronically ill (زمن) (زمناً) and (زمانةً), so he is chronically ill (زمن) from the pattern of (تعب), and it is an illness that lasts a long time.

(3) Al-tajashshu' (التجشّؤ) is the breathing of the stomach (belching).

(1) Al-hashsh (الحشّ) with fath (a vowel sound) and tashdid (doubling), and fath is more common than ḍamm or kasr, means the exit (المخرج) and the place of need (موضع الحاجة). Its origin is from al-hashsh (الحش) (the garden), because they often used to defecate in gardens. When they adopted the privy (الكنيف) and made it a substitute for the gardens, they applied the name to it metaphorically. The plural of al-hashsh (الحش) is ḥishān (حشان), like (ضيف) and (ضيفان).

(2) (أدليتها) means I sent it down (أرسلتها). (تدلّى من الشجرة) means he hung from the tree.

(1) They wandered.

(١١٦) And when Allah said, 'O Jesus son of Mary, In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He did not say it, but He will say it. Indeed, when Allah knows something that will come into existence, He informs about it as a report of what has already been. 'Did you say to the people, "Take me and my mother as two gods besides Allah?"' This is a reprimand for the disbelievers and a rebuke to them.

Al-Qummi said: That is because the Christians claimed that Jesus (peace be upon him) told them that I and my mother are two gods besides Allah. So, when the Day of Resurrection comes, Allah will gather the Christians and Jesus (peace be upon our Prophet and his family and upon him) and He will say: 'Did you say'—the Ayah.

He said, 'Immaculate are You! I declare Your absolute purity, a purification from having any partner. It is not for me to say what I have no right to say. That which it is not rightful for me to say. Had I said it, You would have surely known it. You know what is in my soul, and I do not know what is in Your soul. You know what I conceal, and I do not know what You conceal.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation: Indeed, the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر) consists of seventy-three letters (حرفاً). The Lord, the Exalted, veiled Himself with one letter (حرف)—and for that reason, no one knows what is in His soul, the Mighty and Majestic. He granted Adam seventy-two letters (حرفاً), and the Prophets inherited them until they reached Jesus. That is the saying of Jesus (peace be upon him): You know what is in my soul, meaning the seventy-two letters (حرفاً) of the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر). He says: You taught them to me, so You know them. and I do not know what is in Your soul, meaning: because You veiled Yourself from Your creation with that letter (حرف), so no one knows what is in Your soul.

Indeed, You are the Knower of the Unseen.'

(١١٧) ما قُلْتُ لَهُمْ إِلَّا ما أَمَرْتَنِي بِهِ أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ رَبِّي وَرَبَّكُمْ وَكُنْتُ عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيداً ** (117) I did not tell them anything except what You commanded me to [say]: 'Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.' And I was a witness to them watchful (رقيباً), fully aware (مطّلعاً), preventing them from saying that and believing it ما دُمْتُ فِيهِمْ فَلَمَّا تَوَفَّيْتَنِي as long as I was among them. But when You took me away, by raising [me] to the heaven, based on His saying: إِنِّي مُتَوَفِّيكَ وَرافِعُكَ إِلَيَ ** (Indeed I shall take you and raise you toward Myself). And al-tawaffi (التوفّي) is the taking of a thing completely (وافياً), and death is a type of it. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: يَتَوَفَّى الْأَنْفُسَ حِينَ مَوْتِها وَالَّتِي لَمْ تَمُتْ فِي مَنامِها كُنْتَ أَنْتَ الرَّقِيبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (Allah takes the souls at the time of their death, and those that have not died, in their sleep. You were Yourself the Watcher over them) the Observer (المراقب) of their states. وَأَنْتَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ ** (and You are a witness to all things.) fully aware (مطّلع), observing it.

footnote (١) تاه في الأرض اي ذهب متحيّراً يتيه تيهاً وتَيَهاناً. (1) Tāha fi al-arḍ (تاه في الأرض) means he went away bewildered (متحيّراً), wandering (يتيه) with tīhan (تيهاً) and tayahānan (تَيَهاناً).

footnote (٢) لعلّ التقدير إنّيّ وأمّي اتخذوا الهين ولا يستقيم حكاية عن الآية كما لا يخفى. (2) Perhaps the estimation (التقدير) is: Indeed, I and my mother were taken as two gods (إلهين), but this does not stand as a narration (حكاية) from the verse, as is evident.

(١١٨) إِنْ تُعَذِّبْهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ عِبادُكَ ** (118) If You punish them, they are indeed Your servants You own them and You are aware of their crimes. It was said that in this is an alert that they deserved that because they are Your servants, yet they worshipped other than You. وَإِنْ تَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ فَإِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** And if You forgive them, You are indeed the All-mighty, the All-wise The Powerful (القادر), the Strong (القويّ) over reward and punishment, Who does not reward or punish except based on wisdom (حكمة) and correctness (صواب). For forgiveness is a good deed (حسنة) for every criminal; if You punish, it is justice (عدل), and if You forgive, it is grace (فضل).

(١١٩) The Almighty said: قالَ اللهُ هذا يَوْمُ (١) يَنْفَعُ الصَّادِقِينَ صِدْقُهُمْ (1) (This is a day when the truthful will be benefited by their truthfulness.) And it was recited (يومَ) in the accusative case (النّصب), and this reading is not devoid of difficulty (تكلّف). لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ. (For them are gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is the great success.)

لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما فِيهِنَّ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (120) (To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth and whatever is in them, and He has power over all things.)

In this is an alert regarding the falsehood of the Christians and the corruption of their claim concerning the Messiah and his mother.

Al-Qummi (narrates): And the proof that Jesus (peace be upon him) did not say that to them is His saying, the Almighty: هذا يَوْمُ يَنْفَعُ الصَّادِقِينَ صِدْقُهُمْ. (119) (This is a day when the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness.)

Then he narrated with his chain of transmission from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: When the Day of Resurrection occurs and people are gathered for the Reckoning, they pass through the terrors of the Day of Resurrection, and they do not reach the Plain (العرصة) until they are severely exhausted. He said: They stand at the courtyard (فناء) of the Plain (الْعَرْصَة), and the Almighty (الجبّار) looks down upon them while He is upon His Throne (عرشه). The first one who is called by a proclamation that all creatures hear is that the name of Muhammad ibn Abdullah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), the Qurayshi, Arab Prophet (النّبيّ القرشيّ العَرَبيّ), is shouted. He said: He advances until he stands on the right side of the Throne.

He said: Then your companion (صاحبكم) is called, and he advances until he stands on the left side of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). Then the Ummah of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) is called, and they stand on the left side of Ali. Then prophet after prophet is called, with his Ummah accompanying him, from the first of the Prophets to the last of them, and their Ummahs are with them, and they stand on the left side of the Throne.

He said: Then the first one called for questioning (المسائلة) is the Pen (القلم). He said: It advances and stands before Allah in the form of human beings (في صورة الآدميّين). Allah says: "Did you inscribe upon the Tablet (اللّوح) what I inspired you with and commanded you concerning the Revelation (الوحي)?" The Pen says: "Yes, O Lord. You know that I have inscribed upon the Tablet what You commanded me and inspired me with of Your Revelation." Allah says: "Who bears witness for you concerning that?" It says: "O Lord, did any creature other than You become privy to the hidden secret (مكنون سرّك) of Yours?" He said: He (Allah) says to it: "You have established your proof (أفلجت حجّتك)."

He said: Then the Tablet (اللّوح) is called, and it advances in the form of human beings until it stands with the Pen. He (Allah) says to it: "Did the Pen inscribe upon you what He (Allah) inspired it with and commanded it concerning the Revelation?" The Tablet says: "Yes, O Lord, and I conveyed it to Israfil (إسرافيل)." Then Israfil is called, and Israfil advances with the Tablet and the Pen in the form of human beings. Allah says to him: "Did Israfil convey to you what he conveyed?" He says: "O Lord, I conveyed it to all Your Prophets, and I executed to them everything that reached me of Your command, and I delivered Your messages to every Prophet and every Messenger, and I conveyed to them all Your Revelation (وحيك), Your Wisdom (حكمتك), and Your Books (كتبك). And indeed, the last one to whom I conveyed Your Message (رسالتك), Your Revelation (وحيك), Your Wisdom (حكمتك), Your Knowledge (علمك), Your Book (كتابك), and Your Word (كلامك) is Muhammad ibn Abdullah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), the Arab, the Qurayshi, the Haramite (الحرميّ), Your Beloved."

Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: The first one among the descendants of Adam to be called for questioning is Muhammad ibn Abdullah. Allah brings him near until no creature on that Day is closer to Allah than him. Allah says: "O Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), did Gabriel convey to you what I revealed to him and sent him with to you of My Book, My Wisdom, and My Knowledge? And did he reveal that to you?" The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) says: "Yes, O Lord, Gabriel conveyed to me everything You revealed to him and sent him with of Your Book, Your Wisdom, and Your Knowledge, and he revealed it to me." Allah says to Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "Did you convey to your Ummah what Gabriel conveyed to you of My Book, My Wisdom, and My Knowledge?" The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) says: "Yes, O Lord, I conveyed to my Ummah everything You revealed to me of Your Book, Your Wisdom, and Your Knowledge, and I struggled (جاهدت) in Your way."

Allah says to Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "Who bears witness for you concerning that?" Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) says: "O Lord, You are the Witness for me regarding the conveyance of the Message, and Your angels, and the righteous (الأبرار) among my Ummah. And You are sufficient as a Witness." Then the angels are called, and they bear witness for Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) regarding the conveyance of the Message. Then the Ummah of Muhammad is called, and they are asked: "Did Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) convey to you My Message, My Book, My Wisdom, and My Knowledge, and teach you that?" They bear witness for Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) regarding the conveyance of the Message, the Wisdom, and the Knowledge.

Allah says to Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "Did you appoint a successor (استخلفت) among your Ummah after you, who would uphold My Wisdom and My Knowledge among them, interpret My Book for them, and clarify for them what they differ about after you, as a proof (حجّة) for Me and a vicegerent (خليفة) on earth?" Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) says: "Yes, O Lord, I appointed among them Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him), my brother, my minister (وزيري), my legatee (وصييّ), and the best of my Ummah. I established him as a standard (علماً) for them during my lifetime, and I called them to his obedience, and I made him My vicegerent (خليفتي) in my Ummah, an Imam (إماماً) to be followed by the Ummah after me until the Day of Resurrection."

Then Ali ibn Abi Talib is called, and it is said to him: "Did Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) entrust you (أوصى إليك), appoint you as his successor (استخلفك) in his Ummah, and establish you as a standard (علماً) for his Ummah during his lifetime? And did you uphold his station among them after him?" Ali says to Him: "Yes, O Lord, Muhammad entrusted me and appointed me as his successor in his Ummah and established me as a standard for them during his lifetime. But when You took Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) to Yourself, his Ummah denied me, plotted against me, deemed me weak (استضعفوني), and almost killed me. They put forward those whom I had kept back, and they kept back those whom I had put forward. They did not listen to me and did not obey my command, so I fought them in Your way until they killed me."

It is said to Ali: "Did you appoint a proof (حجّة) and a vicegerent (خليفة) on earth after you among the Ummah of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), who calls My servants to My religion and to My path?" Ali says: "Yes, O Lord, I appointed among them al-Hasan, my son and the son of Your Prophet's daughter." Then al-Hasan ibn Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both) is called, and he is questioned about what Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) was questioned about. He said: Then Imam after Imam is called, along with the people of his time (أهل عالمه), and they present their proof (حجّتهم). Allah accepts their excuse (عذرهم) and validates their proof (حجّتهم). He said: Then Allah says: هذا يَوْمُ يَنْفَعُ الصَّادِقِينَ صِدْقُهُمْ (119) (This is a day when the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness.)

Al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The Quran used to abrogate (ينسخ) parts of itself, and only the last of the commands of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) is to be adopted. The last thing revealed to him was Surat al-Ma’idah (سورة المائِدة). It abrogated what preceded it, and nothing abrogated it. It was revealed to him while He was upon a grayish mule (بغلة شهباء), and the Revelation (الوحي) weighed heavily upon him until it stopped, and its belly hung down so much that I saw its navel almost touching the ground. The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) fainted until he placed his hand on the forelock (ذوابة) of Shaybah ibn Wahb al-Jahmi. Then that was lifted from the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), and he recited Surat al-Ma’idah to us, and the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) acted upon it, and we acted upon it.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Ma’idah was revealed complete (كملاً), and seventy million angels (سبعون ألف ألف ملك) descended with it. And in Thawab al-A‘mal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surat al-Ma’idah every Thursday, his faith will not be mixed with injustice (بظلم), and he will never associate partners with Him, Allah the Almighty willing.

footnote (١) Regarding His saying, the Almighty: **هذا يَوْمُ يَنْفَعُ**. **هذا** is the subject (*مبتدأ*) and **يَوْمُ** is its predicate (*خبر*). It is inflected (*معرب*) because it is annexed (*مضاف*) to an inflected word (*معرب*), thus retaining its proper inflection (*الأعراب*). It is also read as **يَوْمُ** with the *fathah* (*بالفتح*), and it is accusative (*منصوب*) as an adverb of time (*على الظّرف*). There are two interpretations for **هذا**: the first is that it is the object (*مفعول*) of **قالَ اللهُ هذا** (Allah said this) regarding the day; the second is that **هذا** is the subject (*مبتدأ*) and **يَوْمُ** is an adverb of time (*ظرف*) for a suppressed predicate (*للخبر المحذوف*), meaning: this occurs or happens on the day that occurs. The Kufans (*الكوفيّون*) said that **يَوْمُ** is in the nominative position (*في موضع رفع*) as the predicate (*خبر*) of **هذا**, but it is indeclinable (*مبنيّ*) with the *fathah* due to its annexation to a verb (*لاضافته الى الفعل*). According to them, its indeclinability is permissible even if it is annexed to an inflected word (*معرب*), whereas according to us, it is not permissible unless it is annexed to an indeclinable word (*مبنيّ*).
